Business justification: Budget plans are directly owned and executed by country offices, which are accountable for financial utilization and donor financial reporting. Country offices submit budget plans to headquarters and ']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.fund. Business justification: Budget plans must allocate expenses to specific donor funds for restriction compliance and fund accounting. Critical for budget-to-fund allocation, grant financial reporting, restricted fund managemen']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Budget plans are directly constrained by donor requirements specifying allowable costs, indirect cost rate caps, and prior approval thresholds. Finance-compliance officers must link budget plans to th']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: NGO program budget plans are aligned to fiscal periods for annual financial planning and period-close reporting. budget_plan has budget_period_start/end_date but no fiscal_period FK — a nonprofit fina']
  grant_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.grant_amendment. Business justification: NGO financial management: each budget plan version corresponds to a specific grant amendment that authorized the budget revision. Linking budget plans to grant amendments is required for financial aud']
  grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: NGO program budget plans must reconcile with finance grant budgets for donor financial reporting and audit compliance. This link enables budget plan vs. grant budget variance analysis — a core require']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program intervention this budget plan supports.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date on which this budget plan amendment was executed.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why this budget plan was amended or revised, if applicable.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this budget plan was formally approved.']
  budget_assumptions varchar [note: 'Key assumptions underlying the budget plan, such as exchange rates, inflation rates, beneficiary numbers, or activity volumes.']
  budget_narrative varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative justification and explanation of the budget plan, including assumptions, methodologies, and cost calculations.']
  budget_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the program manager or budget holder responsible for this budget plan.']
  budget_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the budget period this plan covers.']
  budget_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the budget period this plan covers.']
  budget_plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the budget plan, typically aligned with program or grant phase.']
  budget_plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or code for this budget plan, used in donor reporting and financial communications.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget plan in the approval and execution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|active|amended|closed|rejected — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget plan indicating whether it is the original approved budget, a revision, supplemental funding, or closeout budget.']
  budget_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this budget plan, incremented with each revision or amendment.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes regarding compliance requirements, restrictions, or special conditions attached to this budget plan.']
  contractual_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount for contractual services, consultants, and sub-awards.']
  cost_share_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount of cost sharing or matching funds contributed by the organization or other sources.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget plan.']
  dac_sector_code varchar [note: 'OECD DAC sector classification code for this budget plan, used for international aid transparency and reporting.']
  donor_budget_code varchar [note: 'Donor-specific budget classification code or identifier required for reporting and compliance.']
  donor_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget plan is visible to the donor in reporting portals or dashboards.']
  equipment_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount for equipment purchases with unit cost exceeding capitalization threshold.']
  finance_contact varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the finance team member responsible for financial oversight of this budget plan.']
  fringe_benefits_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount for fringe benefits associated with personnel costs.']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Approved indirect cost rate percentage applied to the direct cost base, as negotiated in NICRA or per de minimis rate.']
  indirect_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount for indirect costs (Facilities and Administration), calculated by applying the indirect cost rate to the direct cost base.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget plan record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about this budget plan.']
  personnel_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount for personnel salaries and wages.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Sustainable Development Goal(s) this budget plan contributes to, typically represented as SDG numbers (e.g., SDG 1, SDG 3).']
  sector_classification varchar [note: 'Humanitarian or development sector classification for this budget plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wash|health|nutrition|education|protection|livelihoods|shelter|food_security|emergency_response|capacity_building — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submitted_date date [note: 'Date on which this budget plan was submitted for approval or to the donor.']
  supplies_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount for supplies and consumable materials.']
  total_approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget amount for this plan, representing the sum of all cost categories.']
  total_direct_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all direct cost categories before indirect costs are applied.']
  travel_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount for travel expenses including international and domestic travel.']

  Note: 'Program-level budget plan capturing the approved budget envelope for a program intervention, including line-item detail by cost category (personnel, fringe, travel, equipment, supplies, contractual, other direct costs, indirect costs). Stores total approved budget, currency, budget period, budget version, donor-specific budget codes, indirect cost rate (ICR/NICRA), F&A allocation, unit costs, quantities, and budget status. Distinct from the finance domain\'s GL and BvA tracking — this is the program-level planning budget owned by program management.'
}

Table program.budget_plan_line {

  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or funding award that finances this budget line. Enables donor-specific budget reporting and compliance tracking.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Line-level linkage between program and finance budgets enables detailed variance analysis, actual-vs-budget reporting, and financial statement preparation. Essential for grant compliance and donor rep']
  budget_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent budget plan document that contains this line item. Links to the overall program budget structure.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: Budget line items in NGO programs are attributed to specific components for activity-based costing and component-level financial reporting. For example, personnel costs for a WASH engineer belong to t']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget lines are allocated to cost centers for departmental and program-level cost tracking. Essential for indirect cost allocation, NICRA compliance, and management reporting on program efficiency an']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.fund. Business justification: Individual budget line items allocated to specific donor funds enable granular restriction tracking and line-item fund accounting. Required for detailed financial reporting, multi-fund budget allocati']

Business justification: Program budget lines must map to GL accounts for financial posting, trial balance preparation, and financial statement generation. Required for GAAP compliance and ensuring program expenses are correc']
  grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: NGO budget plan lines map to grant budget lines for donor financial reporting and allowable cost compliance. budget_plan_line already has award_id and budget_line_id — adding grant_budget_id completes']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project to which this budget line belongs. Enables program-level budget rollup and tracking.']
  activity_code varchar [note: 'Program activity or intervention code that this budget line supports. Links budget to program implementation activities.']
  allowable_cost_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost is allowable under the grant or donor regulations. Used for budget review and compliance validation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this budget line was formally approved. Critical for compliance and audit documentation.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget line requires explicit donor or management approval before execution. Used for budget control and governance.']
  budget_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the budget period for which this line item is planned. Defines the timeframe for budget execution and BvA analysis.']
  budget_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the budget period for which this line item is planned. Enables multi-year and phased budget tracking.']
  budget_plan_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget line item. Tracks approval workflow and execution state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|active|revised|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  budget_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the budget plan to track revisions and amendments. Incremented with each budget modification.']
  chart_of_accounts_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code from the organization\'s Chart of Accounts. Links budget planning to financial accounting and enables BvA tracking.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Primary budget classification category following standard nonprofit and federal grant budget structures. Used for donor reporting and financial compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: personnel|fringe_benefits|travel|equipment|supplies|contractual|subawards|other_direct_costs|indirect_costs — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_sharing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the total planned amount that represents cost sharing or matching contribution. Used for grant compliance reporting.']
  cost_sharing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget line includes cost sharing or matching funds from the organization or other sources. Critical for grant compliance.']
  cost_sharing_source varchar [note: 'The source of cost sharing funds (e.g., organizational reserves, other grants, in-kind contributions, partner contributions).']
  cost_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory within the primary cost category for granular budget tracking (e.g., international travel, local travel, office supplies, medical supplies).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this budget line record was first created in the system. Required for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget line amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency budget management.']
  dac_sector_code varchar [note: 'OECD DAC 5-digit sector classification code for international development reporting and sector-based budget analysis.']
  direct_cost_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget line is a direct cost (true) or an indirect/overhead cost (false). Used for indirect cost rate calculations and compliance.']
  donor_budget_code varchar [note: 'Donor-specific budget line code or reference number required for grant reporting and financial compliance. Maps to donor financial systems.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this budget line belongs. Enables fiscal year-based budget reporting and analysis.']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The indirect cost rate applied to this budget line if it is a direct cost base. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 for 15%). Links to NICRA.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this budget line record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this budget line record was last updated. Critical for audit trail and version control.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the budget line item explaining the purpose, justification, and planned use of funds.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential or hierarchical line number within the budget plan for ordering and reference purposes (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or clarifications about this budget line item for internal reference and donor communication.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The number of units planned for this budget line item. Used with unit cost to calculate total planned amount.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any revision or amendment to this budget line. Required for donor reporting and audit trail.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'United Nations Sustainable Development Goal number(s) that this budget line contributes to (e.g., SDG 1, SDG 3, SDG 6). Supports impact reporting.']
  total_planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total budgeted amount for this line item, typically calculated as quantity multiplied by unit cost. Primary financial value for budget versus actual (BvA) tracking.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost per single unit of measure. Multiplied by quantity to derive the total planned amount for this line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit by which the budget item is measured (e.g., person-months, trips, units, days, hours, items, sessions).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this budget line record. Supports audit trail and data governance.']

  Note: 'Individual budget line item within a program budget, capturing cost category (personnel, fringe, travel, equipment, supplies, contractual, other direct costs, indirect costs), budget line description, unit cost, quantity, unit of measure, total planned amount, donor budget code, and cost-sharing flag. Enables granular budget management, BvA tracking linkage to finance domain, and donor financial reporting by budget category.'
}

  monitoring_visit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of field monitoring visits conducted to this partners implementation sites for this program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context about this program-partner linkage.']

  partnership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program-partner linkage (e.g., active, pending, suspended, terminated, completed, under review).']
  partnership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partnership arrangement (e.g., direct implementation, sub-award, technical assistance, coordination, joint implementation, government collaboration).']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall performance rating of the partner for this program implementation (e.g., outstanding, exceeds expectations, meets expectations, needs improvement, unsatisfactory, not rated).']
  performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance review or evaluation of the partners implementation activities.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for the partner to submit progress reports for this program (e.g., weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, ad-hoc). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weekly|biweekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|ad_hoc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to this partnership based on capacity assessment, due diligence, and operational context (e.g., low, medium, high, critical).']
  sector_focus varchar [note: 'Primary humanitarian or development sector(s) this partner is responsible for within the program (e.g., WASH, health, nutrition, education, protection, livelihoods). May be multi-sector.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the partner officially began implementation activities for this program.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason for termination if the partnership was ended prematurely (e.g., non-compliance, performance issues, security concerns, mutual agreement).']

  Note: 'Association entity capturing the relationship between a program intervention and its implementing partners (local NGOs, CBOs, INGOs, government counterparts). Stores partner role (lead implementer, sub-grantee, technical partner, government counterpart), partnership type, geographic scope of partnership, capacity assessment status, sub-award reference, start/end dates, and performance rating. Distinct from the partnership domain\'s MoU/LoA/SoW records which own the legal agreement — this product owns the program-specific partner assignment, role definition, and implementation performance tracking for each intervention.'
}

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mid_term_review|annual_review|after_action_review|donor_review_mission|quarterly_progress_report|semi_annual_report|annual_report|final_report|sitrep|flash_report|lessons_learned|best_practice_documentation|innovation_case_study|failure_analysis — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  executive_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the review event or report, capturing key messages for senior management and donors.']
  follow_up_actions varchar [note: 'List or narrative of follow-up actions agreed upon in response to the review findings and recommendations.']
  key_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of the key findings from the review, assessment, or reporting period. Captures evidence-based observations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review event record was last updated.']
  lessons_learned_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of lessons learned during the reporting period or review, intended to inform future programming and organizational learning.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'Formal response from program management to the findings and recommendations, including acceptance, rejection, or modification of recommendations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context related to this review event.']
  progress_narrative varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing progress against planned outputs, outcomes, and activities during the reporting period.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'List or narrative of recommendations arising from the review or report, intended to inform future program design and implementation.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this review or report.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this review or report.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which the review event or mission took place, or the date the report was finalized. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this record.']
  review_team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or narrative description of the review team members or contributors.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the scope of the review or report, including geographic areas, program components, and thematic focus.']
  sphere_standards_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Sphere Humanitarian Charter and Minimum Standards were applied or assessed in this review event.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the report or review findings were formally submitted to the intended audience (donor, management, cluster).']

  Note: 'Unified record for all formal program review, reporting, and learning events including mid-term reviews, annual reviews, after-action reviews, donor review missions, progress reports (quarterly, semi-annual, annual, final, SitRep, flash report), and structured learning outputs (lessons learned, best practices, innovations, failure analyses). Captures event/report type, reporting period, date range, review team/author, scope, key findings, narrative sections (progress against outputs, challenges, beneficiary reach figures, financial summary), recommendations, management response, follow-up actions, submission date, submission status, approval workflow, and dissemination status. Serves as the SSOT for all program knowledge events, donor/cluster reporting submissions, and internal governance reporting. Distinct from MEL domain evaluations (which own evaluation methodology, TOR, and evaluator management) — this product owns the program management reporting and review lifecycle.'
}

Table program.program {
  program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for program']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: NGO programs are managed through designated cost centers for organizational financial reporting, indirect cost allocation, and functional expense classification. A nonprofit CFO expects each program t']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Programs are anchored to and managed by country offices. Country offices own program budgets, staff, and donor relationships. This link is fundamental for country-level program portfolio management, a']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Emergency response programs are directly triggered by declared emergencies. Linking program to emergency enables emergency-specific program portfolio tracking, HRP progress reporting, and CERF/flash a']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Programs are owned and managed by specific org units (country offices, regional offices, HQ departments). This link is fundamental to organizational accountability reporting, budget allocation by unit']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the program proposal was formally approved by internal governance or donor.']
  closeout_date date [note: 'Date when all program activities, financial reconciliation, and final reporting were completed.']
  cluster_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the humanitarian cluster or sector coordination mechanism the program is aligned with (e.g., WASH, Health, Protection).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing with donor requirements, regulatory obligations, and internal policies.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for the primary country of program implementation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual end date when program activities conclude and closeout begins.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Spatial scale of program implementation indicating coverage area.']
  grant_number varchar [note: 'Unique grant or award number assigned by the donor for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program is classified as an emergency response intervention requiring expedited procedures.']
  is_multi_year boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program spans multiple fiscal years requiring multi-year budgeting and planning.']
  logframe_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document link to the program\'s Logical Framework Matrix defining objectives, indicators, and assumptions.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the program record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program record was last updated in the system.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for program monitoring and evaluation activities.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the program for identification across systems and donor reporting.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive narrative describing the program\'s objectives, scope, target population, and expected outcomes.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the humanitarian or development program as registered with donors and governing bodies.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program indicating its operational state.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program based on its primary intervention approach and duration.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or administrative area within the country where the program operates.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for submitting progress and financial reports to donors and stakeholders.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment level for the program considering contextual, operational, financial, and reputational risks.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SDG goal numbers (1-17) and targets that the program contributes to.']
  sector_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit code identifying the primary humanitarian or development sector (WASH, Health, Nutrition, Education, Protection, Livelihood, Shelter, etc.).']
  sector_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the primary sector in which the program operates.']
  start_date date [note: 'Official start date when program implementation begins as per the approved proposal or agreement.']
  subsector varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the primary sector (e.g., Water Supply, Sanitation, Hygiene Promotion under WASH).']
  target_beneficiaries varchar [note: 'Planned total number of direct beneficiaries the program aims to reach.']
  target_population varchar [note: 'Description of the intended beneficiary population including demographic characteristics, vulnerability criteria, and geographic scope.']
  theory_of_change varchar [note: 'Narrative or structured description of the causal pathway linking program inputs, activities, outputs, outcomes, and impact.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the program record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for program. Referenced by program_id.'
}

Table grant.award {
  award_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grant award record. Primary key for the award entity.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the institutional donor or funding agency that issued this award (USAID, BHA, DFID, CERF, UN agencies, foundations, corporates).']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Awards are managed and implemented by country offices. Core to operational structure, financial management, country-level portfolio analysis, compliance oversight, and donor relationship management at']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Awards are issued in direct response to declared emergencies (CERF, flash appeals, BHA emergency grants). Linking award to emergency is essential for emergency portfolio tracking, donor accountability']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Awards must be associated with the active fiscal period for period-end accruals, exchange rate application, and donor report generation. Finance teams use this link during period-end close to identify']
  funding_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.funding_source. Business justification: An award is funded by a specific funding source (e.g., USAID cooperative agreement, BHA grant mechanism). The funding_source table is the reference master for all donor funding mechanisms. Adding fund']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'FK to program.intervention.intervention_id — MUST-HAVE: Cannot link grants to the programs they fund without this join. Essential for BvA reporting, donor reporting, and program-grant traceability.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.program. Business justification: NGO grant portfolio management: awards fund programs, and program-level grant portfolio reporting requires direct award-to-program linkage. NGO finance directors need to aggregate all awards funding a']
  advance_payment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor allows advance payments or if reimbursement-only is required.']
  agreement_signed_date date [note: 'The date on which the award agreement was signed by both the donor and the NGO.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of amendments or modifications made to the original award agreement.']
  audit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the award requires a specific audit (e.g., A-133 single audit, donor-specific audit).']
  audit_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The expenditure threshold that triggers audit requirements under this award.']
  authorized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount authorized for expenditure under this award, which may differ from the obligated amount due to amendments or modifications.']
  award_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique award number assigned by the donor or funding agency. This is the official reference number used in all donor communications and reporting.']
  award_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the award: pipeline (anticipated), active (in execution), no-cost extension (extended without additional funds), suspended, or closed.']
  award_type varchar [note: 'The classification of the funding mechanism: cooperative agreement, contract, grant, sub-award, consortium lead, or consortium member.']
  branding_marking_requirements varchar [note: 'Donor-specific requirements for branding, marking, and visibility of donor support in program materials and communications.']
  closeout_date date [note: 'The date on which the award was officially closed out after all financial and programmatic reporting requirements were completed.']
  cost_share_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of cost-sharing required from the NGO in the award currency.']
  cost_share_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage of total project costs that must be contributed by the NGO as cost-sharing, expressed as a decimal.']
  cost_share_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the award requires the NGO to provide cost-sharing or matching funds.']
  currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the award amount is denominated.']
  dac_sector_code varchar [note: 'The OECD DAC 5-digit sector code classifying the purpose of the award.']
  donor_reference_number varchar [note: 'The donor\'s internal reference or tracking number for this award, distinct from the award number.']
  end_date date [note: 'The official end date of the award period of performance, including any approved extensions.']
  exchange_rate_to_functional decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate used to convert the award currency to the NGO\'s functional currency for accounting purposes.']
  functional_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the NGO\'s functional currency for financial reporting.']
  fund_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the award funds as unrestricted, temporarily restricted, or permanently restricted per ASC 958 accounting standards.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic area or countries covered by this award (e.g., single country, regional, global).']
  indirect_cost_ceiling decimal(5,4) [note: 'The maximum indirect cost rate allowed by the donor for this award, expressed as a decimal.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent amendment or modification to the award agreement.']
  nicra_icr_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The negotiated indirect cost rate applicable to this award, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 for 15%).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the award.']
  notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the NGO received official notification of the award from the donor.']
  original_end_date date [note: 'The original end date specified in the initial award agreement, before any no-cost extensions or amendments.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment method specified in the award: advance, reimbursement, milestone-based, or hybrid.']
  period_of_performance_months varchar [note: 'The total duration of the award period of performance expressed in months.']
  primary_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary country of implementation.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The applicable regulatory framework governing this award (e.g., 2 CFR 200, USAID ADS, DFID Smart Rules, UN Financial Regulations).']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which financial and programmatic reports must be submitted to the donor.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'The primary UN Sustainable Development Goal(s) that this award contributes to.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Any special conditions, restrictions, or requirements imposed by the donor on this award.']
  start_date date [note: 'The official start date of the award period of performance as specified in the award agreement.']
  thematic_sector varchar [note: 'The primary thematic sector or focus area of the award (e.g., WASH, health, education, protection, food security).']
  title varchar [note: 'The official title or name of the grant award as stated in the award agreement.']
  total_obligated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of funds obligated by the donor under this award in the award currency.']
  total_obligated_amount_functional decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total obligated amount converted to the NGO\'s functional currency using the exchange rate.']

  Note: 'Master record for each grant award received by the NGO from institutional donors (USAID, BHA, DFID, CERF, UN agencies, foundations, corporates). Captures the full grant lifecycle from award notification through closeout. Stores award number, title, donor reference, award type (cooperative agreement, contract, grant, sub-award, consortium lead, consortium member), total obligated amount in award currency with exchange rate to functional currency, authorized amount, start and end dates, period of performance, award status (pipeline, active, no-cost extension, closed), funding mechanism, geographic scope, thematic sector, applicable regulatory framework (2 CFR 200, USAID ADS, DFID Smart Rules), NICRA/ICR rate reference, restricted vs. unrestricted classification, indirect cost ceiling, and program linkage allocations with funding percentages. SSOT for all grant award identity in the NGO.'
}

Table grant.proposal {
  proposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grant proposal or concept note submission. Primary key for the proposal data product.']

  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: During proposal development, a preliminary budget is created in the finance system for cost proposal validation and indirect cost rate application. Business development officers reference this budget ']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or thematic area this proposal supports. Links to the program master entity for strategic alignment tracking.']
  consortium_lead_partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Proposals are frequently developed with a consortium lead partner. This FK replaces the denormalized consortium_lead_organization text field with a proper reference to partner_org, enabling business d']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor organization or funding body to whom this proposal is submitted. Links to the donor master entity.']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Proposals are prepared and submitted by country offices. Fundamental for tracking BD pipeline by geography, country-level win rates, proposal workload management, and resource allocation for proposal ']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Proposals are written in response to specific declared emergencies. Linking proposal to emergency supports emergency response pipeline tracking, go/no-go decisions tied to emergency severity, and dono']
  funding_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.funding_source. Business justification: A proposal is submitted in response to a specific funding opportunity from a funding source (e.g., a USAID NOFO, BHA solicitation). Linking proposal to funding_source allows tracking which funding mec']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: Proposals that win awards result in specific interventions. Essential for business development learning, proposal effectiveness tracking, and win/loss analysis. Tracks which proposal led to which impl']
  lead_proposal_writer_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: BD pipeline management and win/loss analysis require linking proposals to the staff member who led writing. Workload reporting, capacity planning, and staff performance reviews reference proposal auth']
  logframe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.mel_logframe. Business justification: Proposals reference donor-mandated logframes during design phase. Proposal writers must align technical approach with donor\'s results framework and indicator requirements specified in RFP logframe tem']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.prospect. Business justification: NGO business development pipeline tracks proposals against prospect records. Win/loss analysis by prospect cultivation stage, proposal conversion rates by prospect rating, and solicitation amount accu']
  award_notification_date date [note: 'Date the organization was officially notified of the award decision by the donor. Null if proposal was not awarded or decision is still pending.']
  business_development_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business development staff member responsible for managing this proposal opportunity and donor relationship.']
  compliance_review_completed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether compliance and risk review was completed before submission. Ensures adherence to donor regulations and organizational policies.']
  cost_proposal_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the cost proposal structure, including major budget categories, cost-sharing arrangements, and indirect cost rate applied. Provides financial overview without detailed line items.']
  cost_share_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost-sharing or matching contribution committed by the organization or partners, expressed in the proposal currency. Represents non-donor funding sources.']
  cost_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Cost-sharing contribution as a percentage of the total project budget. Calculated as cost share amount divided by total project cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this proposal record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL to the submitted proposal document package. Enables retrieval of the full proposal for audit and reference.']
  geographic_focus varchar [note: 'Primary country or region where the proposed intervention will be implemented. May include multiple countries for regional proposals.']
  go_no_go_decision varchar [note: 'Internal decision on whether to pursue this funding opportunity. Tracks strategic alignment, capacity assessment, and risk evaluation outcomes.']
  go_no_go_decision_date date [note: 'Date the internal go/no-go decision was made regarding pursuit of this funding opportunity.']
  indirect_cost_rate_proposed decimal(5,2) [note: 'Indirect cost rate (ICR) or facilities and administration (F&A) rate proposed in the budget, expressed as a percentage. May reference the organization\'s NICRA (Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement).']
  internal_review_date date [note: 'Date the proposal completed internal review and was approved for submission by organizational leadership or review committee.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this proposal record was last updated. Audit trail for record modification tracking.']
  lead_technical_sector varchar [note: 'Primary technical sector or thematic area for the proposed intervention (e.g., WASH, Health, Education, Protection, Livelihoods, GBV, Nutrition). Aligns with DAC sector codes and organizational program taxonomy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the proposal submission, donor feedback, or internal lessons learned.']
  partnership_model varchar [note: 'Organizational role and partnership structure for this proposal. Indicates whether the organization is applying as prime recipient, sub-awardee, consortium member, joint venture partner, or solo applicant.']
  proposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the proposal. Tracks progression from draft through internal review, submission, donor review, shortlisting, and final outcome (awarded, rejected, or withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|internal_review|submitted|under_review|shortlisted|awarded|rejected|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  proposal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the proposal submission format and stage. Indicates whether this is a full application, concept note, expression of interest, unsolicited submission, pre-proposal, or letter of inquiry.']
  proposed_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the proposed project period of performance expressed in months. Calculated from proposed start and end dates.']
  proposed_end_date date [note: 'Proposed end date for the period of performance if the grant is awarded. Marks the conclusion of the planned implementation timeline.']
  proposed_start_date date [note: 'Proposed start date for the period of performance if the grant is awarded. Marks the beginning of the planned implementation timeline.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number or code assigned to this proposal for tracking and communication with the donor. May include organizational prefix and year.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason provided by the donor for proposal rejection, or internal assessment of why the proposal was not successful. Used for lessons learned and win-rate improvement analysis.']
  requested_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total funding amount requested from the donor in the proposal currency. Represents the gross budget request before any donor adjustments or negotiations.']
  requested_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total funding amount requested converted to USD using the exchange rate at submission date. Enables cross-proposal comparison and pipeline analysis in a common currency.']
  requested_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the requested funding amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the proposal was officially submitted to the donor or funding body. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  target_beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of direct beneficiaries the proposed project aims to reach. Used for cost-per-beneficiary analysis and impact projections.']
  technical_approach_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the proposed technical approach, methodology, and intervention strategy. Captures key elements of the Theory of Change (ToC) and implementation plan.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full title of the grant proposal or concept note as submitted to the donor. Describes the proposed intervention or project.']
  win_loss_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the proposal submission. Indicates whether the proposal was won (awarded), lost (rejected), is still pending donor decision, or was withdrawn by the organization.']

  Note: 'Tracks each grant proposal or concept note submitted to a donor or funding body in response to a solicitation or as an unsolicited submission. Captures proposal title, submission date, donor solicitation reference (RFA, RFP, NOFO number), proposal type (full application, concept note, expression of interest, unsolicited), requested amount and currency, proposed period of performance, lead technical sector, proposal status (draft, internal review, submitted, under review, shortlisted, awarded, rejected, withdrawn), win/loss outcome, cost proposal summary, technical approach summary, and linkage to the resulting award if successful. Supports pipeline management, go/no-go decision tracking, win-rate analysis, and strategic business development. Each proposal references the solicitation it responds to (except unsolicited proposals).'
}

Table grant.award_budget {
  award_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the award budget record. Primary key.']
  approved_by_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Award budget approval requires a traceable internal approver for audit trails, segregation-of-duties compliance, and donor reporting. \'approved_by\' is a plain-text denormalized staff reference. Linkin']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent grant award for which this budget is defined.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Grant award budgets must integrate with finance budget records for consolidated financial planning, variance analysis, and donor financial reporting. Nonprofits maintain both grant-specific budget str']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Award budgets are structured by budget period aligned to fiscal periods. Finance teams match award budget periods to fiscal periods for period-end budget vs. actual reporting, exchange rate applicatio']
  grant_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.grant_amendment. Business justification: Budget revisions are often triggered by grant amendments. award_budget has is_amendment flag, amendment_date, and amendment_reason, but no FK to the actual amendment record. Adding amendment_id links ']
  grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: The donor-facing award_budget and internal finance grant_budget represent two sides of the same grant financial plan. Finance teams reconcile these during budget revisions and closeout. Linking them e']
  award_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the budget is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). This is the donor-facing currency.']
  budget_narrative_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document location for the detailed budget narrative that explains and justifies each budget line item. Typically a document management system reference.']
  budget_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, clarifications, or special conditions related to this budget version.']
  budget_period varchar [note: 'The budget period this budget covers, typically expressed as Year 1, Year 2, etc., or Period 1, Period 2, etc., or Full Award Period for multi-year consolidated budgets.']
  budget_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the budget period covered by this budget.']
  budget_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the budget period covered by this budget.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget. Draft indicates internal preparation; Submitted indicates sent to donor; Under Review indicates donor is reviewing; Approved indicates donor has approved; Amended indicates a revision has been made; Superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; Closed indicates budget period has ended. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|amended|superseded|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  budget_submission_date date [note: 'The date this budget version was submitted to the donor for review and approval.']
  budget_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the budget. Increments with each amendment or revision. Version 1 is the original approved budget.']
  contractual_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for sub-awards to implementing partners and consultant fees.']
  cost_share_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost share (matching funds) amount committed by the organization for this budget period, if cost sharing is required.']
  cost_share_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor requires cost sharing (matching funds) for this budget period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget record was first created in the system.']
  donor_approval_date date [note: 'The date the donor officially approved this budget version.']
  donor_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the donors formal approval of this budget (e.g., award letter number, amendment approval letter).']
  equipment_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for equipment purchases (typically items with acquisition cost of $5,000 or more and useful life of more than one year, per donor definition).']
  fringe_benefits_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for fringe benefits associated with personnel costs (health insurance, retirement, payroll taxes, etc.).']
  fund_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the funds according to donor restrictions. Unrestricted funds have no donor-imposed restrictions; Temporarily Restricted funds have purpose or time restrictions; Permanently Restricted funds must be maintained in perpetuity.']
  indirect_cost_base varchar [note: 'The cost base to which the NICRA rate is applied. Common bases include Modified Total Direct Costs (MTDC), Total Direct Costs, or Salaries and Wages.']
  is_amendment boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this budget version is an amendment to a previously approved budget (True) or the original budget (False).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget record was last updated in the system.']
  nicra_rate_applied decimal(5,2) [note: 'The indirect cost rate (expressed as a percentage) applied to calculate F&A costs. This is the rate from the organizations NICRA with the cognizant federal agency.']
  personnel_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for salaries and wages of staff directly working on the grant.']
  prepared_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the staff member who prepared this budget version.']
  supplies_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for supplies and consumables used in grant activities (items below equipment threshold).']
  total_approved_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total approved budget amount for this budget period in the award currency. Sum of all direct and indirect costs.']
  total_direct_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all direct cost categories (personnel, fringe, travel, equipment, supplies, contractual, other direct costs) approved for this budget period.']
  total_indirect_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total indirect costs (Facilities and Administration / F&A) approved for this budget period. Calculated by applying the NICRA rate to the approved direct cost base.']
  travel_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for domestic and international travel expenses directly related to grant activities.']

  Note: 'Defines the approved budget structure for a grant award as negotiated with the donor. Stores budget version number, budget period (Year 1, Year 2, etc.), total approved budget in award currency, direct cost ceiling, indirect cost (F&A) ceiling, NICRA rate applied, cost category breakdown (personnel, fringe, travel, equipment, supplies, contractual, other direct costs, indirect), donor-approved budget narrative reference, and budget amendment history flag. Enables BvA (Budget vs. Actual) tracking and donor financial reporting. Distinct from the finance domain\'s general ledger — this is the donor-facing approved budget in the award\'s denomination currency, not the internal GL in functional currency.'
}

Table grant.award_budget_line {
  award_budget_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the award budget line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  award_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the budget period (fiscal year or project phase) to which this line item applies.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent grant award under which this budget line is authorized.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.commodity. Business justification: Award budget lines for commodity procurement are tied to specific commodity types for donor budget compliance and pipeline planning. NGO supply chain and finance teams track which commodities are budg']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project that this budget line supports, enabling program-level budget tracking and reporting.']
  finance_budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Award budget line items must link to finance budget lines for detailed expenditure tracking, budget-to-actual variance reporting, and grant financial statement preparation. Nonprofits track grant budg']
  grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: Award budget lines (donor-approved line items) must reconcile to the internal grant_budget. This link enables variance analysis between donor-approved amounts and internal budget tracking, supporting ']
  indicator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.indicator. Business justification: Budget lines allocate resources to indicator data collection activities. MEL budget lines (surveys, assessments, evaluations) are directly tied to specific indicators they measure, enabling cost-per-i']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: Award budget lines must map to specific program interventions for cost allocation, financial reporting, and budget vs. actual analysis. Essential for donor financial reports requiring intervention-lev']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: NGOs allocate grant budget lines to specific authorized positions for personnel cost tracking, effort certification, and donor reporting on staffing costs. Essential for grant budget execution and com']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Budget lines are allocated to specific project sites for implementation. Site-level budget tracking is required for donor financial reporting, audit compliance (NICRA, single audit), and operational c']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.reporting_period. Business justification: Integrated financial-results reporting requires matching budget line expenditures to MEL reporting periods. NGO finance and MEL teams produce period-based burn rate vs. results achieved reports — a co']
  allocability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether costs charged to this budget line are allocable to the grant award in accordance with relative benefits received, per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.405.']
  allowability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget line item is allowable under applicable donor regulations (OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 Subpart E, USAID ADS). True if allowable; False if unallowable or requires special approval.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this budget line was approved by the donor or authorized signatory as part of the award agreement or amendment.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original approved budget amount for this line item as authorized in the initial grant award agreement.']
  approved_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget amount converted to United States Dollars for standardized reporting and analysis.']
  budget_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget line item within the award budget structure.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the budget line according to standard cost categories defined by OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 Subpart E and donor regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: personnel|fringe_benefits|travel|equipment|supplies|contractual|other_direct_costs|indirect_costs|subaward — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_share_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of cost sharing or matching contribution committed for this budget line. Null if no cost share is required.']
  cost_share_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget line requires cost sharing or matching funds from the organization or other non-federal sources, per donor agreement terms.']
  cost_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular detail within the cost category (e.g., international travel, local travel, office supplies, medical supplies).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget line record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_expenditure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred against this budget line from award inception through the current reporting period.']
  cumulative_expenditure_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative expenditure converted to United States Dollars for standardized variance analysis and donor reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget line amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  donor_reporting_category varchar [note: 'Donor-specific classification or reporting code required for financial reports submitted to the funding agency (e.g., BHA, USAID, DFID, CERF).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert local currency amounts to USD for donor reporting, if applicable. Null for USD-denominated budgets.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month, quarter, or custom period) within the fiscal year to which this budget line applies.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this budget line applies, aligned with the organizations or donors fiscal calendar.']
  fund_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of donor-imposed restrictions on the use of funds for this budget line, per FASB ASC 958 (Not-for-Profit Entities).']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code in the organizations chart of accounts (CoA) to which expenditures for this budget line are posted.']
  indirect_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated indirect cost (facilities and administration) amount allocated to this budget line based on the applied NICRA rate.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the budget line item, explaining the purpose, scope, and justification for the budgeted expenditure.']
  line_item_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying this budget line within the award budget structure, often aligned with donor or organizational chart of accounts.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget line record was last modified in the system.']
  nicra_rate_applied decimal(5,4) [note: 'Indirect cost rate (ICR) applied to this budget line, as specified in the organizations NICRA or the de minimis rate under 2 CFR 200.414. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.10 for 10%).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or clarifications related to this budget line item for internal tracking and audit purposes.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned quantity of the unit of measure for this budget line (e.g., 12 person-months, 500 units). Null for non-quantifiable line items.']
  reasonableness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budgeted amount is reasonable and does not exceed what a prudent person would incur under similar circumstances, per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.404.']
  revised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current authorized budget amount for this line item after any approved amendments, budget reallocations, or modifications. Null if no revisions have occurred.']
  revised_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revised budget amount converted to United States Dollars. Null if no revisions have occurred.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent budget revision or amendment affecting this line item. Null if no revisions have occurred.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the most recent budget revision, including justification and donor approval reference.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to supporting documentation for this budget line (e.g., quotes, justifications, donor correspondence).']
  unit_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost per unit of measure for this budget line. Null for non-quantifiable line items.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantifiable budget line items (e.g., person-months, units, trips, days), if applicable.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the current authorized budget (revised or approved) and cumulative expenditure. Positive indicates under-spend; negative indicates over-spend.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variance expressed as a percentage of the current authorized budget, used for burn-rate monitoring and budget-versus-actual (BvA) analysis.']

  Note: 'Granular line-item breakdown of an approved award budget. Each record represents a single budget line within a cost category and budget period, capturing line item code, cost category (personnel, fringe, travel, equipment, supplies, contractual, ODC, indirect), budget line description, approved amount, revised amount (post-amendment), cumulative expenditure to date, variance, and allowability flag under applicable donor regulations (2 CFR 200 Subpart E, USAID ADS). Enables detailed BvA analysis, burn-rate monitoring, and donor financial report preparation at the line-item level.'
}

This FK identifies which partnership agreement must be amended when a grant amendment i']

  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.constituent. Business justification: Amendments require donor prior approval and are communicated directly to the donor constituent. NGO compliance teams run \'all amendments pending approval from donor X\' reports. The existing approved_b']

Lin']
  grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: Grant amendments modify budget amounts, period of performance, and scope. Business justification: Grant amendment preparation is a named compliance workflow requiring staff accountability for audit trails. Amendments affecting budget, scope, or timeline must be traceable to the preparing staff mem']
  supersedes_amendment_grant_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a previous amendment that this amendment supersedes or replaces. Used to track amendment version history.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the changes introduced by this amendment. Explains the rationale, scope, and impact of the modification.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential or hierarchical identifier for this amendment within the grant lifecycle (e.g., Amendment 001, A-1, Mod 3). Serves as the business identifier for the amendment.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the amendment. Tracks the amendment from initial drafting through donor approval and execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|executed|rejected|withdrawn|superseded — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the amendment based on the nature of the modification. Indicates whether the amendment extends the period, modifies the budget, changes scope, updates key personnel, expands geographic coverage, or adjusts other terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_cost_extension|budget_modification|scope_change|key_personnel_change|geographic_expansion|period_of_performance_change|funding_increase|funding_decrease|administrative_correction|terms_and_conditions_change — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the donor or funding agency officially approved the amendment request.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this amendment record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this amendment (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  donor_approval_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or document identifier provided by the donor agency confirming approval of the amendment (e.g., USAID approval memo number, BHA authorization code).']
  donor_prior_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment required formal prior approval from the donor agency before implementation, as mandated by OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.308.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the amendment terms become effective. May differ from execution date if the amendment is retroactive or has a future effective date.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment was fully executed and became legally binding. This is the principal business event timestamp for the amendment.']
  funding_change decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net change in funding resulting from this amendment. Positive values indicate funding increases, negative values indicate decreases. Zero for non-financial amendments.']
  geographic_change_description varchar [note: 'Description of any changes to the geographic coverage or implementation locations resulting from this amendment (e.g., expansion to new regions, closure of field sites).']
  internal_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment request was approved internally by the nonprofit organization before submission to the donor.']
  internal_approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal organizational authority who approved the amendment request (e.g., Executive Director, Chief Grants Officer).']
  is_no_cost_extension boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment is a no-cost extension, extending the period of performance without additional funding.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business and programmatic justification for requesting the amendment. Explains why the modification is necessary and how it aligns with program objectives.']
  key_personnel_change_description varchar [note: 'Description of any changes to key personnel roles, including additions, removals, or role modifications. Key personnel typically include Principal Investigator, Project Director, and other critical leadership positions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this amendment record was last updated in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or contextual information related to the amendment. Used for internal tracking and audit purposes.']
  original_end_date date [note: 'Original end date of the grant period of performance before this amendment.']
  original_start_date date [note: 'Original start date of the grant period of performance before this amendment.']
  original_total_obligation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total obligated funding amount before this amendment. Provides baseline for calculating the impact of funding changes.']
  period_extension_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the period of performance is extended by this amendment. Calculated as the difference between revised and original end dates.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment request was formally submitted to the donor or funding agency for review and approval.']
  revised_end_date date [note: 'Revised end date of the grant period of performance after this amendment. Commonly modified in no-cost extension amendments.']
  revised_start_date date [note: 'Revised start date of the grant period of performance after this amendment. Typically unchanged unless the amendment is retroactive.']
  revised_total_obligation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total obligated funding amount after applying this amendment. Equals original total obligation plus amendment funding change.']
  scope_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any changes to the programmatic scope, objectives, deliverables, or activities resulting from this amendment.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to supporting documentation for the amendment (e.g., revised budget, updated work plan, donor correspondence).']
  terms_and_conditions_change varchar [note: 'Description of any modifications to the legal terms, conditions, reporting requirements, or compliance obligations under the grant agreement.']

  Note: 'Records all formal modifications to a grant award after initial execution. Captures amendment number, amendment type (no-cost extension, budget modification, scope change, key personnel change, geographic expansion, period of performance change), amendment date, donor approval reference, description of changes, revised total obligation, revised end date, and amendment status (pending, approved, executed). Tracks the full amendment history for compliance and audit purposes under OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 and USAID ADS.'
}

  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Each subaward is funded from a specific restricted fund.   grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: Subaward disbursements are drawn from a specific grant budget. Finance teams monitor subaward spend against the grant_budget for burn rate analysis, remaining obligation tracking, and donor financial ']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program under which this subaward is executed. Links the subaward to the programmatic context and objectives.']
  manager_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member.   amendment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of amendments or modifications issued to this subaward. Tracks the change history of the subaward.']

  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved the subaward on behalf of the NGO.']
  closeout_date date [note: 'The date when the subaward was officially closed out after all deliverables, reports, and financial reconciliations were completed.']
  cost_share_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The required cost share or matching contribution amount from the implementing partner, if applicable.']
  cost_share_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the implementing partner is required to provide cost share or matching funds as a condition of the subaward.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this subaward record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the subaward amount. Defines the currency in which the subaward is denominated.']
  disbursed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount disbursed to the implementing partner to date. Tracks actual cash transferred against the obligated amount.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'The nine-digit DUNS number of the implementing partner organization. Required for FFATA reporting and federal subawards.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date when the subaward agreement was signed and executed by both parties. Represents the legal effective date of the subaward.']
  ffata_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subaward is subject to FFATA reporting requirements (typically subawards of $30,000 or more from federal sources).']
  flow_down_requirements varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the prime award terms and conditions that must be flowed down to the subrecipient, including compliance obligations, reporting requirements, and audit provisions.']
  fsrs_report_date date [note: 'The date when the subaward was reported to the FFATA Subaward Reporting System (FSRS), if applicable.']
  fund_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the funding restriction level per ASC 958 nonprofit accounting standards. Indicates donor-imposed restrictions on the use of funds.']
  indirect_cost_base varchar [note: 'The cost base to which the indirect cost rate is applied (e.g., Modified Total Direct Costs, Total Direct Costs, Salaries and Wages).']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The approved indirect cost rate (ICR) or Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) rate applicable to the implementing partner for this subaward, expressed as a percentage.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this subaward record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for record updates.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'The required frequency of monitoring activities for this subaward, based on risk rating and donor requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the subaward. Free-text field for capturing contextual information.']
  obligated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current total obligated amount under the subaward, including any amendments. May differ from the original total subaward amount due to modifications.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which payments are made to the implementing partner under this subaward.']
  payment_schedule varchar [note: 'The schedule or basis on which payments are made to the implementing partner (e.g., advance, reimbursement, milestone-based).']
  period_of_performance_end_date date [note: 'The date when the subaward period of performance ends. Defines the end of the implementing partners authorized work period.']
  period_of_performance_start_date date [note: 'The date when the subaward period of performance begins. Defines the start of the implementing partners authorized work period.']
  remaining_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unspent balance of the subaward. Calculated as obligated amount minus disbursed amount.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The required frequency of programmatic and financial reports from the implementing partner.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'The assessed risk level of the implementing partner for this subaward. Determines the level of monitoring and oversight required.']
  single_audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the implementing partner is required to undergo a Single Audit per 2 CFR 200 Subpart F based on federal expenditure thresholds.']
  subaward_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the subaward scope, objectives, deliverables, and expected outcomes.']
  subaward_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier for this subaward. Used in all legal documents, reporting, and correspondence.']
  subaward_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subaward. Tracks the subaward from initial draft through active performance to closeout.']
  subaward_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subaward instrument type. Determines the legal and compliance framework applicable to the subaward.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date when the subaward was terminated, if applicable. Used when the subaward is ended prior to the planned end date.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the reason for subaward termination, if applicable. Documents the circumstances and justification for early termination.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the subaward that summarizes the scope of work or project purpose.']
  total_subaward_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total obligated amount of the subaward in the subaward currency. Represents the maximum financial obligation to the implementing partner.']
  total_subaward_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total subaward amount converted to USD for consolidated reporting and analysis. Conversion uses the exchange rate at the time of subaward execution.']
  uei_number varchar [note: 'The twelve-character alphanumeric Unique Entity Identifier assigned to the implementing partner by SAM.gov. Replaces DUNS for federal reporting.']

  Note: 'Manages sub-awards issued by the NGO as prime recipient to implementing partners (CBOs, local NGOs, contractors). Captures subaward number, type (sub-grant, subcontract, fixed-obligation grant), implementing partner reference, subaward amount, period of performance, flow-down requirements from prime award, FFATA reporting obligation flag, subaward status (active, suspended, terminated, closed), risk rating, monitoring schedule, and pass-through entity compliance obligations under 2 CFR 200.331. Distinct from the partnership domain\'s MoU/LoA — this is the financial and compliance instrument with legal obligation.'
}

  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or employee who is accountable for ensuring this donor condition is met.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the donor condition was actually met or the required action was completed. Null if the condition is not yet met.']

  approval_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the formal approval document or communication (e.g., approval letter number, email reference).']

  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the donor condition.
