  event_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fundraising event is scheduled to begin.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fundraising event (planned, active, completed, cancelled, postponed).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fundraising event by format and purpose (e.g., gala, benefit dinner, golf tournament, auction, virtual fundraiser, cultivation event).']
  fundraising_goal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The target revenue amount the organization aims to raise from this event.']
  is_tax_deductible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contributions made to this event qualify for tax deduction under IRS 501(c)(3) regulations or equivalent.']
  is_virtual_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fundraising event is conducted virtually (online) rather than at a physical venue.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the system user who last modified this fundraising event record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fundraising event record was last updated or modified.']
  net_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net proceeds from the event, calculated as total revenue raised minus total event cost.']
  registration_close_date date [note: 'The date when registration for the event closes and no further registrations are accepted.']
  registration_open_date date [note: 'The date when registration for the event opens to constituents.']
  sponsorship_levels_available varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of sponsorship tiers offered for the event (e.g., Platinum, Gold, Silver, Bronze).']
  tax_deductible_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of ticket price or contribution that qualifies as a tax-deductible charitable donation (e.g., 70% if 30% represents fair market value of goods/services received).']
  ticket_price_tiers varchar [note: 'Description or list of ticket pricing options available for attendees (e.g., General Admission, VIP, Student, Early Bird).']
  total_attendance varchar [note: 'The actual number of individuals who attended the fundraising event, confirmed via check-in or participation tracking.']
  total_event_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total expenses incurred to plan, organize, and execute the fundraising event (venue, catering, marketing, staff, etc.).']
  total_registrations varchar [note: 'The total number of individuals who registered for the fundraising event.']
  total_revenue_raised decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual total revenue generated from the fundraising event, including ticket sales, sponsorships, donations, and auction proceeds.']
  venue_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address for the event venue.']
  venue_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address for the event venue (suite, floor, building).']
  venue_city varchar [note: 'City where the event venue is located.']
  venue_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the event venue location.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'The name of the physical or virtual venue where the fundraising event is hosted.']
  venue_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the event venue location.']
  venue_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the event venue is located.']
  virtual_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the online platform or service used to host the virtual fundraising event (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, custom web portal).']

  Note: 'Master record for donor-facing fundraising events and attendee registrations including galas, benefit dinners, golf tournaments, virtual fundraisers, and cultivation events managed in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud. Captures event name, event type, event date and venue, fundraising goal, ticket price tiers, sponsorship levels, total revenue raised, total attendance, event cost, net revenue, and event manager. Includes individual constituent registration records with registration date, registration type (attendee, table sponsor, individual sponsor, volunteer), ticket type and price paid, table assignment, meal preference, attendance confirmation status, check-in timestamp, and post-event follow-up status. Enables event capacity management, seating logistics, and post-event stewardship workflows. Distinct from field operations events — fundraising events are donor cultivation and revenue generation activities.'
}

Table field.project_site {
  project_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the project site. Primary key for the project site entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Project sites are established and operated under specific partnership agreements defining geographic scope, activities, and funding. Program managers and auditors must trace which agreement governs ea']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Project sites are typically cost centers in nonprofit accounting. All site expenses roll up to the site\'s cost center for financial reporting, budget vs. actual tracking, and management reporting.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: project_site has country_code (STRING) but no FK to the country reference table. The country table contains authoritative country metadata including codes, names, region, operational status, and human']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the country office that oversees operations at this project site.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Field sites are funded by specific restricted or unrestricted funds. Program and finance managers need to track which fund supports each site for donor compliance reporting and fund utilization tracki']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the humanitarian program under which this project site operates.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Project sites in certain jurisdictions require site-specific compliance obligations (environmental permits, local authority operating approvals, land use permits). Linking project_site to obligation e']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization responsible for or collaborating on operations at this project site.']
  site_manager_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: project_site.site_manager_name is a classic denormalization of a staff_member FK. Site manager accountability, performance reviews, and operational reporting all require a proper FK. site_manager_name']
  statutory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.statutory_registration. Business justification: Project sites operate under legal registrations in their jurisdictions. Site establishment requires verification that the organization has legal operating authority (MOU, registration) in that locatio']
  accessibility_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of physical accessibility to the project site considering road conditions, security, terrain, and seasonal factors.']
  admin_level_1 varchar [note: 'First-level administrative division (e.g., state, province, region) where the project site is located.']
  admin_level_2 varchar [note: 'Second-level administrative division (e.g., district, county, department) where the project site is located.']
  admin_level_3 varchar [note: 'Third-level administrative division (e.g., municipality, sub-district, commune) where the project site is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the project site was officially closed or decommissioned. Null for active sites.']
  cluster_affiliation varchar [note: 'OCHA humanitarian cluster(s) that this project site is affiliated with for coordination purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health|nutrition|wash|shelter|protection|education|food_security|logistics|emergency_telecom|camp_coordination|early_recovery — promote to reference product]']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official communication with the project site.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for reaching the project site or site manager.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this project site record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this project site record originated (e.g., KoboToolbox, Dynamics 365, OpenStreetMap).']
  electricity_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether electrical power is available at the project site.']
  establishment_date date [note: 'Date when the project site was officially established and became operational.']
  facility_ownership varchar [note: 'Legal ownership or usage arrangement for the physical facility at the project site.']
  gis_data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or method used to capture GIS coordinates for this project site (e.g., GPS device, OpenStreetMap, satellite imagery).']
  internet_connectivity varchar [note: 'Type of internet connectivity available at the project site for data collection and reporting.']
  kobo_collection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mobile data collection via KoboToolbox is enabled and operational at this site.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent field assessment (FGD, KII, or site visit) conducted at this project site.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the project site in decimal degrees format. Used for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the project site in decimal degrees format. Used for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this project site record was last modified in the system.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the project site in its lifecycle.']
  pcode varchar [note: 'OCHA-assigned unique place code (P-code) for standardized geographic referencing in humanitarian operations and reporting.']
  population_catchment varchar [note: 'Estimated number of beneficiaries or persons of concern (PoC) within the service catchment area of this project site.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the project site location, if applicable.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Current security risk assessment level for the project site based on threat analysis and security incidents.']
  site_address varchar [note: 'Physical street address or location description of the project site.']
  site_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total physical area of the project site measured in square meters.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the project site for field operations and reporting. Used in SitReps and OCHA coordination.']
  site_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the project site including physical characteristics, surrounding environment, and operational context.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the project site or operational location.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the project site based on its primary function and service delivery model.']
  water_source_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a reliable water source is available at or near the project site for WASH operations.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical field project site or operational location where humanitarian programs are delivered. Captures site identity, GIS coordinates, administrative boundaries (country, region, district), site type (health post, distribution point, WASH facility, school, camp), operational status, accessibility rating, population catchment area, cluster affiliation, and OCHA P-code. Serves as the geographic anchor for all field operations, service delivery events, and SitRep generation.'
}

Table field.country_office {
  country_office_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the country office record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Country offices are organizational cost centers. All country-level expenses are tracked against the office\'s cost center for financial management, budget tracking, and HQ consolidation reporting.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: country_office has iso_country_code and country_name as STRING attributes but no FK to the country reference table. The country table contains authoritative country data including country_code_alpha2,']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Country offices are the field operational entity; org units are the HR/finance organizational entity for the same real-world office. Linking them enables integrated HR headcount reporting, budget vs. ']
  parent_office_country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent country office or regional office to which this office reports, enabling hierarchical office structure.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address or building location of the country office.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, floor, or building name.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the country office is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the country office ceased operations or was officially closed.']
  country_director_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the country director for official communications.']
  country_director_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the senior management official responsible for leading the country office operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country office record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the primary local currency used in financial operations at this country office.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official correspondence with the country office.']
  establishment_date date [note: 'Date when the country office was first established and began operations.']
  is_emergency_response boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this office was established specifically for emergency or humanitarian crisis response operations.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the office location in decimal degrees for Geographic Information System (GIS) mapping and field coordination.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name under which the country office is registered with host government authorities, which may differ from the operational office name.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the office location in decimal degrees for Geographic Information System (GIS) mapping and field coordination.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country office record was last modified in the system.']
  mou_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the host government became effective.']
  mou_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the host government expires.']
  mou_with_government boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) is in place with the host government defining the terms of operation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the country office, including operational context, special considerations, or historical information.']
  ocha_cluster_participation varchar [note: 'List of Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) humanitarian clusters in which this country office actively participates (e.g., WASH, Health, Protection, Education).']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the country office following organizational naming convention (e.g., KEN-NAI01 for Nairobi office in Kenya).']
  office_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the country office or sub-office (e.g., Kenya Country Office, Nairobi Sub-Office).']
  office_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the office based on its operational mandate and scope within the organizational hierarchy.']
  operational_mandate varchar [note: 'Description of the office\'s operational mandate, including sectors of focus (e.g., Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH), health, education, protection) and target populations served.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the country office indicating whether it is actively managing field operations.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the country office.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the office location.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the country office was officially registered with the host government.']
  registration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current host government registration expires and requires renewal.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number or certificate number issued by the host government for the country office\'s legal operations.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the office\'s legal registration with the host government authorities, required for operational legitimacy.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Current security risk assessment level for the country office location, informing operational protocols and staff safety measures.']
  staff_count varchar [note: 'Total number of staff members (national and international) currently assigned to this country office.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region within the host country.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the country office location (e.g., Africa/Nairobi, Asia/Damascus) for scheduling and coordination purposes.']

  Note: 'Master record for each NGO country office or sub-office managing field operations within a specific country or region. Captures office name, ISO country code, office type (country office, sub-office, field hub, liaison office), physical address, operational mandate, registration status with host government, legal entity details, and responsible senior management. Links to project sites, grants, and workforce assignments within that country.'
}

Table field.distribution_event {
  distribution_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution event. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Distributions are often governed by specific partnership agreements defining scope, modalities, beneficiary targeting, and compliance requirements. Linking enables agreement-level expenditure tracking']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant funding this distribution event.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Distribution events consume approved budget lines. distribution_event.budget_allocated_amount is a denormalized budget figure; linking to budget_line enables real-time burn-rate monitoring and donor f']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Distributions are charged to cost centers for expense tracking. Finance needs this for cost allocation, budget vs. actual reporting, and management reporting by cost center.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: distribution_event has country_code (STRING) but no FK to the country reference table. Distribution events are country-specific operations requiring linkage to country-level operational context includ']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Distribution events must comply with specific donor requirements (beneficiary targeting criteria, verification methods, visibility rules). Linking distribution_event to donor_requirement enables compl']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Distribution events (NFI, food, cash, voucher) are often conducted as part of emergency response operations. Linking distribution_event to emergency enables direct reporting on distributions per emerg']
  field_deployment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.field_deployment. Business justification: Distribution events are executed by deployed field teams during specific deployment periods. While distribution_event already has field_team_id, linking to the specific field_deployment record enables']
  field_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field team responsible for implementing this distribution event.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Each distribution is charged to a specific fund (often donor-restricted). Financial managers must track fund utilization by distribution for donor reporting, fund balance management, and compliance.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.household. Business justification: Distributions target households as the unit of food/NFI assistance. Core to food security programming, household entitlement tracking, and distribution planning. Standard practice in WFP, UNHCR, and g']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent program under which this distribution event is conducted.']
  meal_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.meal_plan. Business justification: Distribution events have pdm_scheduled_flag and pdm_completion_date governed by the MEAL plan\'s PDM cycle requirements. MEAL coordinators must link distributions to the MEAL plan to enforce post-distr']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Distribution events are frequently implemented by partner organizations in humanitarian operations. Tracking the implementing partner is essential for accountability reporting, partner performance man']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project site where the distribution event took place.']
  registrant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.registrant. Business justification: Individual distributions (cash transfers, vouchers, individual rations) target specific beneficiaries. Essential for individual entitlement tracking, cash programming, and beneficiary-level distributi']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.reporting_period. Business justification: Distribution events are aggregated by MEL reporting period to populate indicator results (beneficiaries reached, households served) for donor reports. MEL teams must link each distribution event to it']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who approved the distribution event plan.']
  actual_beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of beneficiaries who received assistance in this distribution event, verified through registration or biometric verification.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the distribution event concluded.']
  actual_expenditure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred for this distribution event, including commodity costs, logistics, and operational expenses.']
  actual_household_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of households that received assistance in this distribution event.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the distribution event commenced on the ground.']
  admin_level_1 varchar [note: 'First-level administrative division (e.g., governorate, province, state) where the distribution event took place.']
  admin_level_2 varchar [note: 'Second-level administrative division (e.g., district, county) where the distribution event took place.']
  admin_level_3 varchar [note: 'Third-level administrative division (e.g., sub-district, municipality) where the distribution event took place.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the distribution event plan was approved by the program manager or field coordinator.']
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget amount allocated for this distribution event, used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) reconciliation.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of items distributed in this event. For NFI distributions may include shelter materials, hygiene kits, blankets; for food distributions may include staple foods, fortified foods; for WASH may include water containers, purification tablets. Pipe-enum overflow candidate with 15+ standard categories.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this distribution event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this distribution event.']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Tool or method used to collect field data during the distribution event: KoboToolbox, CommCare, ODK (Open Data Kit), paper form, mobile app, or tablet-based system.']
  distribution_event_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the distribution event, typically formatted as country-cluster-sequence (e.g., SYR-WASH-000123).']
  distribution_event_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the distribution event for reporting and communication purposes.']
  distribution_location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution event location, captured via GPS or GIS system.']
  distribution_location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution event location, captured via GPS or GIS system.']
  distribution_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the distribution location (e.g., community center, camp sector, village name).']
  distribution_modality varchar [note: 'Method of assistance delivery: in-kind (physical goods), cash (physical currency), voucher (paper/card voucher), mobile money (digital wallet), e-transfer (bank transfer), or hybrid (combination of modalities).']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the distribution event in the operational workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|suspended|under_review — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distribution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution approach: general (universal coverage), targeted (specific beneficiary criteria), blanket (entire population segment), voucher redemption (voucher exchange), emergency response (rapid onset), or seasonal (recurring planned distribution).']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any incident, disruption, or complaint that occurred during the distribution event.']
  incident_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any security incident, operational disruption, or beneficiary complaint was reported during this distribution event.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this distribution event record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, observations, or context about the distribution event.']
  pdm_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the Post-Distribution Monitoring activity for this distribution event was completed.']
  pdm_scheduled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Post-Distribution Monitoring activity has been scheduled for this distribution event to assess beneficiary satisfaction and assistance effectiveness.']
  planned_beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Target number of beneficiaries planned to receive assistance in this distribution event.']
  planned_household_count varchar [note: 'Target number of households planned to receive assistance in this distribution event.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the distribution event to take place.']
  sitrep_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution event has been included in a Situation Report (SitRep) submitted to OCHA or other coordination bodies.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify beneficiary identity and eligibility at the distribution point: biometric (fingerprint/iris), token (physical token), ID card (government ID), beneficiary list (pre-registered list), household head (representative), or mobile verification (SMS/app-based).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a single distribution event (NFI, food, cash, or voucher) conducted at a project site. Captures event date, site, distribution type (general, targeted, blanket, voucher redemption), planned vs. actual beneficiary count, commodity categories, distribution modality (in-kind, cash, voucher, mobile money), responsible cluster, implementing field team, verification method, and PDM scheduling status. Core operational transaction for field service delivery and the primary unit of work for BvA reconciliation against supply pipeline.'
}

Table field.distribution_line {
  distribution_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution line item. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Each distribution line item is funded and governed by a specific partnership agreement. Financial reconciliation, donor earmark tracking, and IATI transaction reporting require agreement-level attribu']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or funding source that financed this distribution line item. Enables grant-level expenditure tracking and compliance reporting.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Budget vs. actuals reconciliation at line-item level is a core donor reporting requirement. Each distribution line must be traceable to an approved budget line to validate allowable costs and donor ea']
  distribution_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent distribution event header. Links this line item to the overall distribution activity.']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Distribution lines must comply with donor earmarking and targeting requirements. The existing donor_earmark text field is a denormalized representation of the donor_requirement entity. A proper FK enf']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each distribution line item posts to a specific GL account (food commodities, NFI, cash transfers). Required for financial statement preparation, expense classification, and audit trail.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Distribution lines record which partner org executed delivery of specific commodity line items. Donor commodity tracking, IATI transaction reporting, and partner accountability require partner attribu']
  actual_quantity_distributed decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of the item that was distributed to beneficiaries. Used for BvA (Budget versus Actual) reconciliation and MEL reporting.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number of the distributed item. Critical for traceability, quality control, and recall management, especially for food, health, and pharmaceutical items.']
  beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual beneficiaries or households who received this specific item in this distribution line. Supports disaggregated MEL reporting.']
  cluster_sector varchar [note: 'OCHA humanitarian cluster or sector to which this distribution line item is attributed (e.g., Food Security, WASH, Shelter, Health, Protection).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit_value and total_value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'The method or modality used to distribute this item (e.g., direct hand distribution, voucher redemption, cash transfer for purchase, mobile distribution unit, fixed distribution site).']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this distribution line item within the distribution event lifecycle.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the distributed item. Relevant for perishable goods, food, medicines, and time-sensitive NFIs.']
  iati_transaction_type varchar [note: 'IATI-compliant transaction type code for this distribution line, supporting international aid transparency and reporting.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the distributed item. NFI = Non-Food Item, WASH = Water Sanitation and Hygiene.']
  item_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific commodity or NFI item. May align with supply chain master item codes or donor-specific item catalogs.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the distributed item including specifications, brand, quality grade, or other distinguishing characteristics.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the distribution event. Used for ordering and referencing specific items within the distribution.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this distribution line record. Supports accountability and audit trail.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution line record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual information, special instructions, or observations related to this distribution line item.']
  pipeline_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supply pipeline or procurement source from which the item was drawn (e.g., central warehouse, local procurement, in-kind donation, CERF allocation).']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of the item that was planned or targeted for distribution in this line item, as per the distribution plan or LogFrame target.']
  quality_check_date date [note: 'Date when the quality inspection was performed on this distribution line item.']
  quality_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of quality inspection performed on this item batch before or during distribution. Ensures compliance with SPHERE and CHS standards.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Reference to the UN Sustainable Development Goal(s) that this distribution line item contributes to (e.g., SDG 2: Zero Hunger, SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation).']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of this distribution line (typically unit_value × actual_quantity_distributed). Supports financial reconciliation and grant expenditure tracking.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the item quantity is measured (e.g., pieces, kilograms, liters, boxes, kits, households).']
  unit_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value per unit of the distributed item. Used for financial tracking and donor reporting.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between planned_quantity and actual_quantity_distributed. Positive values indicate over-distribution, negative values indicate under-distribution. Used for BvA analysis.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any significant variance between planned and actual quantities. Supports accountability and learning in MEL processes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this distribution line record. Supports accountability and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a distribution event capturing each commodity or NFI item distributed. Records item category, item description, unit of measure, planned quantity, actual quantity distributed, unit value, total value, donor earmarking, and pipeline source. Enables granular tracking of what was distributed per event and supports BvA reconciliation against supply chain records.'
}

  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Assessments are conducted within the operational scope of a country office.   donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Linking assessment to donor_requirement enables compliance tracking of whether each required asse']

  field_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.field_team. Business justification: Assessments are conducted by field teams (FGD, KII, household surveys, PDM). The assessment table already has team_lead_staff_member_id (individual staff) but lacks a FK to the field_team entity that ']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Assessments are funded activities charged to specific funds. Finance needs to track assessment costs against fund budgets for donor reporting and fund management.']
  indicator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.indicator. Business justification: Assessments (baseline, endline, needs) directly measure indicator progress. Nonprofit M&E requires linking assessment findings to specific indicators for results reporting and donor compliance. No exi']
  internal_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.internal_review. Business justification: Assessments undergo internal compliance reviews for methodology quality, data integrity, and adherence to donor/organizational standards. Compliance teams review assessment protocols and findings befo']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: Assessments (needs assessments, PDM, post-distribution monitoring) inform intervention design, measure intervention effectiveness, and provide evidence for program amendments. While some assessments l']
  meal_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.meal_plan. Business justification: Assessments are planned and executed under a governing MEAL plan that defines assessment strategy, methodology, and schedule. A MEAL coordinator would expect every field assessment to reference the ME']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Assessments (needs assessments, PDM, evaluations) are frequently conducted by or jointly with partner organizations. Tracking the partner assessor enables data quality control, capacity building plann']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project site where this assessment was conducted.']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.reporting_period. Business justification: Assessments are conducted within MEL reporting periods and their findings feed logframe verification and donor reporting. MEL coordinators track which reporting period each assessment belongs to for p']
  team_lead_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Assessments require designated team lead for operational accountability, coordination, and donor reporting compliance. Essential for tracking who is responsible for assessment quality and findings val']
  adequacy_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'For PDM assessments: score representing beneficiary assessment of whether the distributed items met their needs in terms of quantity, quality, and timeliness. Typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the assessment, used in reporting and coordination with partners.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date when the field assessment was conducted or initiated. Principal business event timestamp for this assessment.']
  assessment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the assessment, describing its purpose and scope.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment. Tracks progression from planning through validation.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment methodology. FGD (Focus Group Discussion), KII (Key Informant Interview), PDM (Post-Distribution Monitoring), MUAC (Mid-Upper Arm Circumference) screening for malnutrition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FGD|KII|household_survey|rapid_needs_assessment|PDM|market_assessment|MUAC_screening|baseline_assessment|endline_assessment|midterm_review — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  beneficiary_satisfaction_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall satisfaction rating provided by beneficiaries regarding the program or distribution. Supports Accountability to Affected Populations (AAP) and CHS compliance.']
  cluster_coordination_body varchar [note: 'The OCHA cluster or sector coordination group to which this assessment is reported (e.g., WASH Cluster, Health Cluster, Food Security Cluster).']
  complaints_received_count varchar [note: 'Number of complaints or grievances raised by beneficiaries during the assessment. Critical for accountability and feedback mechanisms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assessment record was first created in the system.']
  data_collection_tool varchar [note: 'The digital or physical tool used to capture assessment data in the field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KoboToolbox|CommCare|ODK|paper_form|mobile_app|DHIS2|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal quality score assessing completeness, accuracy, and reliability of the assessment data. Used for MEL quality control.']
  donor_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment is designated for inclusion in donor reports and external visibility materials.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the assessment fieldwork was completed.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic coverage level of the assessment.']
  key_findings_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of the main findings, insights, and observations from the assessment. Used for SitRep generation and donor reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assessment record was last updated.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the primary assessment location. Supports GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the primary assessment location. Supports GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  mel_indicator_linked boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this assessment is linked to specific MEL indicators in the logical framework (LogFrame) or results framework.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'The research methodology employed for data collection and analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional contextual notes, observations, or operational details about the assessment not captured in structured fields.']
  protection_concerns_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any protection concerns identified, including nature of risk and affected population. Confidential to ensure beneficiary safety.']
  protection_concerns_noted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether any protection concerns (GBV, child protection, safety risks) were identified during the assessment. Triggers referral protocols.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Actionable recommendations derived from the assessment findings for program adjustments, response planning, or advocacy.']
  report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the full assessment report document stored in the document management system or shared drive.']
  sample_methodology varchar [note: 'The sampling approach used to select assessment participants. Particularly important for PDM assessments to ensure representativeness.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'The number of individuals, households, or units included in the assessment sample.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the assessment fieldwork began.']
  target_population varchar [note: 'Description of the population segment or beneficiary group targeted by this assessment (e.g., IDPs, host community, women-headed households, children under 5).']
  team_size varchar [note: 'Number of enumerators, facilitators, or team members who participated in conducting the assessment.']
  utilization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'For PDM assessments: percentage of beneficiaries who reported using the distributed items as intended. Key indicator of program effectiveness.']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the staff member who validated the assessment data and findings.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date when the assessment data was validated and approved by the MEL team or program management.']

  Note: 'Master record of a structured field assessment conducted to evaluate needs, context, or program outcomes. Covers all assessment types including FGD (Focus Group Discussion), KII (Key Informant Interview), household survey, rapid needs assessment, PDM (Post-Distribution Monitoring) with linked distribution event and utilization/adequacy scoring, market assessment, and MUAC screening. Captures assessment date, project site, methodology, target population, sample size, assessment team, KoboToolbox or CommCare form ID, data collection tool, key findings summary, recommendations, and assessment status (planned, in-progress, completed, validated). For PDM assessments: captures linked distribution event, sample methodology, utilization rate, adequacy score, beneficiary satisfaction, complaints received, and protection concerns noted. Links to assessment_response for individual submissions. Supports MEL indicator tracking, SitRep generation, CHS compliance, accountability to affected populations (AAP), and donor reporting on program quality.'
}

Table field.assessment_response {
  assessment_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the assessment response record. Primary key for individual survey or interview responses collected during field assessments.']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assessment form or questionnaire template used for this response. Links to the specific KoboToolbox or CommCare form definition.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.household. Business justification: Household-level assessments link responses to household units. Standard for food security assessments, household economy analysis, and vulnerability assessments. Enables household-level analysis of ne']
  primary_assessment_registrant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.registrant. Business justification: PDM and assessment surveys collect individual beneficiary responses. Required for beneficiary satisfaction tracking, accountability to affected populations (AAP), and individual-level feedback analysi']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project site or field location where the assessment was conducted. Links to geographic and operational site master data.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field staff member or community health worker who conducted the assessment and collected the response data.']
  children_under_5_count varchar [note: 'Number of children under 5 years of age in the household. Critical for nutrition, health, and protection program targeting.']
  chronic_illness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that one or more household members have a chronic illness requiring ongoing medical care. Informs health service delivery planning.']
  consent_data_sharing boolean [note: 'Indicator that the respondent has provided informed consent for their data to be shared with partner organizations for service delivery purposes.']
  consent_follow_up boolean [note: 'Indicator that the respondent has consented to be contacted for follow-up assessments or post-distribution monitoring activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this assessment response record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to collect the assessment response data. Distinguishes between face-to-face interviews, phone surveys, and other modalities.']
  debt_burden_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that the household is experiencing significant debt burden affecting their ability to meet basic needs. Used for economic vulnerability profiling.']
  disability_present_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that one or more household members have a disability. Used for inclusive programming and accessibility considerations.']
  displacement_duration_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the household has been displaced from their place of origin. Indicates protractedness of displacement and informs durable solutions programming.']
  displacement_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the respondent household displacement situation. Critical for targeting humanitarian assistance and understanding population movements.']
  education_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that school-age children in the household have access to education services. Monitors education in emergencies programming effectiveness.']
  elderly_over_60_count varchar [note: 'Number of elderly individuals over 60 years of age in the household. Used for vulnerability assessment and targeted assistance programs.']
  female_count varchar [note: 'Number of female individuals in the household. Enables sex-disaggregated analysis as required by humanitarian accountability standards.']
  food_security_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated food security index based on household consumption patterns and coping strategies. Higher scores indicate greater food insecurity.']
  gps_accuracy_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Accuracy radius of the GPS coordinates in meters. Indicates the precision and reliability of the location data captured during submission.']
  household_size varchar [note: 'Total number of individuals in the respondent household. Key demographic indicator for needs assessment and resource allocation calculations.']
  interview_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes taken to complete the assessment interview. Used for quality control and enumerator performance monitoring.']
  interview_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the assessment interview was conducted. Important for data quality assessment and ensuring linguistic accessibility.']
  livelihood_status varchar [note: 'Current employment and income generation status of the household. Critical for economic recovery and livelihoods programming.']
  male_count varchar [note: 'Number of male individuals in the household. Enables sex-disaggregated analysis as required by humanitarian accountability standards.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this assessment response record was last modified. Tracks data quality corrections and validation updates.']
  monthly_income_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total monthly household income in United States Dollars. Used for economic vulnerability assessment and cash transfer program targeting.']
  primary_need_category varchar [note: 'The most urgent need identified by the respondent household. Used for prioritization and resource allocation in humanitarian response. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food|water|shelter|health|protection|education|nfi|wash|livelihoods|psychosocial — promote to reference product]']
  protection_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that the household has reported protection concerns requiring specialized follow-up. Triggers referral to protection case management services.']
  protection_concern_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the protection concern reported. Highly sensitive field requiring strict confidentiality and ethical data handling protocols.']
  referral_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that the assessment identified needs requiring referral to specialized services such as protection, health, or case management.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment response indicating data quality validation state and approval workflow position.']
  shelter_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical condition and habitability of the household shelter. Used for prioritizing shelter repair and upgrade interventions.']
  shelter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the household current shelter arrangement. Critical for shelter and NFI (Non-Food Item) programming and vulnerability assessment.']
  submission_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate captured at the time and location of survey submission. Used for spatial analysis and verification of field coverage.']
  submission_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate captured at the time and location of survey submission. Used for spatial analysis and verification of field coverage.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assessment response was submitted by the enumerator. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from data validation and quality assurance review. Documents any data quality issues, corrections, or contextual information.']
  water_access_minutes varchar [note: 'Time in minutes required for household members to access safe drinking water. Key WASH (Water Sanitation and Hygiene) indicator for service delivery monitoring.']

  Note: 'Individual survey or interview response record collected during a field assessment, sourced from KoboToolbox or CommCare mobile data collection. Captures respondent anonymized ID, assessment form ID, submission timestamp, GPS coordinates of submission, enumerator ID, response status (submitted, validated, rejected), and key structured response fields (household size, displacement status, primary needs, protection concerns). Enables disaggregated analysis by sex, age, IDP status, and vulnerability category.'
}

  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the situation report (draft, under review, approved, submitted to OCHA/donors, published, archived).']
  report_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the situation report, typically including crisis name and reporting period.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this situation report is issued (daily, weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, ad-hoc for special events, flash for immediate crisis updates).']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this situation report.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this situation report.']
  resource_gaps_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative description of critical resource gaps, funding shortfalls, or supply needs identified during the reporting period.']
  security_incidents_count varchar [note: 'Number of security incidents affecting staff, operations, or beneficiaries during the reporting period.']
  shelter_units_provided varchar [note: 'Number of shelter units (tents, transitional shelters, housing units) provided during the reporting period.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the situation report was submitted to OCHA, donors, or coordination bodies.']
  total_beneficiaries_reached varchar [note: 'Total number of unique beneficiaries reached across all sectors during the reporting period.']
  wash_beneficiaries_reached varchar [note: 'Number of beneficiaries reached through WASH interventions during the reporting period.']

  Note: 'Situation Report (SitRep) record capturing operational status for a defined reporting period and geographic scope. Includes reporting period (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly), issuing country office, crisis/emergency context, key achievements by sector (beneficiaries reached, distributions completed, WASH coverage, health consultations), operational constraints and access issues, resource gaps, cluster coordination updates, and submission status to OCHA/donors. Supports HRP progress tracking, CERF/CBPF applications, donor narrative reporting, and inter-agency coordination. Generated from aggregated field transaction data.'
}

  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special considerations, or context about the team\'s deployment and activities.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the team. Active: currently deployed and operational; Standby: ready for deployment; Deployed: in field assignment; Resting: between deployments; Disbanded: team dissolved; Suspended: temporarily inactive.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance assessment rating for the team based on MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) indicators, service delivery quality, and beneficiary feedback.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-3 three-letter code for the primary language spoken by the team for beneficiary communication (e.g., ENG for English, ARA for Arabic, FRA for French).']
  safety_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Security risk level classification for the team\'s operational area, aligned with UN security phase system. Determines safety protocols and movement restrictions.']
  size varchar [note: 'Total number of staff and volunteers currently assigned to this team.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used for operational identification and reporting (e.g., \'DT-001\', \'MHU-03\', \'WASH-A\').']
  team_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the field team (e.g., \'Delta Distribution Team\', \'Mobile Health Unit 3\', \'WASH Response Team Alpha\').']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the team based on its primary operational function. Distribution teams handle NFI (Non-Food Item) distributions; CHW (Community Health Worker) teams provide mobile health outreach; WASH (Water Sanitation and Hygiene) teams deliver water and sanitation interventions; assessment teams conduct FGD (Focus Group Discussion) and KII (Key Informant Interview); protection teams address GBV (Gender-Based Violence) and child protection; rapid response teams handle emergency deployments; nutrition teams manage SAM (Severe Acute Malnutrition) and GAM (Global Acute Malnutrition) programs; education teams deliver learning programs; shelter teams provide housing assistance; logistics teams manage supply chain and transport. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: distribution|community_health_worker|wash|assessment|protection|rapid_response|nutrition|education|shelter|logistics — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of key training certifications held by team members (e.g., \'SPHERE\', \'CHS\', \'PSS (Psychosocial Support)\', \'GBV Response\', \'First Aid\').']

  Note: 'Master record for a field team or mobile unit deployed to deliver services at project sites. Captures team name, team type (distribution, CHW, WASH, assessment, protection, rapid response), assigned country office, team leader, team composition and size, operational status (active, standby, disbanded), deployment period, assigned vehicle/transport, communication equipment, and language capabilities. Represents the operational unit of deployment distinct from individual workforce records. Enables workload balancing, team performance tracking, and deployment planning.'
}
