  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Deployments are funded activities charged to specific funds. Finance tracks deployment costs against fund budgets for donor reporting and fund management.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program under which this deployment is conducted, linking the deployment to programmatic objectives and funding sources.']
  meal_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.meal_plan. Business justification: Field deployments are executed under program MEAL plans that specify what monitoring and data collection activities the deployed team must conduct. MEAL coordinators need to track which deployments ar']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a partner organization (CSO, CBO, INGO) collaborating on this deployment, if applicable.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: NGO surge/TDY deployments fill a specific authorized position at the receiving office. HR planning, deployment authorization workflows, and headcount management all require knowing which position a de']
  primary_field_deployed_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual staff member being deployed. Nullable if the deployment is for a team rather than an individual.']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination project site where the field team or staff member is being deployed.']
  accommodation_type varchar [note: 'Type of accommodation arranged for the deployed personnel during the mission.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date the deployment concluded, which may differ from the planned end date due to operational needs, early completion, or extension.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date the deployment commenced, which may differ from the planned start date due to logistical delays, security clearances, or travel disruptions.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the deployment was officially approved by the authorizing manager.']
  cluster_affiliation varchar [note: 'The OCHA humanitarian cluster or sector that this deployment supports, enabling coordination with inter-agency response mechanisms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WASH|Health|Nutrition|Shelter|Protection|Education|Food_Security|Logistics|Emergency_Telecommunications|Camp_Coordination — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of the deployment in the organization\'s functional currency, including travel, accommodation, per diem, and operational expenses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this deployment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the deployment cost estimate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system from which this deployment record originated (e.g., Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits, KoboToolbox, custom field operations system).']
  deployment_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique code for the deployment, typically formatted as country-office-prefix followed by sequential number (e.g., SYR-000123).']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deployment: planned (approved but not yet started), active (team is currently deployed), completed (deployment finished successfully), cancelled (deployment did not proceed), or suspended (temporarily paused).']
  deployment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deployment purpose: emergency response for rapid humanitarian crises, routine program delivery for ongoing operations, assessment mission for needs evaluation, training for capacity building, surge support for temporary reinforcement, or monitoring visit for MEL activities.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'The planned duration of the deployment in calendar days, calculated from start date to end date.']
  end_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date when the deployment concludes and the team or staff member returns or transitions.']
  gis_track_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GPS tracking and GIS data collection are enabled for this deployment to capture movement and location data for security and operational monitoring.']
  handover_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting key information, lessons learned, outstanding issues, and recommendations for follow-up recorded at the conclusion of the deployment.']
  insurance_coverage_type varchar [note: 'Level of insurance coverage provided for this deployment, reflecting the risk profile of the destination and activities.']
  medical_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether medical clearance or specific vaccinations are required for this deployment due to health risks at the destination.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this deployment record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes capturing additional context, special instructions, or operational considerations for this deployment.']
  purpose_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific objectives, activities, and expected outcomes of this deployment mission.']
  response_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the humanitarian context for this deployment: rapid onset emergency (sudden disaster), protracted crisis (long-term displacement), development program (routine service delivery), or recovery transition (post-crisis stabilization).']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'The minimum security clearance level required for personnel on this deployment, aligned with the destination site security rating and operational risk assessment.']
  sitrep_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the situation report or operational update that triggered or documented this deployment, enabling traceability to humanitarian response coordination.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the field team or staff member begins the deployment at the destination project site.']
  team_size varchar [note: 'Number of personnel in the deployed team. Set to 1 for individual staff deployments.']
  transportation_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used to reach the deployment site.']
  travel_authorization_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or code for the official travel authorization document approving this deployment, used for duty-of-care and insurance purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a field team or individual staff member being deployed to a specific project site for a defined operational period. Captures deployment start and end dates, deploying country office, destination project site, deployed field team or individual, deployment purpose (emergency response, routine program delivery, assessment mission, training, surge support), deployment status (planned, active, completed, cancelled), security clearance level required, travel authorization reference, and handover notes. Field domain owns the operational deployment record linking teams to sites for specific missions; workforce domain owns the underlying HR assignment, duty-of-care obligations, and R&R scheduling.'
}

Table field.access_constraint {
  access_constraint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the humanitarian access constraint record.']
  affected_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Access constraints (roadblocks, armed conflict, bureaucratic delays) directly impact shipment delivery. Linking access_constraint to affected shipment enables donor reporting on pipeline delays, insur']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Access constraints affecting partner-implemented activities must be documented against the governing partnership agreement for donor notification, force majeure clauses, and program timeline impact re']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Access constraints directly impact award implementation timelines and trigger donor notification obligations (donor_notification_required, donor_notification_date already on access_constraint). Linkin']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: access_constraint has country_code (STRING) but no FK to the country reference table. Access constraints are country-specific operational issues. Adding country_id FK normalizes the country reference ']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the country office responsible for managing and reporting the access constraint. Links to organizational structure for accountability and escalation.']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Access constraints are strongly correlated with emergency contexts — armed conflict, natural disasters, and disease outbreaks all generate specific access impediments. Linking access_constraint to eme']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Access constraints may involve compliance issues such as partner misconduct, bribery demands, regulatory violations, or sanctions concerns. Compliance team investigates access constraints for potentia']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the humanitarian program affected by the access constraint. Links to program-level impact analysis and donor reporting.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Access constraints often affect specific partner operations or are reported by partners. Tracking which partner is affected or reporting enables targeted mitigation planning, partner risk management, ']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the project site affected by the access constraint. Links to the field.project_site product for site-level analysis and reporting.']
  reporting_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Access constraints are reported and owned by specific staff members (security/access officers). Accountability frameworks, OCHA reporting, and incident follow-up all require identifying the responsibl']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.security_incident. Business justification: Access constraints are often caused by security incidents. The access_constraint table has a boolean flag \'security_incident_linked\' indicating when a constraint is related to a security incident. Add']
  admin_level_1 varchar [note: 'First-level administrative division (e.g., state, province, governorate) where the access constraint is located.']
  admin_level_2 varchar [note: 'Second-level administrative division (e.g., district, county) where the access constraint is located.']
  affected_activities_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the planned humanitarian activities that were delayed, cancelled, or modified due to the access constraint. May include distribution events, mobile health clinics, WASH interventions, or protection services.']
  affected_location_name varchar [note: 'Name of the geographic location, project site, or supply route affected by the access constraint. May reference a specific village, district, road segment, or border crossing.']
  affected_pcode varchar [note: 'OCHA-standard place code (P-Code) identifying the administrative area affected by the constraint. Enables standardized geographic aggregation and mapping across humanitarian datasets.']
  alternative_route_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an alternative access route or delivery mechanism is available to reach the affected beneficiaries.']
  alternative_route_description varchar [note: 'Description of the alternative route, delivery mechanism, or programming modality used to mitigate the access constraint (e.g., river transport, air drop, remote cash transfer, partner-led distribution).']
  armed_actor_involved varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the armed actor (state or non-state) responsible for or associated with the access constraint. Confidential due to security and negotiation sensitivity.']
  cluster_affiliation varchar [note: 'OCHA cluster or sector most affected by the access constraint (e.g., Health, WASH, Protection, Food Security, Shelter, Education). Supports cluster coordination and sector-specific reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health|wash|protection|food_security|shelter_nfi|education|nutrition|camp_coordination|early_recovery|logistics|emergency_telecommunications — promote to reference product]']
  constraint_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the access constraint, typically formatted as AC-{COUNTRY}-{SEQUENCE} for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  constraint_end_date date [note: 'Date when the access constraint was resolved or mitigated, allowing resumption of normal operations. Null if constraint remains active.']
  constraint_notes varchar [note: 'Additional contextual notes, observations, or lessons learned related to the access constraint. May include details on community dynamics, seasonal patterns, or coordination challenges.']
  constraint_start_date date [note: 'Date when the access constraint was first identified or reported. Marks the beginning of the impediment to humanitarian operations.']
  constraint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the access constraint. Active indicates ongoing impediment; monitoring indicates under observation; mitigated indicates workaround implemented; resolved indicates constraint removed; escalated indicates elevated to senior management or OCHA coordination.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the humanitarian access constraint affecting field operations. Active conflict includes armed hostilities; road blockage includes physical barriers; administrative denial includes government refusals; armed actor checkpoint includes non-state armed group controls; bureaucratic impediment includes visa/permit delays; weather/natural hazard includes floods, landslides, or extreme weather.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the access constraint record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system or data collection platform from which the access constraint record originated (e.g., KoboToolbox, OCHA Access Monitoring System, internal incident management system).']
  donor_notification_date date [note: 'Date when donors were formally notified of the access constraint and its impact on program delivery. Null if notification not required or not yet sent.']
  donor_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the access constraint requires formal notification to donors due to significant impact on grant deliverables, budget reallocation, or no-cost extension requests.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the access constraint was escalated to senior management or OCHA coordination. Null if not escalated.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether and to what level the access constraint has been escalated for senior management attention or inter-agency coordination. Used for tracking high-severity incidents requiring strategic intervention.']
  estimated_beneficiaries_unreached varchar [note: 'Estimated number of beneficiaries (Persons of Concern, PoC) who could not be reached or served due to this access constraint. Used for impact assessment and donor reporting.']
  impact_on_program_timeline varchar [note: 'Description of how the access constraint affected program implementation timelines, including delays in days/weeks and impact on grant deliverables and reporting milestones.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the access constraint location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the access constraint location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  mitigation_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Description of actions taken to mitigate or work around the access constraint, such as alternative routes, negotiation with armed actors, remote programming, or partner-led delivery.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the access constraint record was last modified. Audit trail for tracking updates to constraint status, mitigation actions, or resolution.']
  negotiation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether humanitarian access negotiation with armed actors, government authorities, or community leaders is required to resolve the constraint.']
  reporting_source varchar [note: 'Entity or system that reported the access constraint. OCHA (Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs), UNDSS (UN Department of Safety and Security), INSO (International NGO Safety Organisation), field staff, partner organization, cluster lead, or other source. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocha|undss|inso|field_staff|partner_org|cluster_lead|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  security_incident_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the access constraint is linked to a specific security incident (e.g., attack on humanitarian workers, kidnapping, armed confrontation).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the access constraint on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 represents minor delays and 5 represents complete access denial with life-threatening conditions. Used for prioritization and escalation decisions.']
  sitrep_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access constraint was included in an OCHA or organizational Situation Report (SitRep) for external stakeholder communication.']

  Note: 'Transactional record documenting a humanitarian access constraint affecting field operations at a specific location or along a supply route. Captures constraint type (active conflict, road blockage, administrative denial, armed actor checkpoint, bureaucratic impediment, weather/natural hazard), affected geographic area, start and resolution dates, severity level (1-5), estimated beneficiaries unreached, impact on planned activities, mitigation actions taken, reporting source (OCHA, UNDSS, INSO, field staff), and escalation status. Feeds access monitoring dashboards, donor reporting on operational challenges, and contingency planning.'
}

Table field.security_incident {
  security_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the security incident record. Primary key.']
  affected_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Security incidents (hijacking, looting, convoy attacks) compromise shipments. Linking security_incident to affected shipment supports insurance claims, UNDSS reporting, cargo loss documentation, donor']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Security incidents involving partner staff or assets must be reported under the incident reporting clauses of the governing partnership agreement. Donor notification obligations and timeline impact as']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Security incidents affecting award implementation must be reported to donors and may trigger grant amendments or force majeure clauses. Linking security_incident to award supports donor notification c']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: security_incident has country_code (STRING) but no FK to the country reference table. Security incidents are country-specific events requiring linkage to authoritative country data including security_']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the country office under whose jurisdiction the security incident occurred.']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Security incidents disproportionately occur during active emergency responses when operational tempo is high and access constraints are elevated. Linking security_incident to emergency enables emergen']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Security incidents may trigger compliance incidents when they involve fraud, misconduct, policy violations, or regulatory breaches. Compliance team investigates security incidents for potential ethica']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project under which the affected staff or activities were operating at the time of the security incident, if applicable.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Security incidents involving partner staff or assets must be tracked by partner organization for duty of care obligations, insurance claims, risk register updates, and partner capacity assessment. Cri']
  primary_security_focal_point_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the designated security focal point or security manager responsible for managing the incident response and investigation.']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific project site or field location where the security incident occurred, if applicable.']
  admin_level_1 varchar [note: 'First-level administrative division (e.g., state, province, region) where the incident occurred.']
  admin_level_2 varchar [note: 'Second-level administrative division (e.g., district, county) where the incident occurred.']
  affected_personnel_count varchar [note: 'Total number of NGO staff, volunteers, or contractors directly affected by the security incident (injured, detained, threatened, or otherwise impacted).']
  asset_damage_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of damage to organizational assets (vehicles, equipment, facilities, supplies) resulting from the security incident.']
  case_closed_date date [note: 'Date when the security incident case was formally closed after investigation and resolution (yyyy-MM-dd format).']
  corrective_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Description of corrective or preventive actions implemented following the security incident to mitigate future risk (e.g., enhanced security protocols, staff training, route changes).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the security incident record was first created in the database (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system or platform from which the security incident record was sourced (e.g., security management system, field data collection tool).']
  estimated_asset_loss_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of asset damage or loss in United States Dollars (USD) resulting from the security incident.']
  immediate_response_actions varchar [note: 'Description of immediate actions taken by field staff or security personnel in response to the security incident (e.g., evacuation, medical assistance, notification of authorities, lockdown).']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date when the security incident occurred in the field (yyyy-MM-dd format).']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the security incident, including sequence of events, circumstances, and context.']
  incident_location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or address of the specific location where the security incident occurred (e.g., village name, road name, facility name).']
  incident_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the security incident based on impact to personnel, assets, and operations. Critical = life-threatening or major asset loss; Major = serious injury or significant damage; Moderate = minor injury or moderate damage; Minor = no injury, minimal damage.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the security incident case in the investigation and resolution workflow.']
  incident_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the security incident occurred, in local time zone (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format).']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the security incident by nature of threat or event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: armed_robbery|carjacking|kidnapping|staff_detention|explosive_hazard|civil_unrest|armed_conflict|theft|burglary|assault|sexual_violence|harassment|vehicle_accident|fire|natural_disaster|health_emergency|other — promote to reference product]']
  inso_report_date date [note: 'Date when the security incident was reported to INSO, if applicable (yyyy-MM-dd format).']
  investigation_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings from the security incident investigation, including root causes, contributing factors, and lessons learned.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the internal or external investigation into the security incident.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the incident location in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum).']
  local_authorities_report_date date [note: 'Date when the security incident was reported to local authorities, if applicable (yyyy-MM-dd format).']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the incident location in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the security incident record was last modified or updated (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format).']
  pcode varchar [note: 'OCHA-standardized place code (P-Code) uniquely identifying the administrative location of the incident for humanitarian coordination.']
  police_case_number varchar [note: 'Official case or reference number assigned by local law enforcement authorities for the security incident, if reported.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the security incident was first reported to the organization\'s security management system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format).']
  reported_to_inso boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security incident was formally reported to the International NGO Safety Organisation (INSO) for coordination and information sharing among NGOs.']
  reported_to_local_authorities boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security incident was reported to local law enforcement or government authorities.']
  reported_to_undss boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security incident was formally reported to the United Nations Department of Safety and Security (UNDSS) for coordination and situational awareness.']
  sitrep_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security incident was included in a formal Situation Report (SitRep) distributed to stakeholders, donors, or coordination bodies.']
  staff_fatality_count varchar [note: 'Number of staff members who died as a result of the security incident.']
  staff_injured_count varchar [note: 'Number of staff members who sustained physical injuries as a result of the security incident.']
  undss_report_date date [note: 'Date when the security incident was reported to UNDSS, if applicable (yyyy-MM-dd format).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a security incident affecting NGO staff, assets, or operations in the field. Captures incident date, location, incident type (armed robbery, carjacking, staff detention, explosive hazard, civil unrest), affected personnel count, asset damage, incident severity (critical, major, minor), immediate response actions taken, reporting status to UNDSS or INSO, and case closure details. Supports duty-of-care obligations and security risk management.'
}

Table field.country {
  country_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for country']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Countries impose country-level compliance obligations on NGOs (annual NGO registration renewals, government reporting mandates). Linking country to its primary obligation enables compliance teams to t']
  parent_country_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on country (parent_country_id)']
  access_constraints varchar [note: 'Primary type of access constraints affecting humanitarian operations in the country. Used for operational planning and risk mitigation.']
  active_clusters varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of active humanitarian clusters in the country (e.g., WASH, Health, Nutrition, Shelter, Protection). Used for coordination and reporting alignment.']
  capital_city varchar [note: 'Name of the capital city of the country. Often the location of the country office headquarters.']
  cluster_coordination_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OCHA cluster coordination system is active in this country. Affects reporting obligations and inter-agency coordination requirements.']
  code_alpha2 varchar [note: 'Two-letter country code as defined by ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 standard. Used for international identification and data exchange.']
  code_alpha3 varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code as defined by ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 standard. Commonly used in humanitarian reporting and OCHA coordination.']
  code_numeric varchar [note: 'Three-digit numeric country code as defined by ISO 3166-1 numeric standard.']
  country_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the country as recognized by the United Nations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the countrys official currency. Used for financial reporting, budget planning, and cash transfer programming.']
  customs_procedures varchar [note: 'Summary of customs clearance procedures and requirements for importing humanitarian supplies. Critical for supply chain and logistics planning.']
  fragility_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the countrys fragility and conflict status. Critical for risk assessment, security planning, and humanitarian response prioritization.']
  gis_data_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether comprehensive GIS data layers (administrative boundaries, roads, facilities) are available for this country to support location-based analysis.']
  government_partnership_status varchar [note: 'Level of partnership and collaboration with the national government. Affects program implementation modalities and coordination mechanisms.']
  humanitarian_response_plan_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an active Humanitarian Response Plan coordinated by OCHA exists for this country. Used to determine cluster coordination requirements and reporting obligations.']
  income_classification varchar [note: 'World Bank income classification category for the country. Used for program eligibility, funding allocation, and needs assessment.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this country record is currently active in the system. Used for filtering and data quality management.']
  kobo_deployment_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether KoboToolbox mobile data collection is actively deployed for field assessments and monitoring in this country.']
  land_area_sq_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total land area of the country in square kilometers. Used for logistics planning, geographic information system (GIS) analysis, and distribution coverage calculations.']
  landlocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country is landlocked with no direct access to the sea. Critical for supply chain and logistics planning.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  least_developed_country boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country is classified as a Least Developed Country by the United Nations. Affects funding eligibility and program prioritization.']
  memorandum_of_understanding_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Memorandum of Understanding has been signed with the national government for humanitarian operations.']
  mou_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the current Memorandum of Understanding with the national government. Null if no MOU exists or if MOU is indefinite.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, context, or special considerations for this country. Used for knowledge management and operational guidance.']
  ocha_presence boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OCHA has an active presence in the country. Determines cluster coordination mechanisms and humanitarian reporting requirements.']
  office_established boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization has an established country office with permanent presence in this country.']
  office_establishment_date date [note: 'Date when the country office was officially established. Null if no country office exists.']
  official_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of official languages spoken in the country. Used for translation requirements, field staff recruitment, and beneficiary communication planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the country for field operations. Indicates whether the organization has active programs, suspended operations, or restricted access.']
  population varchar [note: 'Total population of the country based on the most recent census or UN estimate. Used for needs assessment and program scale planning.']
  population_year varchar [note: 'Year of the population estimate or census data. Indicates the currency of population data.']
  primary_donor_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of primary donor countries or institutions funding operations in this country. Used for donor reporting and relationship management.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region classification for the country. Used for regional program coordination and reporting aggregation.']
  security_risk_level varchar [note: 'Current security risk assessment level for the country. Determines staff travel restrictions, security protocols, and operational modalities.']
  small_island_developing_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country is classified as a Small Island Developing State. Relevant for climate adaptation and disaster risk reduction programs.']
  sub_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sub-region classification providing more granular regional grouping for operational planning and analysis.']
  tax_exemption_status varchar [note: 'Tax exemption status granted to the organization by the national government. Affects financial planning and procurement.']
  visa_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of visa requirements and procedures for international staff deployment to this country. Used for staff travel planning and compliance.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for country. Referenced by country_id.'
}

Table field.emergency {
  emergency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for emergency']
  coordinator_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Every NGO emergency response has a designated emergency coordinator or incident commander. Donor reports, flash appeals, and emergency response plans all identify this responsible staff member. No sta']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: emergency has primary_country_code (STRING) but no FK to the country reference table. The country table is the authoritative source for country data. Adding country_id FK normalizes the country refere']
  escalated_from_emergency_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on emergency (escalated_from_emergency_id)']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Emergencies are financed by dedicated funds (CERF, flash appeal, emergency reserve). Linking emergency to finance_fund enables tracking of fund utilization against emergency response — a core NGO fina']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Emergencies create new compliance obligations (mandatory OCHA reporting, donor flash reporting, host government notifications). Linking emergency to obligation captures which primary obligation was tr']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Emergencies designate a primary implementing partner organization for the response. Emergency response coordination, OCHA cluster reporting, and flash appeal documentation require identifying which pa']
  affected_population_count varchar [note: 'Estimated total number of individuals directly affected by the emergency event.']
  affected_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of administrative regions, provinces, or districts affected by the emergency.']
  cerf_allocation_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization received funding from the UN Central Emergency Response Fund for this emergency.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the emergency was officially closed and all operations ceased.']
  cluster_coordination_mechanism varchar [note: 'Description of the OCHA cluster coordination structure activated for this emergency (e.g., \'Full cluster activation with 11 sectors\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this emergency record was first created in the system.']
  declaration_date date [note: 'Date when the emergency was officially declared by the organization or governing authority.']
  disaster_category varchar [note: 'Specific disaster category providing granular classification of the emergency event type.']
  displaced_population_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of individuals displaced or forced to leave their homes due to the emergency.']
  emergency_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the emergency, following organizational coding standards (e.g., SYR-2023-EQKE01).']
  emergency_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the emergency context, impact, and response strategy.']
  emergency_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the emergency event (e.g., \'Syria Earthquake Response 2023\', \'Bangladesh Flood Relief\').']
  emergency_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the emergency response operation.']
  emergency_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the emergency by its primary cause or nature.']
  fgd_conducted_count varchar [note: 'Number of Focus Group Discussions conducted as part of field assessments for this emergency.']
  flash_appeal_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an OCHA Flash Appeal was issued for this emergency to mobilize rapid funding.']
  funding_received_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total funding received in US Dollars for the emergency response as of the last update.']
  funding_requirement_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total funding requirement in US Dollars for the organizations emergency response operations.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scale of the emergency impact and response operations.']
  gis_mapping_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GIS-based location mapping and spatial analysis were completed for affected areas.']
  hrp_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Humanitarian Response Plan was developed and issued for this emergency.']
  implementing_partners_count varchar [note: 'Number of local and international partner organizations collaborating in the emergency response.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emergency record is currently active and operational in the system.']
  kii_conducted_count varchar [note: 'Number of Key Informant Interviews conducted as part of field assessments for this emergency.']
  kobo_survey_deployed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mobile data collection surveys via KoboToolbox were deployed for field assessments in this emergency.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this emergency record was last updated in the system.']
  lead_clusters varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of humanitarian clusters where the organization holds lead or co-lead responsibility (e.g., \'WASH, Shelter, Protection\').']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Summary of key lessons learned and best practices documented during or after the emergency response.']
  mobile_health_outreach_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mobile health clinics or outreach services are planned as part of the health response.']
  nfi_distribution_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether distribution of non-food items (shelter materials, hygiene kits, household items) is planned.']
  onset_date date [note: 'Date when the emergency event first occurred or began impacting the affected population.']
  protection_concerns_identified varchar [note: 'Summary of key protection concerns identified during assessments (e.g., \'GBV risk, child protection, housing land property rights\').']
  rapid_assessment_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a rapid needs assessment was completed within the first 72 hours of the emergency.']
  response_end_date date [note: 'Date when the emergency response operations were concluded or transitioned to recovery phase.']
  response_modality varchar [note: 'Description of the primary response delivery mechanisms employed (e.g., \'In-kind distribution, Cash and Voucher Assistance (CVA), Service delivery\').']
  response_start_date date [note: 'Date when the organization initiated its emergency response operations.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'IASC system-wide emergency classification level indicating scale and required response capacity (Level 1: country-led, Level 2: UN coordination, Level 3: system-wide mobilization).']
  sitrep_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which situation reports are generated and disseminated for this emergency.']
  targeted_beneficiaries_count varchar [note: 'Number of beneficiaries the organization plans to reach through its emergency response interventions.']
  wash_interventions_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether WASH sector interventions are included in the emergency response plan.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for emergency. Referenced by emergency_id.'
}

Table mel.indicator {
  indicator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) indicator. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Indicators are agreement-specific reporting obligations. Business need: agreement compliance monitoring, donor reporting, performance-based payment triggers.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the grant or award that mandates or funds the tracking of this indicator. Null if the indicator is used across multiple grants or is organization-wide.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: MEL indicators are often defined to satisfy specific donor reporting obligations (e.g., USAID F indicators, ECHO visibility requirements). Linking indicator definitions to the compliance obligations t']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: In NGO results frameworks, indicators are assigned at the component level — each funded component has distinct indicators for donor reporting. indicator currently links only to intervention_id, making']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Indicators are often mandated by specific donor requirements beyond the obligation level (e.g., a donor requires specific WASH indicators as grant conditions). indicator already has compliance_obligat']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the program that owns or primarily uses this indicator. An indicator may be used across multiple programs but typically has a primary owning program.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: NGO MEAL systems assign indicators to specific org units (country offices, departments) for reporting accountability and data ownership. Indicator performance dashboards and donor reports require org ']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Indicators frequently track partner-specific performance metrics in sub-award agreements. Business need: partner accountability reporting, disaggregated results by implementing partner, performance-ba']
  responsible_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: NGO MEAL accountability requires a named staff member responsible for each indicator\'s data collection and reporting. The plain-text \'responsible_role\' is a denormalization of staff_member. Indicator ']
  responsible_volunteer_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to volunteer.role. Business justification: NGO MEL systems assign specific volunteer roles as responsible data collectors for each indicator (e.g., community health volunteer role for health indicators). This drives training compliance checks ']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The date when the baseline value was measured or established.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The initial measured value of the indicator at the start of the program or project, used as the reference point for measuring change and progress.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Formula or methodology used to calculate the indicator value. For ratio indicators, includes numerator and denominator definitions. For count indicators, specifies aggregation rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this indicator record was first created in the system.']
  dac_criteria_alignment varchar [note: 'The OECD DAC (Development Assistance Committee) evaluation criteria that this indicator addresses: relevance, coherence, effectiveness, efficiency, impact, or sustainability. Multiple criteria may be comma-separated.']
  data_collection_frequency varchar [note: 'How often data for this indicator is collected and reported (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annually, one-time baseline/endline). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi-annually|annually|one-time|event-based — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to collect data for this indicator (e.g., household survey, key informant interview, direct observation, facility records, mobile data collection). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: survey|interview|observation|administrative-records|focus-group|sensor|mobile-app|registry — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The primary source(s) from which data for this indicator is collected (e.g., DHIS2, KoboToolbox surveys, PDM (Post-Distribution Monitoring), FGD (Focus Group Discussion), KII (Key Informant Interview), administrative records, CommCare).']
  definition varchar [note: 'Detailed technical definition of what the indicator measures, including scope, boundaries, and any specific criteria or conditions that must be met.']
  denominator_description varchar [note: 'Definition of the denominator for percentage or ratio indicators. Describes the total population or base being measured. Null for count-based indicators.']
  dhis2_indicator_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this indicator in the DHIS2 (District Health Information System 2) platform, used for integration and data synchronization.']
  direction_of_change varchar [note: 'The desired direction of change for this indicator to represent positive program impact (increase for positive indicators like vaccination coverage, decrease for negative indicators like malnutrition rates).']
  disaggregation_dimensions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of dimensions by which this indicator should be disaggregated for analysis (e.g., age, gender, location, disability status, IDP/refugee status). Critical for equity and inclusion analysis.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this indicator definition is no longer active. Null for currently active indicators.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this indicator definition became active and applicable for data collection and reporting.']
  indicator_category varchar [note: 'Measurement category of the indicator: quantitative (numeric), qualitative (descriptive), or composite (combination of multiple measures).']
  indicator_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the indicator used across programs and grants. Often follows organizational or donor-specific coding conventions.']
  indicator_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the indicator as it appears in LogFrames (Logical Frameworks), donor reports, and MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) dashboards.']
  indicator_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the indicator in the MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) system.']
  indicator_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the indicator within the Results-Based Management (RBM) results chain: input (resources), process (activities), output (deliverables), outcome (short-term change), or impact (long-term change).']
  is_custom boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a custom indicator developed specifically for this organization or program (true) or a standard indicator from an external framework (false).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this indicator is mandatory for reporting to donors or governing bodies (true) or optional/supplementary (false).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this indicator record was last updated or modified.']
  logframe_level varchar [note: 'The level in the LogFrame (Logical Framework) hierarchy where this indicator is positioned: goal (impact), purpose (outcome), output (deliverable), or activity (process).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, guidance, or context for data collectors and analysts regarding this indicator, including any special considerations, limitations, or interpretation guidance.']
  numerator_description varchar [note: 'Definition of the numerator for percentage or ratio indicators. Describes what is being counted in the top of the fraction. Null for count-based indicators.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this indicator must be reported to donors, management, or external stakeholders. May differ from collection frequency.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'The specific SDG (Sustainable Development Goal) goal and target numbers that this indicator contributes to (e.g., SDG 2.1, SDG 3.2). Multiple goals may be comma-separated.']
  sector varchar [note: 'The humanitarian or development sector this indicator belongs to (e.g., WASH (Water Sanitation and Hygiene), Health, Nutrition, Education, Protection, Shelter, Food Security, Livelihoods).']
  target_date date [note: 'The date by which the target value is expected to be achieved.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned or target value that the program aims to achieve for this indicator by the end of the reporting period or project lifecycle.']
  theme varchar [note: 'Cross-cutting theme or focus area this indicator addresses (e.g., Gender Equality, Child Protection, Climate Adaptation, Disability Inclusion, GBV (Gender-Based Violence) Prevention).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the indicator is measured (e.g., number of people, percentage, households, liters, kilograms, days, sessions).']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the accuracy and reliability of the indicator data (e.g., spot checks, third-party evaluation, beneficiary feedback, document review, field monitoring visits).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this indicator definition, used to track changes to calculation methods, definitions, or disaggregation requirements over time.']

  Note: 'Master library of all MEL indicators used across programs and grants. Each record defines one measurable indicator with its definition, calculation method, numerator/denominator (for percentage indicators), unit of measure, data collection frequency, disaggregation dimensions, baseline value, and alignment to SDG goals, DAC criteria, and LogFrame output/outcome levels. Serves as the single source of truth for indicator metadata referenced by targets, results, DHIS2 reporting, and donor performance frameworks.'
}

Table mel.logframe {
  logframe_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the logical framework record. Primary key for the MEL LogFrame entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Logframes are core agreement annexes defining results frameworks. Business need: agreement results tracking, donor reporting compliance, partnership performance monitoring.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Donor logframes are governed by specific compliance obligations (IATI publication, CHS commitments, statutory reporting). Linking logframe to the obligation that mandates its structure enables complia']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: NGO logframes are structured around components — each component (output, outcome workstream) has its own logframe section. mel_logframe links to intervention_id but not component_id, preventing compon']
  consortium_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.consortium. Business justification: Consortium programs operate under a shared logframe that governs all member organizations\' results. NGOs develop consortium-level logframes for joint donor reporting and collective results measurement']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Logframe hierarchies (impact/outcome/output levels) are directly dictated by specific donor requirements. Linking logframe to the donor_requirement that mandated its structure enables traceability fro']
  grant_award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or award that this logical framework supports. Links the LogFrame to the funding source and donor requirements.']
  grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: Logframes are the programmatic framework for a grant and must align with the grant budget structure for results-based management. Linking logframe to grant_budget enables budget-to-results framework a']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project that this logical framework defines. Links the LogFrame to operational program delivery.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: NGO logframes are owned and managed by specific org units (country offices, program departments). Logframe approval workflows, reporting hierarchies, and donor submissions require org unit attribution']
  parent_logframe_mel_logframe_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing foreign key to the parent LogFrame entry in the hierarchy. Enables nested results chains where outputs roll up to outcomes, outcomes to goals.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Logframes track partner-implemented results in sub-award/consortium contexts. Business need: partner performance reporting against logframe indicators, disaggregated results tracking by implementing p']
  responsible_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: NGO logframe management assigns accountability to positions (not just individuals) for continuity during staff turnover. The plain-text \'responsible_party\' denormalizes position data. Logframe account']
  responsible_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Logframes track responsible_party as text but MEL accountability frameworks and donor reporting (USAID, FCDO) require linking to actual staff member for performance management and results framework ow']
  responsible_volunteer_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to volunteer.role. Business justification: Logframe result levels in community programs assign volunteer roles as responsible parties (e.g., community health volunteer role responsible for output-level results). mel_logframe.responsible_party ']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent actual measurement. Used to track reporting currency and identify data staleness.']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Most recent measured or reported value for the indicator. Updated through Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL) data collection activities such as Post-Distribution Monitoring (PDM), Focus Group Discussions (FGD), or District Health Information System 2 (DHIS2) reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or committee that approved this LogFrame. Critical for governance and donor compliance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this LogFrame was formally approved. Establishes the baseline for performance tracking and donor reporting.']
  assumptions varchar [note: 'External conditions or factors that must hold true for the intervention logic to succeed. Critical for risk management and Theory of Change (ToC) validation.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Date when the baseline measurement was taken. Critical for establishing the temporal reference point for all subsequent measurements.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Measured value of the indicator at the start of the program or intervention. Provides the starting point for measuring change and impact.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this LogFrame record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance.']
  dac_evaluation_criterion varchar [note: 'Development Assistance Committee (DAC) evaluation criterion that this LogFrame element addresses. Used to align program design with international evaluation standards.']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to collect data for this indicator. Examples include household surveys, Key Informant Interviews (KII), Focus Group Discussions (FGD), Post-Distribution Monitoring (PDM), or District Health Information System 2 (DHIS2) aggregate reporting.']
  disaggregation_dimensions varchar [note: 'Dimensions by which the indicator should be disaggregated for equity and inclusion analysis. Common dimensions include gender, age group, disability status, geographic location, and vulnerability category.']
  donor_template_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the donor-specific logical framework template or format used. Different donors require different LogFrame structures and terminology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DFID|FCDO|USAID|EU|ECHO|UN|World Bank|Generic|Other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this LogFrame version was superseded or the program ended. Null for currently active LogFrames.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this LogFrame version became effective. Used to track LogFrame amendments and revisions over the program lifecycle.']
  hierarchy_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering within the results chain hierarchy. Used to maintain the logical sequence of goals, outcomes, outputs, and activities.']
  intervention_statement varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the intervention logic at this level of the results chain. Describes what the program intends to achieve or deliver.']
  is_custom_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a custom indicator defined by the implementing organization rather than a standard donor or sector indicator.']
  is_mandatory_donor_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this indicator is a mandatory reporting requirement from the donor. Mandatory indicators cannot be modified without prior approval.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this LogFrame record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes and amendments.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review or evaluation of this LogFrame element. Used to track adaptive management cycles and mid-term reviews.']
  logframe_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference code for the logical framework, often aligned with donor proposal numbering or internal program codes.']
  logframe_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the logical framework, typically reflecting the program or intervention name.']
  means_of_verification varchar [note: 'Data source or method by which the indicator will be measured and verified. Examples include surveys, administrative records, Post-Distribution Monitoring (PDM), or District Health Information System 2 (DHIS2) reports.']
  mel_logframe_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this LogFrame element in its lifecycle. Tracks whether targets are being met and whether the intervention logic remains valid. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Active|On Track|At Risk|Behind Schedule|Achieved|Closed|Cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review or evaluation of this LogFrame element. Drives Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL) planning and resource allocation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, lessons learned, or adaptive management decisions related to this LogFrame element.']
  objectively_verifiable_indicator varchar [note: 'The measurable indicator that will be used to verify achievement of this results chain level. Links to the indicator library for tracking.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this indicator must be measured and reported to donors. Drives Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL) data collection schedules.']
  results_chain_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level within the results chain that this LogFrame row represents. Defines whether this is a goal, outcome, output, or activity level entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Goal|Impact|Purpose|Outcome|Output|Activity|Input — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risks varchar [note: 'Identified risks or threats that could prevent achievement of this results chain level. Used for risk mitigation planning and adaptive management.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) and target that this LogFrame element contributes to. Enables SDG reporting and impact mapping.']
  target_date date [note: 'Date by which the target value is expected to be achieved. Aligns with program end date or milestone dates.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned or target value for the indicator at the end of the program period. Used for performance tracking and variance analysis.']
  theory_of_change_component varchar [note: 'Identifies which component of the Theory of Change (ToC) this LogFrame row represents. Links the LogFrame to the broader causal pathway and intervention logic.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the indicator is measured. Examples include count, percentage, ratio, households, beneficiaries, or Mid-Upper Arm Circumference (MUAC) measurements.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the logical framework. LogFrames are often revised during program amendments or mid-term reviews.']

  Note: 'Logical Framework (LogFrame) master records defining the results chain hierarchy for each program or grant. Each logframe captures the full intervention logic: goal (impact), purpose (outcome), outputs, activities, assumptions, risks, and objectively verifiable indicators (OVIs) at each level. Includes Theory of Change (ToC) components as embedded rows within the hierarchy. Aligns with DAC evaluation criteria, RBM standards, and donor-specific logframe templates (DFID/FCDO, USAID, EU, ECHO). Links to indicator targets for performance tracking and supports results framework reporting.'
}

Table mel.indicator_target {
  indicator_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the indicator target record. Primary key.']

  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or award funding this indicator target.']
  distribution_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_plan. Business justification: NGO indicator targets for distribution-related indicators (e.g., target beneficiaries to receive NFIs) are set against specific distribution plans. This link supports \'target-setting against distribut']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Specific indicator targets are often set to satisfy explicit donor compliance requirements (e.g., contractual deliverables, earmark requirements, congressional directives). This link enables tracking ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Indicator targets are time-bound to fiscal periods for integrated financial and programmatic donor reporting. Finance teams reconcile indicator targets against fiscal period closings; this link is req']
  grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: Indicator targets must be reconciled against grant budgets during budget realignment and donor reporting. NGO MEL officers cross-reference programmatic targets with funded grant budgets to ensure targ']
  indicator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the indicator definition in the MEL indicator library for which this target is set.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project under which this indicator target is tracked.']
  logframe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.mel_logframe. Business justification: The indicator_target product description explicitly states it defines \'Planned targets for each indicator within a specific logframe.\' Every target belongs to a specific LogFrame results chain. Withou']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit, team, or country office responsible for achieving this target.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Targets are set at partner level in sub-award agreements as performance benchmarks. Business need: partner-specific target setting, milestone tracking, performance-based disbursement triggers.']
  primary_indicator_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or MEL officer accountable for tracking and reporting on this target.']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific reporting period (quarterly, annual, etc.) for which this target applies.']
  responsible_volunteer_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to volunteer.role. Business justification: Indicator targets in community health and development programs assign specific volunteer roles as accountable for achieving them. indicator_target has primary_indicator_staff_member_id for staff accou']
  target_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.target_population. Business justification: NGO indicator targets are set per population group (e.g., 500 female refugees, 300 male IDPs). indicator_target has disaggregation fields but no FK to target_population, preventing population-specific']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this indicator target was formally approved by the responsible authority or donor.']
  assumptions varchar [note: 'Key assumptions underlying the achievement of this target, including external factors and preconditions.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The date when the baseline value was measured or established.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The baseline measurement value at the start of the program or reporting period, used as the starting point for target setting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this indicator target record was first created in the system.']
  dac_sector_code varchar [note: 'The OECD DAC sector classification code for this target, used for international aid transparency and reporting.']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'The primary method used to collect data for this target (e.g., household survey, FGD, KII, PDM, administrative records, DHIS2 aggregate).']
  disaggregation_age_group varchar [note: 'Age group category for this target (e.g., 0-5, 6-17, 18-59, 60+). Supports age-sensitive programming and reporting.']
  disaggregation_disability_status varchar [note: 'Disability status disaggregation for this target, supporting inclusive programming and Washington Group standards.']
  disaggregation_displacement_status varchar [note: 'Displacement or migration status disaggregation (e.g., IDP, refugee, returnee, host community, Person of Concern).']
  disaggregation_sex varchar [note: 'Sex-based disaggregation category for this target (male, female, other, not specified). Used for gender-sensitive monitoring.']
  donor_reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific donor reporting requirement or framework that this target must satisfy (e.g., USAID F indicator, DFID logframe output 2.1).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this indicator target record was last updated or modified.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this indicator target is measured and reported (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, annually, one-time, continuous). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annually|annually|one_time|continuous — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mitigation_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned strategies or actions to mitigate identified risks and ensure target achievement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or contextual information about this indicator target.']
  revision_date date [note: 'The date when the target was last revised or amended.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any revision to the original target value or date, including contextual changes or donor amendments.']
  risks varchar [note: 'Identified risks that may prevent the achievement of this target, including contextual, operational, and external risks.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'The SDG goal and target number(s) that this indicator target contributes to (e.g., SDG 1.1, SDG 3.2).']
  target_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for this indicator target, often used in donor reports and LogFrame documentation.']
  target_date date [note: 'The date by which the target value is expected to be achieved.']
  target_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of what this target represents, including context and rationale.']
  target_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for this indicator target.']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the indicator target: draft (being defined), approved (validated by stakeholders), active (currently being tracked), on_track (progressing as planned), at_risk (may not be achieved), off_track (unlikely to be achieved), achieved (target met), not_achieved (target not met), revised (target has been modified), cancelled (target no longer applicable). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|active|on_track|at_risk|off_track|achieved|not_achieved|revised|cancelled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the target based on the results chain level: output (direct deliverables), outcome (intermediate results), impact (long-term change), process (operational efficiency), or input (resources).']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned or target value to be achieved for this indicator within the specified period and geographic scope.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the target and baseline values are measured (e.g., number of beneficiaries, percentage, households, liters).']
  verification_source varchar [note: 'The source or means of verification for this target (e.g., program records, beneficiary surveys, third-party evaluation, DHIS2 reports).']

  Note: 'Planned targets for each indicator within a specific logframe, grant reporting period, and geographic scope. Stores baseline, target value, target date, disaggregation breakdowns (sex, age, location), and the responsible program unit. Supports BvA-style target vs. actual tracking and donor performance reporting.'
}
