  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse is certified to store hazardous materials according to international standards.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety, security, or compliance inspection of the warehouse facility.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the warehouse facility in decimal degrees for GIS mapping and logistics planning.']
  loading_docks_count varchar [note: 'Number of loading docks available at the warehouse for receiving and dispatching cargo.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the warehouse facility in decimal degrees for GIS mapping and logistics planning.']
  managing_entity varchar [note: 'Type of entity responsible for the day-to-day management and operation of the warehouse facility.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warehouse record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special considerations regarding the warehouse facility and its operations.']
  operational_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours of the warehouse facility including days and times of operation.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the warehouse facility in its lifecycle.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership or usage arrangement for the warehouse facility.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the warehouse location.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification level of the warehouse based on threat assessment and protection measures in place.']
  storage_capacity_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total storage capacity of the warehouse measured in cubic meters (m³).']
  storage_capacity_pallets varchar [note: 'Total storage capacity of the warehouse measured in standard pallet positions.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse has temperature control capability for cold-chain commodities.']
  temperature_range_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature that can be maintained in the warehouse for cold-chain storage, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_range_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature that can be maintained in the warehouse for cold-chain storage, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the warehouse facility used in logistics documentation and tracking systems.']
  warehouse_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the warehouse facility as recognized by the organization and partners.']
  wms_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the warehouse management system software used to track inventory and operations at this facility.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical storage facilities in the humanitarian supply network including central warehouses, field warehouses, transit hubs, pre-positioning depots, and cold-chain facilities. Captures location (GIS coordinates, country, admin level), storage capacity (m³, pallets), facility type, operational status, managing entity, temperature control capability, and OCHA cluster affiliation. Supports last-mile logistics planning and inventory positioning.'
}

Table supply.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor record. Primary key for the vendor entity.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Many vendors are partner organizations (local NGOs, CBOs). Critical for due diligence integration—vendor screening must reference partnership capacity assessments, risk registers, and accreditation re']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: OFAC/UN sanctions screening is mandatory pre-award requirement for all vendors in nonprofit sector. Real business process: vendor vetting before contract execution to ensure donor compliance.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the vendor\'s primary business address, typically containing street number and street name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the vendor\'s primary business address, typically containing building, suite, or unit information.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s primary bank account number for payment processing and fund transfers.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution where the vendor\'s primary bank account is held.']
  bank_swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code of the vendor\'s bank, used for international wire transfers and cross-border payments.']
  blacklist_effective_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor blacklist or debarment status became effective.']
  blacklist_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor blacklist or debarment status expires, after which the vendor may be eligible for reinstatement (nullable for permanent debarments).']
  blacklist_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) showing whether the vendor is currently blacklisted or debarred from humanitarian procurement due to fraud, corruption, poor performance, or ethical violations.']
  blacklist_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the vendor was blacklisted or debarred, including the nature of the violation and the decision authority.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the vendor\'s primary business address is located.']
  country_of_operation varchar [note: 'Primary country where the vendor operates or is registered, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, GBR, KEN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vendor record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Preferred currency for transactions with the vendor, using ISO 4217 three-letter currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles or transport units in the vendor\'s fleet (applicable for transport and logistics providers). Indicates capacity for humanitarian logistics operations.']
  gmp_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) showing whether the vendor holds Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certification, required for pharmaceutical and medical supply vendors.']
  humanitarian_network_membership varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of humanitarian logistics networks or consortia the vendor is a member of, such as WFP Logistics Hub Framework (LHF), UNHRD (UN Humanitarian Response Depot), or Logistics Cluster.']
  iso_certification varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO certifications held by the vendor, such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), ISO 14001 (Environmental Management), or ISO 45001 (Occupational Health and Safety).']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor\'s performance was last formally reviewed and assessed by the procurement or supply chain team.']
  last_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The most recent performance score assigned to the vendor during the last performance review, typically on a scale of 0-100 or 0-5, measuring quality, delivery, compliance, and responsiveness.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vendor record was last modified or updated in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms offered by the vendor, expressed as the number of days from invoice date to payment due date (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days).']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier based on the vendor\'s historical performance, quality, delivery reliability, and compliance with humanitarian standards. Tier 1 represents preferred vendors with excellent track records.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the vendor\'s primary business address.']
  prequalification_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor successfully completed the prequalification process and was approved for humanitarian procurement.']
  prequalification_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor\'s prequalification status expires and requires renewal or reassessment.']
  prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has completed and passed the organization\'s prequalification process for humanitarian procurement, ensuring compliance with quality, ethical, and operational standards.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the vendor contact person used for procurement communications, purchase orders, and supply chain coordination.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the vendor organization for procurement and supply chain coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the vendor contact person, including country code, used for urgent procurement and logistics coordination.']
  registration_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor was first registered in the organization\'s vendor management system.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the vendor\'s primary business address is located.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s tax identification number (TIN), VAT number, or equivalent tax registration identifier issued by the country of operation.']
  transport_modes_offered varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of transportation modes offered by the vendor (applicable for logistics and transport providers), such as air freight, sea freight, road transport, rail transport, or last-mile delivery.']
  un_vendor_number varchar [note: 'The unique vendor registration number assigned by the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM) for vendors participating in UN procurement.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Internal unique alphanumeric code assigned to the vendor for procurement and supply chain operations.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the vendor, supplier, or service provider as registered with governing authorities.']
  vendor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor in the procurement system, indicating whether the vendor is eligible for new contracts and purchase orders.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor based on the primary service or product category they provide in the humanitarian supply chain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commodity_supplier|manufacturer|freight_forwarder|clearing_agent|trucking_company|air_cargo_operator|last_mile_delivery_partner|warehouse_operator|service_provider|customs_broker|cold_chain_provider — promote to reference product]']
  warehouse_capacity_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total warehouse storage capacity in square meters available from the vendor (applicable for warehouse operators and logistics providers).']

  Note: 'Master record for all suppliers, vendors, service providers, and logistics operators engaged in humanitarian procurement and supply chain operations. Covers commodity suppliers, international manufacturers, freight forwarders, clearing agents, trucking companies, air cargo operators, and last-mile delivery partners. Captures vendor registration details, country of operation, commodity categories supplied, transport modes offered (where applicable), pre-qualification status, performance tier, fleet size (for transport providers), blacklist/debarment flags, UN vendor registration number, compliance certifications (e.g., ISO, GMP), and humanitarian logistics network membership (e.g., WFP LHF, UNHRD). SSOT for all supplier and service provider identity within the supply domain.'
}

Table supply.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the purchase order record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: NGO procurement officers must trace each PO to the governing partnership agreement for donor compliance audits and sub-award financial accountability reporting. A nonprofit supply chain expert would e']
  approved_by_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: NGO procurement compliance (USAID, ECHO, UN rules) requires a named approving officer on every PO for donor audits and segregation-of-duties controls. The PO tracks approval_workflow_status and approv']
  award_budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award_budget_line. Business justification: POs must be coded to a specific donor award budget line for expenditure tracking and donor financial reporting. NGO finance teams post PO commitments against award_budget_line to monitor burn rates an']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor grant or funding source that finances this purchase order. Critical for donor-restricted fund compliance and Budget versus Actual (BvA) tracking per 2 CFR 200.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: Component-level procurement expenditure tracking: POs must be attributed to program components for donor budget compliance. Grants with multiple components require direct PO-to-component linkage for f']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Purchase orders must be charged to cost centers for expense tracking, budget control, and financial reporting. Essential for nonprofit expense allocation and grant compliance.']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the country office, field office, or headquarters unit that issued this purchase order. Used for budget tracking and procurement authority validation.']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donor procurement requirements (competitive bidding thresholds, sole-source rules, vendor eligibility) govern how each PO is structured and awarded. Compliance officers auditing procurement must trace']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Emergency procurement (no-bid contracts, fast-track POs) must reference the triggering emergency for donor compliance, audit trails, and HRP financial tracking. purchase_order has emergency_flag (plai']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: NGO procurement requires POs to be charged against specific finance funds (donor-restricted or unrestricted) for fund balance management and donor financial reporting. Existing FKs cover cost_center a']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Purchase orders require GL account coding for proper financial classification, accrual accounting, and financial statement preparation. Core requirement for nonprofit financial management.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the humanitarian program or project that requested this procurement. Links PO to program budget and donor restrictions.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: NGOs routinely procure from local partner organizations to support localization commitments and capacity building. Links PO to partnership agreements, due diligence records, and performance reviews. E']
  procurement_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.procurement_request. Business justification: A purchase order is the fulfillment of a procurement request — the PR is the parent document that authorizes procurement, and the PO is the resulting transactional document issued to a vendor. Adding ']
  requesting_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: NGO internal charge-back, budget accountability, and donor reporting require POs to be attributed to the requesting org unit directly. While procurement_request links to org_unit, the PO itself needs ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor organization receiving this purchase order. Links to vendor master data for payment and performance tracking.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse, distribution center, or project site where commodities should be delivered. Used for logistics planning and goods receipt verification.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when goods were actually received and verified. Populated upon goods receipt and used for vendor on-time delivery KPI tracking.']
  approval_workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the purchase order in the multi-level approval workflow. Tracks progression through procurement authority levels per delegation of authority matrix.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the purchase order received final approval. Used for procurement cycle time measurement and compliance audit trail.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of commodities on this purchase order: NFI (Non-Food Items), medical supplies, food, WASH (Water Sanitation and Hygiene), shelter materials, education supplies, protection items, or logistics equipment. Used for sector-specific reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nfi|medical|food|wash|shelter|education|protection|logistics — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and procurement cycle time analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this purchase order (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency procurement and exchange rate tracking.']
  delivery_address varchar [note: 'Full physical delivery address for commodity shipment. Captured for logistics coordination and proof of delivery. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  donor_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order should be visible in donor reporting and transparency portals (e.g., IATI - International Aid Transparency Initiative). Used for public accountability and donor stewardship.']
  emergency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order was issued under emergency procurement procedures due to humanitarian crisis or disaster response. Allows expedited approval and relaxed competitive bidding requirements per CERF (Central Emergency Response Fund) guidelines.']
  erp_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document ID from the source ERP system (SAP S/4HANA, Unit4, etc.). Used for system integration, reconciliation, and audit trail back to operational system of record.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Vendor-committed delivery date as agreed in the purchase order. Used for goods receipt scheduling and vendor performance measurement.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight, shipping, and logistics charges for delivery of commodities. Critical for last-mile logistics cost tracking.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate status of goods receipt across all purchase order line items. Used for three-way matching (PO-GRN-Invoice) and inventory management.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Delivery terms defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller per Incoterms 2020 (e.g., DDP = Delivered Duty Paid, FOB = Free On Board). Critical for humanitarian supply chain cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_matching_status varchar [note: 'Status of three-way matching between purchase order, goods receipt note, and vendor invoice. Critical for accounts payable processing and financial control per 2 CFR 200.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and special instructions for the vendor, logistics team, or procurement staff. May include delivery instructions, quality requirements, or donor-specific compliance notes.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which vendor will be paid (bank transfer, wire, check, letter of credit, mobile money, cash). Used for treasury planning and compliance with donor payment restrictions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms with vendor (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 50% advance / 50% on delivery). Governs accounts payable scheduling and cash flow management.']
  po_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was officially issued to the vendor. Principal business event timestamp for procurement tracking and lead time calculation.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique purchase order document number issued to vendor. Business identifier used in procurement communications and three-way matching (PO-GRN-Invoice).']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order in the procurement workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval, issuance, goods receipt, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|issued|partially_received|fully_received|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order type: standard (one-time procurement), blanket (recurring deliveries), contract (long-term agreement), emergency (urgent humanitarian response), framework (pre-negotiated terms).']
  procurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to select the vendor: competitive bidding, request for quotation (RFQ), sole source justification, framework agreement, or emergency procurement waiver. Required for donor compliance and audit trail per 2 CFR 200.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the requesting program needs the commodities delivered. Used for procurement lead time planning and emergency response timeline tracking.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all line item amounts before taxes, duties, and freight charges. Base procurement value for budget tracking.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) applied to this purchase order. May be zero for tax-exempt humanitarian procurement.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Grand total value of the purchase order including subtotal, taxes, freight, and all other charges. Used for budget commitment and three-way matching.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all procurement purchase orders issued to vendors for humanitarian commodities and services, including embedded line-item detail (commodity, quantity, unit price, delivery schedule per line). Captures PO number, issuing office, vendor, line items with per-line commodity reference, ordered quantity, unit of measure, agreed unit cost, delivery location, and requested delivery date. Also records total PO value, currency, funding source (grant/donor), delivery terms (Incoterms), expected delivery date, approval workflow status, goods receipt status per line, invoice matching status, and ERP document reference. Supports BvA tracking, three-way matching (PO–GRN–Invoice), and donor-restricted fund compliance per 2 CFR 200 Uniform Guidance.'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the goods receipt transaction. Primary key for this entity.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or funding source that is financing this procurement. Used for fund accounting and donor reporting.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Goods receipts confirm actual commodity delivery and trigger budget line expenditure recognition in NGO commitment accounting. Budget line-level actual vs. planned tracking is a core donor financial r']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the commodity or Non-Food Item (NFI) being received. Links to the master commodity catalog.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: Component-level in-kind and commodity receipt attribution: donor financial reports require goods receipts attributed to specific components for in-kind donation valuation and budget utilization tracki']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Goods receipts trigger expense recognition that must be allocated to cost centers for budget consumption tracking and program expense reporting. Essential for nonprofit financial control.']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donor requirements specify inspection standards, quality certifications, and acceptance criteria for received goods (e.g., USAID quality assurance requirements). Linking goods_receipt to donor_require']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Goods received under emergency response must be traceable to the emergency for CERF accountability, donor reporting, and post-emergency asset registration. goods_receipt has no emergency FK. Nonprofit']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Goods receipts must be charged to funds for donor reporting and fund accounting. Critical for nonprofit grant compliance and restricted fund tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Goods receipts require GL account assignment for inventory/expense posting and financial statement preparation. Core requirement for nonprofit inventory and expense accounting.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the humanitarian program or project for which these goods are intended. Links supply chain to program delivery.']
  needs_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.needs_assessment. Business justification: Goods receipts fulfill commodities identified in needs assessments. Closes the loop from assessment to procurement to delivery. Essential for needs-based accountability, procurement justification audi']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Goods often received by partner organizations at field locations. Establishes custody accountability, validates against partnership agreement authorized locations, supports audit trails for partner-ma']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field project site if goods were received directly at a program location rather than a central warehouse.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which goods are being received. Links this receipt to the originating procurement document.']
  purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order_line. Business justification: Goods receipts are processed against specific purchase order lines (line-level receipt matching), not just the header PO. Adding purchase_order_line_id enables three-way matching at the line level (PO']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound shipment or logistics movement that delivered these goods.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who physically received and inspected the goods. Responsible for verifying quantity and condition.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor who delivered the goods. Used for three-way matching and vendor performance tracking.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or field storage location where goods were physically received.']
  waybill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.waybill. Business justification: Goods receipts are recorded when commodities arrive at a warehouse. The waybill is the shipping document that accompanies the delivery. The goods_receipt table has delivery_note_number (STRING) which ']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer batch or lot number for the received commodity, critical for traceability and recall management, especially for medical supplies and food items.']
  condition_on_arrival varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical condition of the goods upon receipt. Good indicates acceptable condition, damaged indicates physical damage, expired indicates past expiry date, partial_damage indicates some units damaged, quality_issue indicates substandard quality.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this goods receipt record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customs_clearance_date date [note: 'Date on which the goods cleared customs and were released for delivery to the warehouse or field location.']
  customs_cleared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goods have cleared customs and import formalities. True if cleared, False if pending customs clearance.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether there was a discrepancy between ordered and received quantities or condition. True if discrepancy exists, False if receipt matches order exactly.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes explaining any discrepancies in quantity, quality, or condition between what was ordered and what was received. Includes reasons for rejection or partial acceptance.']
  donor_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt should be included in donor reporting and visibility dashboards. True for donor-funded procurements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Manufacturer expiry or best-before date for perishable commodities, medical supplies, and food items. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management.']
  freight_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Transportation and freight costs associated with this goods receipt, if separately tracked.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods require formal quality inspection before being released to inventory. True for medical supplies, food items, and high-value commodities.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the quality inspection process. Pending indicates inspection not yet completed, passed indicates goods approved for use, failed indicates goods rejected, waived indicates inspection was not required.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier or donor lot number for the received commodity, used for tracking and quality control purposes.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date on which the commodity was manufactured, used to calculate shelf life and ensure quality standards.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this goods receipt record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General notes and comments about the goods receipt, including special handling instructions, observations, or contextual information.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of the commodity that was originally ordered on the purchase order or expected from the donation.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of the commodity physically received and accepted at the warehouse or field location.']
  quantity_rejected decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of the commodity that was rejected upon receipt due to damage, expiry, or quality issues.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the goods were physically received at the warehouse or field location. This is the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  receipt_document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique document number for this goods receipt, typically generated by the ERP system (e.g., SAP goods receipt document number).']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt transaction. Draft indicates pending finalization, posted indicates completed and inventory updated, reversed indicates a correction was applied, cancelled indicates the receipt was voided.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the goods receipt was recorded in the system, including time zone information.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for individually tracked items such as medical equipment, vehicles, or high-value assets.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific storage location or bin within the warehouse where the received goods were placed. Used for precise inventory tracking.']
  total_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of the goods received, calculated as quantity received multiplied by unit cost. Used for accounts payable and budget tracking.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost per unit of the received commodity as stated on the purchase order or donation valuation. Used for inventory valuation and financial accounting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the commodity quantity is measured. EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, LT=Liter, MT=Metric Ton, BX=Box, CS=Case, PK=Pack. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|LT|MT|BX|CS|PK — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the physical receipt of commodities at a warehouse or field location against a purchase order or in-kind donation. Records receipt date, receiving warehouse, commodity, quantity received, condition on arrival (good/damaged/expired), batch/lot number, expiry date, receiving officer, discrepancy notes, and SAP goods receipt document number. Triggers inventory update and initiates three-way matching for AP payment processing.'
}

Table supply.inventory_balance {
  inventory_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory balance record. Primary key for this entity.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor grant funding this inventory. Used for fund accounting and donor reporting. Links to grant master data.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity or Non-Food Item (NFI) being tracked in inventory. Links to the commodity master data.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: Component-level pipeline stock reporting: NGO pipeline monitoring reports track inventory balances per program component to assess supply adequacy against component targets. Donor visibility requireme']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory balances must be attributed to cost centers for cost allocation, indirect cost rate calculations, and program vs. support cost reporting. NGO financial auditors expect cost center-level inve']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donor-restricted inventory (inventory_balance.donor_restriction_flag=true) must be ring-fenced and reported against specific donor requirements. Direct FK enables donor reporting systems to query exac']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: NGO inventory is frequently donor-restricted to specific finance funds. Fund-level inventory valuation is required for donor reporting, fund balance reconciliation, and ensuring restricted commodities']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory balances must map to GL accounts for balance sheet reporting and inventory valuation. Essential for nonprofit financial statement preparation and asset accounting.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Unexplained stock losses or discrepancies identified during physical counts (last_physical_count_date) trigger compliance incidents for diversion investigation. Direct FK from inventory_balance to com']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the humanitarian program or project for which this inventory is allocated or reserved. Links to program master data.']
  last_count_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Physical inventory counts in NGO warehouses require a named responsible staff member for audit accountability, donor commodity verification reports, and SPHERE/cluster compliance. The inventory_balanc']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or storage location where the commodity is held. Links to warehouse master data.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer batch or lot number for the commodity. Used for quality control, recall management, and traceability.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the warehouse location. Used for geographic reporting and OCHA SitRep aggregation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory balance record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the inventory valuation. Used for multi-currency financial reporting.']
  donor_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory is subject to donor-imposed restrictions on use, distribution, or geographic allocation. True if restricted, False if unrestricted.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date for perishable commodities such as medical supplies, food, or nutrition items. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) distribution planning.']
  in_kind_donation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory was received as an in-kind donation rather than purchased. True if in-kind donation, False if purchased. Used for donor reporting and GAAP compliance.']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inventory transaction (receipt, issue, transfer, or adjustment) for this balance record. Used for aging analysis and pipeline monitoring.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count for this commodity at this warehouse. Used for inventory accuracy tracking and audit compliance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory balance record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  maximum_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum stock capacity or target level for this commodity at this warehouse. Used for inventory optimization and storage planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this inventory balance record. Used for documenting special conditions, quality issues, or operational context.']
  pipeline_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the inventory balance in the commodity pipeline. Used for OCHA cluster reporting and pipeline gap analysis.']
  quantity_available decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net quantity available for new distribution allocations. Calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_reserved minus quantity_quarantined.']
  quantity_in_transit decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity currently in transit to this warehouse from suppliers or other warehouses. Not yet available for distribution.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,3) [note: 'Current physical stock quantity available in the warehouse. Represents the total inventory balance at the snapshot date.']
  quantity_quarantined decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity held in quarantine due to quality issues, expiration concerns, or pending inspection. Not available for distribution.']
  quantity_reserved decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity allocated or reserved for planned distribution events but not yet dispatched. Reduces available stock for new allocations.']
  reorder_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum stock level threshold that triggers a replenishment order. Used for pipeline management and pre-positioning decisions.']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'Date for which this inventory balance snapshot is recorded. Supports historical trend analysis and time-series reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this inventory balance record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Unit4 ERP). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the commodity. Used for warehouse capacity planning and quality assurance.']
  total_valuation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the inventory balance. Calculated as quantity_on_hand multiplied by unit_cost. Used for financial statements and donor reporting.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or average cost per unit of the commodity. Used for inventory valuation and financial reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the commodity quantity. Used for consistent reporting and distribution planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|LTR|MTR|BOX|CARTON|PALLET|DOSE|VIAL|TABLET|KIT — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  warehouse_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location of the warehouse including city, region, or country. Used for logistics planning and SitRep reporting.']

  Note: 'Current and historical stock balance records for each commodity at each warehouse location. Captures commodity, warehouse, stock quantity on hand, quantity reserved for distribution, quantity in transit, quantity quarantined, reorder level, maximum stock level, last physical count date, and stock valuation. Supports commodity pipeline management, pre-positioning decisions, and OCHA cluster reporting on pipeline gaps.'
}

Business justification: Stock movements can be directly implicated in compliance incidents beyond audit findings — diversion during transfer, unauthorized movements, or theft in transit. stock_movement already has compliance']

Business justification: Intervention-level commodity accountability: stock_movement has no intervention_id FK despite being the core commodity tracking record. NGO donor reports and commodity accountability frameworks (e.g.,']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Stock transfers to/from partner warehouses are routine in partnership arrangements. Essential for inventory accountability, validates movements against partnership agreement scope, supports partner re']
  primary_stock_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, distribution point, or field location to which the commodity is being moved. Null for issue transactions to final beneficiaries or write-offs.']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the program or project for which this stock movement is being executed. Links commodity pipeline to program delivery for MEL reporting.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Stock movements of type \'receipt\' are directly triggered by purchase orders. Adding purchase_order_id normalizes the existing denormalized purchase_order_number (STRING) field, enabling proper referen']
  source_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse. Business justification: Stock movements track inventory transfers between warehouses or within a warehouse. The source_location_id (BIGINT) currently holds the origin warehouse reference. Renaming to source_warehouse_id and ']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who authorized or approved this stock movement transaction. Required for accountability and audit trail, especially for adjustments, losses, and write-offs.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external supplier or vendor from whom goods were received (applicable for movement_type = receipt). Null for internal movements.']
  authorizing_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the staff member who authorized this stock movement. Denormalized for reporting and audit trail readability.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier batch/lot number for the commodity. Critical for traceability, recall management, and quality control, especially for medical supplies and food items.']

  count_team_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the physical inventory count team or cycle that generated this movement record (applicable only for movement_type = physical_count). Links to physical inventory count documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this stock movement record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost fields. Typically the organization\'s functional currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the commodity batch being moved. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management, especially for medical supplies, food items, and time-sensitive NFIs.']
  in_kind_donation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity was received as an in-kind donation (True) or procured through cash purchase (False). Critical for donor reporting and IATI transparency.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date on which quality inspection was performed on the commodity. Null if inspection was not required or waived.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member or third-party inspector who performed the quality inspection. Null if inspection was not required or waived.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this stock movement record was last updated. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  movement_date date [note: 'Business date on which the stock movement occurred or is scheduled to occur. This is the operational event date, distinct from system audit timestamps.']
  movement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for this stock movement transaction, used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation. Format typically includes movement type prefix and sequential number.']
  movement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock movement transaction. Tracks workflow state from initiation through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_transit|completed|cancelled|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical stock movement was executed or recorded. Used for detailed audit trail and sequence reconstruction.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stock movement transaction. Receipt = goods received into warehouse; Issue = goods distributed/issued out; Transfer = movement between warehouses; Adjustment = correction entry; Physical_count = reconciliation from physical inventory count; Loss = documented loss/damage; Write_off = removal from inventory due to expiry/obsolescence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: receipt|issue|transfer|adjustment|physical_count|loss|write_off — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this stock movement transaction. Used to capture additional context, special handling instructions, or operational observations.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of quality inspection performed on the commodity at the time of receipt or movement. Pending = inspection not yet completed; Passed = meets quality standards; Failed = rejected; Waived = inspection requirement waived for emergency; Not_required = commodity type does not require inspection.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the commodity being moved in the transaction. Always positive; movement direction is determined by movement_type. Precision supports fractional units for medical supplies and bulk commodities.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining the business reason for the stock movement. Critical for loss analysis, donor reporting, and pipeline management. Normal_receipt = routine procurement; Emergency_receipt = rapid response; Beneficiary_distribution = direct aid delivery; Program_issue = program consumption; Inter_warehouse_transfer = logistics optimization; Count_adjustment = inventory reconciliation; Expiry/Damage/Theft/Loss_in_transit/Obsolescence = loss categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: normal_receipt|emergency_receipt|beneficiary_distribution|program_issue|inter_warehouse_transfer|count_adjustment|expiry|damage|theft|loss_in_transit|obsolescence — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text detailed explanation of the reason for the stock movement, especially for adjustments, losses, and write-offs. Provides context beyond the standardized reason code.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Unique number of the source document (GRN, waybill, distribution order, count sheet, etc.) that authorizes or records this movement. Provides full audit trail linkage.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of source document that authorizes or records this stock movement. GRN = Goods Received Note; Waybill = transport document; Distribution_order = beneficiary distribution plan; Count_sheet = physical inventory count; Adjustment_memo = correction authorization; Loss_report = documented loss/damage; Write_off_authorization = disposal approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GRN|waybill|distribution_order|count_sheet|adjustment_memo|loss_report|write_off_authorization — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for individually tracked items such as medical equipment, vehicles, or high-value assets. Null for bulk commodities.']
  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost of this stock movement transaction (quantity × unit_cost), in the organization\'s functional currency. Used for inventory valuation, grant expenditure tracking, and donor reporting. Confidential business data.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for the shipment associated with this stock movement. Enables real-time visibility of in-transit commodities.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for this stock movement (applicable for transfers and receipts). Road = truck/vehicle; Air = aircraft; Sea = ship/barge; Rail = train; Pipeline = bulk liquid/gas; Hand_carry = manual transport for small quantities.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the commodity at the time of this movement, in the organization\'s functional currency. Used for inventory valuation and donor financial reporting. Confidential business data.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field. Standardized to organizational commodity catalog units. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: piece|box|carton|pallet|kg|liter|meter|dose|kit|set — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional log of all inventory movements including receipts, issues, transfers between warehouses, adjustments, physical count reconciliations, losses, and write-offs. Each record captures movement type (receipt/issue/transfer/adjustment/physical_count/loss/write-off), commodity, source location, destination location, quantity, movement date, reference document (GRN/waybill/distribution order/count sheet), reason code, authorizing officer, and count team reference (for physical count adjustments). Provides full commodity pipeline audit trail for donor reporting, IATI transparency, inventory accuracy management, and loss detection.'
}

  estimated_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total budget allocated for this distribution plan including commodity costs, logistics, and operational expenses.']

  geographic_coverage_admin3 varchar [note: 'Third-level administrative division (sub-district, municipality) where distribution will occur.']
  geographic_coverage_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the distribution will take place.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution plan record was last updated or modified.']
  mel_indicator_alignment varchar [note: 'Key MEL indicators or KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) that this distribution plan contributes to achieving.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this distribution plan.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the distribution plan indicating its approval and execution state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when distribution activities are planned to be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when distribution activities are planned to begin.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment level for this distribution plan considering security, logistics, and operational challenges.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Sustainable Development Goals that this distribution plan supports (e.g., SDG 1: No Poverty, SDG 2: Zero Hunger).']
  security_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether security clearance or authorization is required before distribution can proceed in the target area.']
  target_beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Planned number of individual beneficiaries or households to be reached by this distribution plan.']
  target_household_count varchar [note: 'Planned number of households to receive assistance under this distribution plan.']

  Note: 'Master plan for the distribution of humanitarian commodities to beneficiaries at field locations. Captures distribution plan name, target program, geographic coverage (country/admin levels), planned distribution date range, target beneficiary count, commodity list with planned quantities per beneficiary, distribution modality (direct/voucher/cash-in-kind), responsible field office, and approval status. Links supply chain planning to field operations and MEL targets.'
}

Table supply.distribution_order {

  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.component. Business justification: Component-level beneficiary distribution tracking: distribution orders must be attributed to program components for MEL indicator reporting (e.g., beneficiaries reached under nutrition component vs. s']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor organization funding the commodities in this distribution order. Links order to donor restrictions and reporting requirements.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Distribution orders trigger distribution expenses that must be allocated to cost centers for program cost tracking. Essential for nonprofit expense allocation and reporting.']
  distribution_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating distribution plan that authorized this order. Links the order to the strategic distribution planning process.']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Distribution orders execute donor-funded programs and must comply with donor requirements on beneficiary targeting, quantities, documentation, and reporting. Direct FK enables compliance verification ']
  emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Distribution orders executed under emergency response must reference the emergency for donor compliance, OCHA reporting, and post-emergency audits. distribution_order has emergency_response_flag but n']
  field_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.field_team. Business justification: Distribution orders are physically executed by field teams for last-mile delivery. Logistics coordinators assign a field team to each order for operational tracking, performance reporting, and account']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Distribution orders draw from specific finance funds for commodity release authorization. Fund balance checks at order creation prevent over-expenditure of donor-restricted funds. Existing FKs cover b']

Common in protection, health, and individual case management programs. Enables']

  dispatch_date date [note: 'Date when commodities were physically dispatched from the issuing warehouse. Marks the start of last-mile logistics execution.']
  distribution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution approach. General distributions serve entire populations, targeted distributions serve specific vulnerable groups, emergency distributions respond to crises.']
  driver_contact varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the driver during transit. Enables real-time communication for tracking and issue resolution during delivery.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the driver responsible for transporting the commodities. Used for accountability and security purposes in field operations.']
  emergency_response_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution order is part of an emergency response operation. True for rapid-onset disaster or crisis response orders.']
  estimated_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total monetary value of all commodities in this distribution order, expressed in US dollars. Used for financial tracking and donor reporting.']

  medical_supplies_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution order contains medical supplies or pharmaceuticals. Triggers special handling and regulatory compliance requirements.']
  nfi_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution order contains Non-Food Items such as shelter materials, hygiene kits, or household supplies. Used for commodity classification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or observations related to this distribution order. Captures context, issues, or special circumstances not covered by structured fields.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution order was created and entered into the system. Business event timestamp for order initiation.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique order number assigned to this distribution order for tracking and reference purposes. Format: DO-YYYYMMDD-sequence.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the distribution order. Tracks progression from draft through approval, dispatch, transit, delivery, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|dispatched|in_transit|delivered|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the distribution order. Determines urgency of dispatch and delivery, with emergency orders receiving expedited processing.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery of commodities to the destination distribution point. Used for logistics planning and beneficiary communication.']
  special_handling_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific handling requirements for commodities in this order, such as fragile items, hazardous materials, or temperature-sensitive goods. Ensures proper care during transport.']
  total_commodity_lines varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct commodity line items included in this distribution order. Summary count for order complexity assessment.']
  total_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all commodities in this distribution order, expressed in standard units. Summary measure for order volume.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of all commodities in this distribution order, measured in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space and transport capacity planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total weight of all commodities in this distribution order, measured in kilograms. Used for transport planning and capacity management.']
  transport_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual or estimated cost of transporting commodities from warehouse to distribution point, expressed in US dollars. Used for budget tracking and cost analysis.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for dispatching commodities from warehouse to distribution point. Critical for logistics planning and cost allocation.']
  vehicle_registration varchar [note: 'Registration number or identifier of the vehicle used for transporting commodities. Used for fleet management and security tracking.']
  waybill_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the transport waybill or shipping document accompanying the commodity shipment. Used for tracking and proof of dispatch.']

  Note: 'Operational order authorizing the release and dispatch of commodities from a warehouse to a field distribution point for beneficiary delivery. Captures order number, originating distribution plan, issuing warehouse, destination distribution point, commodity lines with quantities, dispatch date, transport mode, waybill reference, loading officer, and order status (draft/approved/dispatched/delivered/closed). Triggers stock movement and last-mile logistics execution.'
}

Table supply.waybill {
  waybill_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the waybill shipping document. Primary key for the waybill entity.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or funding source that finances this shipment. Required for donor reporting and fund accounting.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Transport costs recorded on waybills are charged to specific budget lines in NGO grant budgets. Budget line-level freight cost tracking is required for donor financial reports. Existing FKs cover cost']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Waybills represent transport costs that must be allocated to cost centers for logistics expense tracking. Essential for nonprofit program cost allocation and reporting.']
  dispatched_by_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: NGO supply chain accountability requires a named NGO staff member who authorized and dispatched each waybill — distinct from the external driver. This supports internal audit, loss/diversion investiga']
  distribution_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_order. Business justification: Waybill is the shipping document that accompanies commodity movements from warehouse to distribution point. Each waybill is issued for a specific distribution order. The distribution_order table has w']
  field_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.field_team. Business justification: Waybills document handover of goods to a receiving field team at destination. Supply chain staff track which field team received a consignment for stock reconciliation and accountability audits. No ex']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Waybill transport costs are charged to specific finance funds in NGO logistics. Fund-level freight cost tracking is required for donor financial reporting and fund balance management. Existing FKs cov']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Waybills require GL accounts for freight expense posting and financial reporting. Essential for nonprofit expense accounting and financial statement preparation.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Transport incidents (accidents, seal tampering, driver misconduct, cargo diversion) trigger compliance incident reports requiring investigation. Real business process: logistics incident management an']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the humanitarian program or project that this shipment supports. Links commodities to program activities and beneficiaries.']
  origin_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse from which the commodities were dispatched. Links to warehouse master data.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Waybills often involve partner organizations as shippers or receivers in last-mile logistics. Tracks logistics responsibility, validates against partnership agreement transport obligations, supports a']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Waybill is the shipping document for a shipment. In humanitarian logistics, waybills accompany both international shipments (sea/air freight) and domestic distribution movements. This FK links the way']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transport company or carrier responsible for moving the commodities. Links to partner or vendor master data.']
  waybill_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination location (warehouse, distribution point, or project site) where commodities are being delivered.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when commodities were delivered and received at destination. Used for performance measurement against estimated delivery date.']
  arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the vehicle arrived at the destination location. Used to calculate actual transit time and delivery performance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this waybill record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  customs_clearance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs clearance is required for this shipment (cross-border movements). True for international shipments.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs declaration or clearance document number for cross-border shipments. Required for international humanitarian aid movements.']
  departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the vehicle departed from the origin warehouse. Used for transit time analysis and security tracking.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of any quantity discrepancies, damage, or quality issues identified upon receipt. Critical for loss investigation and donor reporting.']
  discrepancy_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between total dispatched and total received quantities. Positive values indicate shortages, negative values indicate overages.']
  dispatch_date date [note: 'Date when the shipment was dispatched from the origin warehouse. Critical for lead time calculation and delivery performance tracking.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance in kilometers between origin and destination. Used for cost calculation and transit time estimation.']
  driver_contact_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number of the driver for real-time communication during transit. Essential for emergency contact and route coordination.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Official driver license number for verification and compliance purposes. Required for audit trails and security checks.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the driver responsible for transporting the commodities. Used for accountability and security verification.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned or expected date of arrival at destination. Used for distribution planning and beneficiary communication.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling. True if hazardous materials present.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the cargo insurance policy covering this shipment. Required for claims processing in case of loss or damage.']
  insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo insurance is required for this shipment. True for high-value or high-risk shipments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this waybill record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change for audit and synchronization purposes.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency classification for the shipment. Critical priority typically reserved for life-saving commodities in emergency response.']
  receipt_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical or digital signature was captured upon receipt. True if signature obtained, False otherwise.']
  received_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who received and signed for the shipment at destination. Critical for accountability and audit trail.']
  received_by_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the person who received the shipment. Provides context for authorization level and responsibility.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'General notes, special instructions, or additional context about the shipment. May include handling instructions, security concerns, or coordination notes.']
  route_description varchar [note: 'Description of the planned transport route including major waypoints, checkpoints, or transit hubs. Important for security planning in conflict zones.']
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security seal was intact upon arrival at destination. True if intact, False if broken or tampered.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique security seal number applied to the shipment container or vehicle to prevent tampering. Critical for loss prevention and donor audit compliance.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the waybill shipment. Tracks progression from dispatch through final receipt and reconciliation.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment purpose. Distinguishes between emergency relief, routine program supplies, warehouse transfers, and returns.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment requires temperature-controlled transport (cold chain). True for medical supplies, vaccines, or perishable items.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Exceedance may compromise commodity efficacy.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Critical for cold chain compliance and commodity quality.']
  total_dispatched_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all commodity items dispatched on this waybill. Sum of all line-item dispatched quantities.']
  total_received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all commodity items received at destination. Used to calculate overall shipment discrepancy.']
  transport_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for transporting this shipment. Includes carrier fees, fuel surcharges, and handling charges.']
  transport_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transport cost amount. Essential for multi-currency operations and financial reporting.']
  vehicle_registration varchar [note: 'Official registration or license plate number of the vehicle used for transport. Critical for security tracking and incident investigation.']
  waybill_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique waybill document number used for tracking and reference by transporters, warehouses, and auditors. Typically follows organizational numbering convention.']

  Note: 'Transactional shipping document accompanying commodity movements between warehouses or to distribution points. Records waybill number, dispatch date, origin warehouse, destination location, transporter/carrier, vehicle registration, driver details, commodity lines with dispatched quantities, seal numbers, departure time, arrival time, received quantities, and discrepancy notes. Critical for last-mile accountability, loss tracking, and donor audit compliance.'
}

Table supply.procurement_request {

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: team_lead|program_manager|country_director|regional_director|hq_procurement|cfo|ceo — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  local_procurement_preference boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this procurement should prioritize local or in-country vendors to support local economies and reduce lead times. True if local preference applies, false otherwise.']
  quantity_requested decimal(18,3) [note: 'Numeric quantity of the item or service being requested. May represent units, volume, weight, or service hours depending on the request type.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation if the procurement request was rejected. Includes reasons such as insufficient budget, lack of justification, non-compliance with procurement policy, or program priority changes.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date on which this procurement request was formally submitted for review and approval. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the procurement request: draft (being prepared), submitted (awaiting review), under review (being evaluated), approved (authorized for procurement), rejected (not approved), cancelled (withdrawn), in procurement (being sourced), or fulfilled (completed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|cancelled|in_procurement|fulfilled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement request by the nature of what is being requested: goods, services, works, consultancy, Non-Food Items (NFI), medical supplies, equipment, vehicle, or construction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: goods|services|works|consultancy|nfi|medical_supplies|equipment|vehicle|construction — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the requested items or services must be delivered to the requesting location. Critical for supply chain planning and program delivery timelines.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique requisition number assigned to this procurement request. Used for tracking and reference across systems and communications.']
  sole_source_justification varchar [note: 'Justification narrative if this procurement is requested as a sole-source (non-competitive) procurement. Must explain why competitive bidding is not feasible or appropriate.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the requested quantity: each, box, carton, kilogram, liter, meter, cubic meter, pallet, set, service hour, day, or month. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|box|carton|kg|liter|meter|m3|pallet|set|service_hour|day|month — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the urgency of this procurement request: routine (standard lead time), urgent (expedited processing), emergency (immediate need), or life-saving (critical humanitarian response).']

  Note: 'Internal requisition raised by program or field teams requesting procurement of commodities or services. Captures requisition number, requesting unit/program, commodity or service description, quantity, estimated budget, urgency level (routine/urgent/emergency), required delivery date, funding source, justification narrative, and approval workflow status. Initiates the procurement cycle and links program needs to supply chain execution.'
}

Business justification: Shipment freight and customs costs are charged to cost centers for program vs. support cost allocation and indirect cost rate calculations. Fund-level freight cost tracking is required for donor financial reporting and fund balance management. No existing FK from shi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account.
