GL coding of logistics costs is required for accurate functional expense ']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Cargo loss, theft, diversion, quality failures, or customs violations trigger compliance incidents requiring investigation and donor notification. Real business process: incident reporting and correct']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: Intervention-level shipment tracking: shipments are a critical missing link — they have no intervention FK despite being the primary logistics vehicle for program delivery. Donor logistics reports and']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Shipments frequently managed by or consigned to partner organizations for last-mile delivery. Critical for logistics coordination, tracks custody transfer points, validates against partnership agreeme']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field project site receiving or sending this shipment.']
  responsible_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: NGO import/export compliance and customs clearance processes require a named responsible staff member per shipment for duty exemption applications, import permit management, and donor audit trails. Th']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Shipments are executed by logistics vendors/freight carriers who are registered in the vendor master. Adding vendor_id to shipment normalizes the existing carrier_name (STRING) denormalized field, ena']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin or destination warehouse for this shipment.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date when the shipment arrived at the destination port or warehouse, as confirmed by receiving personnel.']
  actual_departure_date date [note: 'Actual date when the shipment departed from the origin port or warehouse, as confirmed by the carrier or freight forwarder.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number for sea freight or Airway Bill number for air freight, serving as the legal document of title and receipt for the cargo.']
  carrier_reference_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number or booking reference for the shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO container number for sea freight (e.g., ABCD1234567) or vehicle registration/truck number for road transport.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of container or vehicle used: 20ft or 40ft standard containers, 40ft high cube for volume cargo, refrigerated for cold chain medical supplies, open top or flat rack for oversized items, truck for road transport, or aircraft for air freight. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 20ft_standard|40ft_standard|40ft_high_cube|refrigerated|open_top|flat_rack|truck|aircraft — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customs_broker_name varchar [note: 'Name of the licensed customs broker or clearing agent engaged to facilitate customs clearance and import documentation in the destination country.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance process: not required for domestic shipments, pending initiation, documentation submitted to customs broker, under review by customs authority, cleared and released, held for inspection or additional documentation, or exemption granted under humanitarian duty-free privileges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_required|pending|documentation_submitted|under_review|cleared|held|exemption_granted — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the shipment destination country (e.g., SYR, YEM, SDN for field operations).']
  destination_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the final destination warehouse, project site, or distribution point for the shipment.']
  destination_port_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE or IATA code for the arrival port (sea) or airport (air) where the shipment enters the destination country.']
  duty_exemption_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or letter from host government granting duty and tax exemption for humanitarian relief goods under bilateral agreements or United Nations privileges.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned or estimated date when the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination port or warehouse.']
  estimated_departure_date date [note: 'Planned or estimated date when the shipment is scheduled to depart from the origin port or warehouse.']
  freight_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight transportation cost in United States Dollars, including carrier charges, fuel surcharges, and handling fees.']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'UN hazardous materials classification code (e.g., UN1170 for ethanol, UN3373 for biological substances) if the shipment contains dangerous goods requiring special handling and documentation.']
  import_permit_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued import permit or license number required for controlled goods such as medical supplies, pharmaceuticals, or telecommunications equipment.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Incoterms 2020 code defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller: EXW (Ex Works), FCA (Free Carrier), CPT (Carriage Paid To), CIP (Carriage and Insurance Paid To), DAP (Delivered at Place), DPU (Delivered at Place Unloaded), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid), FAS (Free Alongside Ship), FOB (Free on Board), CFR (Cost and Freight), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Insurance policy number covering the shipment cargo against loss, damage, or theft during transit.']
  insured_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Declared value of the shipment cargo in United States Dollars for insurance purposes, representing the replacement cost of the goods.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking for status updates and documentation changes.']
  number_of_cartons varchar [note: 'Total count of individual cartons, boxes, or packages in the shipment, used for inventory reconciliation and distribution planning.']
  number_of_pallets varchar [note: 'Total count of pallets or skids in the shipment, used for warehouse handling and loading dock planning.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the shipment origin country (e.g., USA, DNK, ARE for procurement hubs).']
  origin_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the origin location, warehouse, or facility from which the shipment departs.']
  origin_port_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE or IATA code for the departure port (sea) or airport (air) from which the shipment originates.']
  phytosanitary_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued by the origin country plant protection authority for shipments containing agricultural products, seeds, or wooden packaging materials, certifying compliance with international phytosanitary standards.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for the shipment, used for tracking and coordination with freight forwarders, customs brokers, and field offices.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment: planned for pipeline forecasting, booked with carrier, in transit en route, customs clearance awaiting documentation, cleared and released, delivered to final destination, or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|booked|in_transit|customs_clearance|cleared|delivered|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment purpose: emergency relief for rapid response, pre-positioned stock for contingency warehouses, program supply for ongoing operations, in-kind donation from corporate or government donors, medical supply for health programs, or Non-Food Item (NFI) consignment for shelter and household goods.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing special handling requirements such as fragile goods, keep upright, protect from moisture, security escort required, or priority unloading for emergency relief supplies.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment requires temperature-controlled transport (cold chain) for medical supplies, vaccines, or perishable goods.']
  temperature_range_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments, ensuring cold chain integrity for vaccines and medical supplies.']
  temperature_range_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments, ensuring cold chain integrity for vaccines and medical supplies.']
  total_cargo_volume_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment cargo in cubic meters, used for warehouse space planning and freight costing for volumetric shipments.']
  total_cargo_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment cargo in kilograms, including packaging and pallets, used for freight costing and capacity planning.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this shipment: sea freight for ocean containers, air freight for urgent relief supplies, road transport for last-mile delivery, rail transport for overland corridors, or multimodal for combined methods.']

  Note: 'Master record for international and domestic commodity shipments including sea freight, air freight, and road transport consignments. Captures shipment reference, origin country/port, destination country/port/warehouse, transport mode, carrier/freight forwarder, container/vehicle details, bill of lading or airway bill number, estimated and actual departure/arrival dates, customs clearance status and documentation (import permits, phytosanitary certificates, exemption letters), duty/tax exemption reference, and total cargo weight/volume. Supports pipeline tracking for pre-positioned emergency stocks, customs broker coordination, and import compliance for humanitarian duty-free privileges under host government agreements.'
}

Table supply.purchase_order_line {
  purchase_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for this purchase order line item. Primary key.']
  award_budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award_budget_line. Business justification: Multi-line POs in NGOs span multiple donor budget lines; each line must be coded individually for allowability/allocability verification and donor financial reporting. Line-level budget coding is stan']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: PO lines create budget commitments at the most granular level in NGO commitment accounting. Budget line-level commitment tracking per PO line is required for encumbrance accounting and donor financial']
  budget_plan_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.budget_plan_line. Business justification: PO line to budget plan line matching: NGO financial controls require PO lines matched to specific budget plan lines for expenditure commitment tracking. This enables line-level budget utilization repo']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific commodity being procured on this line']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Multi-commodity PO lines require individual GL account coding (e.g., medical supplies vs. food vs. NFI on the same PO). Line-level GL coding is standard NGO procurement practice for accurate financial']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the parent purchase order document']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the purchase order (1, 2, 3...). Used for human reference and document display order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of this purchase order line (pending, approved, ordered, partially_received, fully_received, cancelled). Tracks line-level receipt progress.']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount for this line (quantity_ordered × unit_price). Contributes to purchase_order.subtotal_amount.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(15,2) [note: 'Quantity of the commodity ordered on this line, expressed in the commodity\'s standard unit of measure.']
  quantity_outstanding decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining quantity yet to be received (quantity_ordered - quantity_received). Drives open PO reporting and follow-up with vendors.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of this commodity actually received against this line. Updated by goods receipt notes (GRN). Used for three-way matching.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Program-requested delivery date for this specific commodity line. May differ from other lines on the same PO due to urgency or program schedules.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for this line (e.g., EA, KG, BOX, PALLET). Typically matches commodity.unit_of_measure but captured at line level for procurement flexibility.']
  unit_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of measure for this commodity on this purchase order. Vendor-specific and time-specific pricing.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the line-item detail of procurement purchase orders, capturing the specific commodities ordered on each PO. Each record links one purchase order to one commodity with line-specific quantities, pricing, delivery schedules, and receipt tracking. This is the operational record that procurement officers create when building multi-line purchase orders and that warehouse staff update when receiving shipments.. Existence Justification: In humanitarian procurement operations, purchase orders routinely contain multiple commodity line items (medical supplies, shelter materials, WASH items on a single PO), and each commodity appears across many purchase orders over time as programs continuously procure relief items. Procurement officers actively manage line-item details including per-line quantities, vendor pricing, delivery schedules, and receipt tracking—this is the operational record that drives three-way matching and inventory management.'
}

Table supply.distribution_order_line {
  distribution_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the distribution_order_line association']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this order line to the specific humanitarian commodity being authorized for dispatch.']
  distribution_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this commodity line to its parent distribution order header.']
  donor_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific donor funding this commodity line on this distribution order. A single order may have different commodities funded by different donors, making this a line-level attribute rather than an order-header attribute.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the specific lot being dispatched on this order line. Critical for food, medical, and WASH commodities. Belongs to the dispatched lot on this order line, not to the commodity master (which tracks shelf_life_days generically).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Production lot or batch number of the specific commodity stock being dispatched on this order line. Enables traceability from distribution point back to procurement source. Belongs to the order-commodity pairing, not to either entity alone.']
  quantity_ordered decimal [note: 'Number of units of this commodity authorized for dispatch on this specific distribution order line. Belongs to the order-commodity pairing, not to the order header or commodity master.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for this commodity line on this distribution order. May differ from the commodity master UOM (e.g., commodity master tracks in kg, but distribution order line authorizes in ration packs).']
  unit_value_usd decimal [note: 'Monetary value per unit of this commodity at the time of dispatch on this order line, used for waybill valuation and donor reporting. Distinct from the standard_unit_cost on the commodity master, which is a catalog average.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Line (order line event) between distribution_order and commodity. It captures each individual commodity line item authorized within a distribution order, recording the specific quantity, valuation, lot traceability, and donor attribution for that commodity on that order. Each record links one distribution_order to one commodity with attributes — quantity, unit value, lot number, expiry date, donor restriction code — that exist only in the context of this specific order-commodity pairing and cannot reside on either the order header or the commodity master alone.. Existence Justification: In humanitarian supply chain operations, a single distribution order invariably authorizes the release of MULTIPLE commodity types simultaneously (e.g., NFI kits, food rations, medical supplies, WASH materials in one dispatch). Conversely, a single commodity (e.g., plastic sheeting) appears across MANY distribution orders over time. The relationship between a distribution order and its commodity lines is a well-recognized operational business entity — the \'distribution order line\' — that carries its own data (quantity ordered, unit value, lot number, expiry date, donor restriction code) that belongs to neither the order nor the commodity master alone.'
}

Table supply.vendor_catalog_item {
  vendor_catalog_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the vendor_catalog_item association']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific humanitarian commodity or relief item covered by this vendor catalog entry.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor (supplier or service provider) who is authorized to supply the commodity under this catalog item.']
  commodity_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of commodity categories that the vendor supplies, such as NFI (Non-Food Items), medical supplies, WASH (Water Sanitation and Hygiene) equipment, shelter materials, food commodities, or logistics services. [Moved from vendor: This denormalized comma-separated string on vendor attempts to capture which commodity categories a vendor supplies, but it cannot represent individual commodity-level agreements, negotiated prices, lead times, or prequalification statuses. The vendor_catalog_item association replaces this field with proper FK-based records per commodity, making commodity_categories redundant and structurally incorrect on the vendor entity.]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the negotiated unit price in this vendor catalog entry. Currency is specific to the vendor-commodity agreement, not the vendor alone.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this vendor catalog entry expires. After this date, the vendor is no longer authorized to supply this commodity under these terms without renewal.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this vendor catalog entry is valid and the vendor is authorized to supply this commodity at the negotiated terms.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from purchase order issuance to commodity receipt as committed by this vendor for this specific commodity. Differs from the commodity-level procurement_lead_time_days which is a catalog average.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal [note: 'Minimum quantity of this commodity that must be ordered from this specific vendor per purchase order, as per the vendor\'s catalog terms. Vendor-specific and may differ from the commodity-level minimum_order_quantity.']
  prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of this vendor for supplying this specific commodity. A vendor may be approved for some commodities but not others. Drives sourcing eligibility in procurement workflows.']
  unit_price decimal [note: 'The negotiated price per unit of measure for this commodity as agreed with this vendor. Varies by vendor-commodity combination and is the basis for price benchmarking and procurement decisions.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between a vendor and a commodity in the humanitarian supply chain. It captures the negotiated terms under which a vendor is authorized and prequalified to supply a specific commodity, including pricing, lead time, minimum order quantity, and approval status. Each record links one vendor to one commodity and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this vendor-commodity agreement. This is the SSOT for sourcing decisions, price benchmarking, and procurement compliance within the supply domain.. Existence Justification: In humanitarian procurement, vendor prequalification is explicitly commodity-specific — a vendor is approved to supply particular commodities at negotiated prices, lead times, and minimum order quantities. This means one vendor can be prequalified for many commodities, and one commodity can have many approved vendors, each with their own negotiated terms. The \'Vendor Catalog\' or \'Vendor Item Agreement\' is a well-recognized procurement concept in UN/NGO supply chains (used by WFP, UNICEF, ICRC) that is actively created, maintained, and queried by procurement officers.'
}

Table supply.distribution_plan_line {
  distribution_plan_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the distribution_plan_line association']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this plan line to the specific humanitarian commodity being distributed']
  distribution_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this plan line to its parent distribution plan']
  donor_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor funding this specific distribution plan imposes restrictions on the use of this particular commodity within this plan. Distinct from the commodity-level donor_restricted_flag, as restrictions are plan-and-donor-specific.']
  estimated_unit_cost decimal [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of this commodity as captured at plan creation time. May differ from the commodity master standard_unit_cost due to regional pricing, emergency procurement premiums, or donor-negotiated rates.']
  pipeline_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of this commodity line within the distribution plan. Tracks the commodity through the supply pipeline from planning to last-mile delivery. Belongs to the plan-commodity relationship, not to either entity alone.']
  planned_quantity decimal [note: 'Total planned quantity of this commodity to be distributed under this plan. Belongs to the plan-commodity combination, not to either entity alone — the same commodity has different planned quantities across different plans.']
  target_beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Number of beneficiaries targeted to receive this specific commodity under this plan. May differ from the plan-level target_beneficiary_count for partial distributions where not all beneficiaries receive all commodities (e.g., only pregnant women receive nutrition supplements).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for this commodity within this specific distribution plan. May differ from the commodity master UOM (e.g., commodity tracked in kg but distributed in ration packs).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Commodity Allocation (Distribution Plan Line) between a distribution_plan and a commodity. It captures the specific quantity, cost, pipeline status, and donor restrictions for each commodity included in a distribution plan. Each record links one distribution_plan to one commodity and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that specific plan-commodity combination — such as the planned quantity per beneficiary, the pipeline fulfillment status, and any donor restrictions applicable to that commodity within that plan. This is the operational record used for pipeline planning, stock reservation, and donor reporting.. Existence Justification: In humanitarian operations, a distribution plan is explicitly a multi-commodity document — a single plan for a refugee camp routinely covers 10-20 different commodity types (food, NFIs, WASH materials, medical supplies) each with distinct planned quantities per beneficiary, unit costs, and pipeline statuses. Conversely, a single commodity (e.g., plastic sheeting) appears across dozens of simultaneous distribution plans across different geographies and programs. The business actively manages this relationship as \'distribution plan lines\' or \'commodity allocations\' — operational records that humans create, update, and delete as part of pipeline planning, stock reservation, and donor reporting.'
}

Table volunteer.volunteer {
  volunteer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the volunteer record. Primary key for the volunteer entity.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.constituent. Business justification: Volunteers are frequently also donors in nonprofits. Linking enables integrated constituent relationship management, lifetime value analysis across giving and service, unified recognition programs, an']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Each volunteer is managed by a specific country office that handles their onboarding, compliance, and deployment. This is the primary administrative ownership relationship — NGO volunteer managers exp']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Federal funding recipients must screen volunteers against OFAC and sanctions lists. Required for USAID, EU funding compliance. Links volunteer record to their most recent screening result for audit tr']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the volunteers residential address, typically street number and name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the volunteers residential address, typically apartment or unit number.']
  availability_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours per week the volunteer is available to contribute, used for scheduling and capacity planning.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of the volunteer for new assignments. Available indicates ready for deployment, unavailable indicates temporarily not available, on assignment indicates currently deployed, on leave indicates scheduled absence, and inactive indicates not currently volunteering.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent background check was completed for the volunteer.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the volunteers background check or vetting process. Not required indicates no check needed for this role, pending indicates check requested, in progress indicates check underway, cleared indicates check passed, flagged indicates issues identified, and expired indicates check needs renewal.']
  certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of professional certifications, licenses, and credentials held by the volunteer, such as first aid, WASH (Water Sanitation and Hygiene) training, PSS (Psychosocial Support) certification, or medical licenses.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the volunteers residential address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the volunteers residential address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the volunteer record was first created in the system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the volunteer, used for age verification, insurance, and compliance purposes.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for volunteer communication, coordination, and notifications.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the volunteers designated emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the volunteers designated emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the volunteer, such as spouse, parent, sibling, or friend.']
  external_volunteer_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the volunteer as known in Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits or other external systems. Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'The given name of the volunteer.']
  first_volunteer_date date [note: 'Date of the volunteers first volunteer activity or assignment with the organization.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self-identified gender of the volunteer, used for demographic reporting and program planning.']
  geographic_base varchar [note: 'Primary geographic location or region where the volunteer is based and prefers to serve, used for matching volunteers to local programs and field operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the volunteer record was last updated or modified.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'The family name or surname of the volunteer.']
  last_volunteer_date date [note: 'Date of the volunteers most recent volunteer activity or assignment with the organization.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'The middle name or initial of the volunteer, if applicable.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number for the volunteer, used for SMS notifications and field coordination.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the volunteers nationality or citizenship.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the volunteer, including special considerations, preferences, feedback, and coordination details.']
  onboarding_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the volunteer completed all onboarding requirements and became eligible for deployment.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the volunteers onboarding process. Pending indicates application received, in progress indicates onboarding steps underway, completed indicates fully onboarded and ready for deployment, expired indicates onboarding credentials need renewal, and inactive indicates volunteer is not currently active.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the volunteer.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the volunteers residential address.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the volunteer prefers to be called, which may differ from their legal name.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the volunteers primary or preferred language for communication.']
  recognition_level varchar [note: 'Recognition tier or award level assigned to the volunteer based on their contribution hours, impact, and tenure. Used for volunteer appreciation programs and engagement tracking.']
  skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of skills, competencies, and professional qualifications the volunteer possesses, used for matching volunteers to program needs and deployment opportunities.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the volunteers residential address.']
  total_volunteer_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative total number of hours the volunteer has contributed across all assignments and programs since registration.']
  volunteer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the volunteer based on their role and engagement model. Community health workers provide ongoing health services, field volunteers support program delivery, event-based supporters assist with specific events, skilled professionals offer specialized expertise, youth volunteers are under 25, and corporate volunteers come through employer partnerships.']
  willing_to_travel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the volunteer is willing to travel outside their geographic base for assignments, deployments, or emergency response.']

  Note: 'Master record for all individuals who serve as volunteers across the organization\'s programs and events. Captures volunteer identity, contact details, demographic profile, volunteer type (community health worker, field volunteer, event-based supporter), onboarding status, availability, language skills, geographic base, emergency contact, and engagement history. This is the SSOT for volunteer identity within the volunteer domain, distinct from paid workforce managed in the workforce domain. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits.'
}

Compliance officers']

Supports the Volunteer and Staff Recruitment and Management core business process.'

Table volunteer.role {
  role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the volunteer role. Primary key for the volunteer role catalog.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Volunteer roles with stipend_eligible flag and stipend_amount must map to a GL account for correct accounting classification of volunteer-related expenses. NGO chart-of-accounts mapping for volunteer ']
  governance_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governance_policy. Business justification: NGO roles are defined under specific governance policies (safeguarding policy, code of conduct, child protection policy). The role\'s safeguarding_training_required and background_check_required fields']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: NGO roles are defined against specific compliance obligations (CHS safeguarding standards, donor-mandated background check requirements). Role designers reference the governing obligation to set manda']
  background_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal background check or police clearance is required before assignment to this volunteer role, typically for roles involving vulnerable populations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this volunteer role record was first created in the system.']
  deployment_context varchar [note: 'The operational context in which this volunteer role is typically deployed, aligned with program phases and humanitarian response cycles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Emergency Response|Development Program|Advocacy Campaign|Community Outreach|Capacity Building|Disaster Relief|Long-Term Recovery — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this volunteer role is scheduled to be discontinued or archived. Null for ongoing roles with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this volunteer role became or will become active and available for volunteer assignments.']
  estimated_time_commitment_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Expected number of volunteer hours per week or per assignment period for this role, used for scheduling and capacity planning.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'The primary program or operational area where this volunteer role is deployed. WASH (Water Sanitation and Hygiene), GBV (Gender-Based Violence), PSS (Psychosocial Support), MEAL (Monitoring Evaluation Accountability and Learning), ICT4D (Information and Communication Technology for Development). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WASH|GBV|PSS|Nutrition|Logistics|Health|Education|Protection|Shelter|Livelihoods|MEAL|Communications|Fundraising|Administration|ICT4D — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  insurance_coverage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization must provide or verify insurance coverage (medical, liability, travel) for volunteers in this role.']
  language_requirements varchar [note: 'Languages required or preferred for this volunteer role, including proficiency levels, to ensure effective communication with beneficiaries and team members.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that most recently updated this volunteer role record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this volunteer role record was most recently updated.']
  maximum_concurrent_assignments varchar [note: 'Maximum number of volunteers that can be simultaneously assigned to this role, used for capacity planning and deployment management.']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years that a volunteer must be to be eligible for this role, based on legal, safety, and program requirements.']
  physical_demands varchar [note: 'Level of physical exertion and mobility required for this volunteer role, ranging from sedentary office work to strenuous field activities.']
  preferred_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of desirable but not mandatory skills that would enhance volunteer effectiveness in this role.']
  recognition_program_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether volunteers in this role are eligible for formal recognition programs, awards, or certificates upon completion.']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this volunteer role can be performed remotely or requires physical presence at a specific location.']
  reporting_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of reporting obligations for volunteers in this role, including frequency, format, and content of activity reports or timesheets.']
  required_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of essential skills, competencies, and technical abilities required to perform this volunteer role effectively.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Security and safety risk classification for this volunteer role based on deployment context, location, and nature of activities.']
  role_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the volunteer role used across systems and documentation.']
  role_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the volunteer role including responsibilities, typical activities, and expected contributions to program outcomes.']
  role_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the volunteer role indicating whether it is available for recruitment and assignment.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the volunteer role by engagement model and location context.']
  stipend_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard stipend or per diem amount provided to volunteers in this role, if applicable. Null if no stipend is provided.']
  stipend_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the stipend amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  stipend_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether volunteers in this role are eligible to receive a stipend, per diem, or other financial support to cover expenses.']
  supervision_level varchar [note: 'Level of supervision and oversight required for volunteers in this role, ranging from independent work to intensive direct supervision.']
  time_commitment_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the estimated time commitment (e.g., hours per week, total hours for event-based roles).']
  title varchar [note: 'The official title of the volunteer role as presented to volunteers and used in recruitment materials.']
  travel_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this volunteer role requires travel to field sites, beneficiary locations, or other operational areas.']
  typical_assignment_duration_days varchar [note: 'Standard length of a volunteer assignment in this role measured in days, used for planning and volunteer expectation setting.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this volunteer role record.']

  Note: 'Defines the catalog of volunteer roles available across programs and field operations. Each role specifies the role title, functional area (e.g., WASH, GBV, PSS, nutrition, logistics), required skills, minimum certification requirements, physical demands, deployment context (field/office/remote), maximum concurrent assignments, and whether the role is active. Acts as the reference catalog for role-based volunteer deployment.'
}

  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member responsible for supervising and managing the volunteer during this deployment.']

Business justification: Deployments require verification that the organization holds valid statutory registration in the deployment jurisdiction. Essential for legal volunteer operations and insurance coverage, particularly ']
  subaward_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.subaward. Business justification: Volunteers deployed under partner-org subaward arrangements must reference the governing subaward instrument for FFATA compliance, flow-down requirement enforcement, and subaward monitoring. volunteer']
  volunteer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the volunteer being deployed to the program or field operation.']
  volunteer_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to volunteer.volunteer_team. Business justification: Volunteer deployments can be team-based assignments where the volunteer is deployed as part of a team structure. Not all deployments are team-based (many are individual), so this FK is nullable. This ']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse. Business justification: Volunteers are frequently deployed to warehouse operations (inventory management, goods receipt, kit assembly, donation sorting). Tracking warehouse assignment is essential for security clearance, acc']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date the volunteer completed or left the deployment, which may differ from the scheduled deployment end date.']
  actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of volunteer hours actually contributed during this deployment, tracked for MEL and donor reporting.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date the volunteer began work on the deployment, which may differ from the scheduled deployment start date.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the volunteer is deployed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this deployment record was first created in the system.']
  deployment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique business identifier for the deployment assignment, used for tracking and reporting.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the volunteer deployment: active (currently deployed), completed (assignment finished), withdrawn (volunteer left early), suspended (temporarily paused), pending (approved but not started), or cancelled (assignment cancelled before start).']
  deployment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deployment based on the nature of the assignment: emergency response, development program, event support, capacity building, advocacy campaign, or disaster relief.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the volunteer deployment to the partner organization ended or is scheduled to end. Null indicates an ongoing deployment.']
  fte_equivalent decimal(5,4) [note: 'The full-time equivalent contribution of the volunteer during this deployment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.5000 for half-time, 1.0000 for full-time).']
  hours_contributed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the volunteer has contributed during this specific deployment to this partner organization. Used for tracking volunteer engagement and reporting.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific location, community, or facility where the volunteer is deployed.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who last modified this deployment record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this deployment record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the deployment, including coordination details, performance observations, or special circumstances.']
  orientation_completed_date date [note: 'The date when the volunteer completed the deployment-specific orientation or onboarding.']
  orientation_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the volunteer has completed the required orientation or onboarding for this specific deployment.']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Performance assessment score for the volunteer during this deployment, typically on a scale (e.g., 1.00 to 5.00). Reflects the partner organization\'s evaluation of the volunteer\'s effectiveness in this specific assignment.']
  planned_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of volunteer hours planned or expected for this deployment assignment.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level of this deployment assignment, indicating urgency and importance for resource allocation and coordination.']
  recognition_awarded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the volunteer received formal recognition or an award for their contribution during this deployment.']
  region varchar [note: 'The administrative region, province, or district within the country where the volunteer is deployed.']
  remote_deployment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a remote or virtual deployment where the volunteer contributes remotely rather than being physically present at the project site.']
  role varchar [note: 'Specific role or function the volunteer performs during this deployment (e.g., Community Health Worker, Field Coordinator, Training Facilitator). The same volunteer may have different roles at different partner organizations.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'The level of security clearance required or obtained for this deployment, particularly relevant for high-risk or conflict-affected areas.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which this deployment record originated, typically Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this deployment record in the source system.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Any special conditions, requirements, or accommodations associated with this deployment (e.g., remote work, hazard pay equivalent, specific equipment needs).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the volunteer deployment to the partner organization began. Tracks the beginning of the assignment period.']
  volunteer_deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deployment. Active indicates ongoing assignment, Completed indicates successfully finished deployment, Suspended indicates temporarily paused, Cancelled indicates terminated before completion.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date when the volunteer withdrew from or was removed from the deployment.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided when a volunteer withdraws from or is removed from a deployment before completion.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who created this deployment record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative operational record of a volunteer\'s assignment to a specific program, project site, field operation, or event for a defined period. Captures deployment start and end dates, assigned program, field location or country office, role performed (FK to volunteer_role), deployment status (active, completed, withdrawn, suspended), supervising staff member, deployment type (emergency response, development program, event support), FTE equivalent contribution, grant or funding source supporting the position, and any special conditions or security clearance requirements. This is the SSOT for volunteer-to-program assignments, serving both field coordination and MEL/donor accountability reporting. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365.'
}

Linking hour_log to its pa']

  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse. Business justification: Volunteer hours logged at warehouse locations (sorting in-kind donations, physical inventory counts, emergency kit assembly) must capture warehouse location for cost center allocation, in-kind contrib']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the volunteer activity performed during the logged hours. Provides context for the contribution.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the volunteer activity performed. Categorizes the nature of the volunteer contribution for reporting and analysis.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the volunteer hour log entry. Tracks the workflow state of the timesheet record.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the volunteer hours were approved. Records the moment of supervisor sign-off.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to detailed audit trail records for this hour log entry. Supports institutional donor compliance and audit requirements.']
  beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Number of beneficiaries directly served or impacted during the volunteer activity. Supports MEL indicator calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hour log record was first created in the system. System audit field for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the fair market value rate and in-kind value. Supports multi-currency volunteer operations.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to submit or verify the volunteer hours. Supports digital check-in and GPS verification methods.']
  donor_report_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the volunteer hours are eligible for inclusion in donor reports and institutional compliance reporting.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the volunteer activity ended. Used to calculate duration and validate claimed hours.']
  fair_market_value_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fair market value rate per hour used to calculate the in-kind contribution value of volunteer hours. Based on IRS guidelines or local equivalents.']
  hours_claimed decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of volunteer hours claimed or self-reported by the volunteer for the activity. Initial submission value before verification.']
  hours_verified decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of volunteer hours verified and approved by a supervisor or through automated validation. Final accepted value for reporting.']
  in_kind_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated in-kind contribution value of the volunteer hours (hours_verified × fair_market_value_rate). Used for donor reporting and financial statements.']
  is_group_activity boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the volunteer activity was performed as part of a group or team (true) versus individually (false).']
  is_overtime boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the volunteer hours exceed the standard expected commitment for the period. Used for volunteer engagement monitoring.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the volunteer activity was performed virtually or remotely (true) versus on-site (false).']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the volunteer activity location. Supports GPS-based verification and spatial analysis.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Name or description of the specific location where the volunteer hours were contributed. Provides geographic context.']
  log_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the volunteer hours were contributed. Primary business event date for timesheet reporting.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the volunteer activity location. Supports GPS-based verification and spatial analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hour log record was last modified. System audit field for change tracking and compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the volunteer hour log entry. Captures supervisor feedback, exceptions, or contextual information.']
  recognition_milestone_triggered boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this hour log entry triggered a volunteer recognition milestone (e.g., 100 hours, 500 hours). Used to automate recognition workflows.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the supervisor for rejecting the volunteer hour log entry. Populated only when approval_status is rejected.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the volunteer activity started. Provides precise timing for shift-based or event-based volunteer work.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method or channel through which the volunteer submitted the hour log entry. Tracks data collection channel for quality assessment.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the volunteer hour log entry was initially submitted by the volunteer. Records the moment of timesheet submission.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the volunteer hours. Indicates the level of validation applied to the hour log entry.']

  Note: 'Authoritative transactional record of volunteer hours contributed per activity, deployment, or event — the timesheet equivalent for unpaid contributors. Captures log date, volunteer (FK to volunteer), deployment or program context (FK to volunteer_deployment), activity type, location, hours claimed, hours verified, verification method (supervisor sign-off, digital check-in/GPS, self-reported), approval status, approving supervisor, and notes. Primary source for donor reporting on volunteer in-kind contributions (valued at fair market rates per IRS guidelines or local equivalents), MEL indicator tracking (e.g., volunteer-hours per beneficiary), recognition milestone triggers, and BvA reporting on volunteer effort allocation across grants. Supports audit trail requirements for institutional donor compliance.'
}

Business justification: Training sessions are organized and delivered by specific country offices, especially mandatory safeguarding, protection, and technical trainings. The existing plain-text funding_source column is a denormalized representation of finance_fund. Replacing it with a proper F']

Business justification: NGO L&D compliance requires formal linkage between training programs and job profiles to enforce mandatory training requirements. training.mandatory_for_roles is a denormalized text field; replacing i']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Partner organizations frequently deliver training to volunteers (local context, safeguarding, technical skills). Tracking the delivering partner is essential for quality assurance, certification valid']
  accrediting_body varchar [note: 'Official organization or institution that accredits or endorses this training program, such as SPHERE Association, CHS Alliance, or national certification bodies.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assess volunteer competency and completion: written exam, practical demonstration, project submission, peer review, or no formal assessment.']
  available_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-3 language codes for all languages in which this training is available, supporting multilingual volunteer populations.']
  certification_awarded varchar [note: 'Name of the certificate or credential awarded to volunteers upon successful completion of the training program.']
  certification_validity_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the certification remains valid before renewal or recertification is required. Null if certification does not expire.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this training program record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_modality varchar [note: 'Method by which the training is delivered to volunteers: in-person classroom, e-learning online, blended (combination), virtual instructor-led, or self-paced.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the training program measured in hours, including all sessions and modules.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when this training program becomes available for volunteer enrollment and delivery.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date when this training program is scheduled to be retired or replaced. Null if no end date is planned.']
  facilitator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary facilitator or instructor responsible for delivering the training program.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training is mandatory for specific volunteer roles or deployment types. True if required, False if optional.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the training is delivered, using ISO 639-3 three-letter language codes (e.g., ENG for English, FRA for French, ARA for Arabic).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the training content and materials were last reviewed and updated to ensure relevance and accuracy.']
  materials_url varchar [note: 'Web URL where training materials, resources, and documentation can be accessed by volunteers and facilitators.']
  max_participants varchar [note: 'Maximum number of volunteers that can enroll in a single session or cohort of this training program. Null if no limit.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the staff member or partner who last modified this training program record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this training program record was last modified in the system.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review and update of the training program content and materials.']
  passing_score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required for volunteers to pass the training assessment and receive certification. Null if no assessment.']
  prerequisites varchar [note: 'Required prior training, certifications, or experience that volunteers must have before enrolling in this training program.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of United Nations Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) numbers that this training program supports (e.g., SDG 3 Good Health, SDG 4 Quality Education).']
  sphere_standard_alignment varchar [note: 'Specific SPHERE Humanitarian Charter and Minimum Standards chapters or technical standards that this training covers (e.g., WASH, Shelter, Health).']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended volunteer audience for this training, such as community health workers, field volunteers, event-based supporters, or program-specific volunteers.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full title of the training program or workshop as presented to volunteers.']
  training_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the training program. Categories include safety and security, technical skills, Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) orientation, SPHERE standards, Gender-Based Violence (GBV) awareness, Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (PSEA), first aid, and other capacity-building areas.']
  training_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the training program used in volunteer systems and communications.']
  training_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the training program content, learning objectives, and expected outcomes for volunteers.']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training program: draft (under development), active (available for enrollment), suspended (temporarily unavailable), retired (no longer offered), or under review (being updated).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the training program content, following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1), to track curriculum updates and revisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the staff member or partner who created this training program record in the system.']

  Note: 'Catalog of training programs, workshops, and capacity-building courses available to volunteers. Captures training title, delivery modality (in-person, e-learning, blended), training category (safety and security, technical skills, CHS orientation, SPHERE standards, GBV awareness, PSEA, first aid), duration, facilitator, accrediting body, certification awarded upon completion, and whether training is mandatory for specific roles. Distinct from the workforce LMS — this catalog is specific to volunteer capacity building.'
}

  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Source or channel through which the volunteer was enrolled in the training course.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training enrollment indicating the volunteers progress through the course.']
  feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments provided by the volunteer regarding their experience with the training course, including suggestions for improvement.']
  feedback_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating provided by the volunteer evaluating the quality and effectiveness of the training course, typically on a scale of 1 to 5.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the training enrollment record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  mandatory_training_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training course is mandatory for the volunteer based on their role, program assignment, or organizational policy (e.g., PSEA, CHS, safeguarding).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the training enrollment, including special accommodations, exceptions, or contextual information.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the training course assessment, typically expressed as a percentage.']
  recertification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the volunteer must complete recertification or refresher training to maintain their certification status.']
  recertification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the volunteer is required to undergo recertification or refresher training after the certificate expiry date.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the volunteer successfully completed the training course.']
  training_delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Mode or format in which the training course was delivered to the volunteer.']
  training_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the volunteer spent in the training course, including instruction, practice, and assessment time.']
  training_language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the training course content was delivered to the volunteer.']
  training_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the volunteer began the training course.']
  training_withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the volunteer withdrew from the training course before completion.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the volunteer or organization for withdrawing from the training course before completion.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a volunteer\'s enrollment in and completion of a specific training course. Captures enrollment date, training, volunteer, enrollment status (registered, in-progress, completed, failed, withdrawn), completion date, assessment score, certification issued, certificate expiry date, and continuing education credits earned. Supports compliance tracking for mandatory training (e.g., PSEA, CHS) and volunteer certification management.'
}

Table volunteer.certification {
  certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the certification record. Primary key for the certification entity.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Certifications with reimbursed_by_organization flag and cost_amount must be charged to specific grant budget lines for capacity-building expenditure reporting. NGO grant budgets include volunteer cert']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.commodity. Business justification: Commodity-specific certifications (certified to handle controlled medical supplies, food safety certification for nutrition commodities, hazmat certification for chemical storage) must link to commodi']
  governance_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governance_policy. Business justification: Certifications must meet standards defined in governance policies (safeguarding policy specifies required certifications, volunteer management policy defines minimum qualifications). Links certificati']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: NGO field deployment compliance requires certifications to be formally linked to job profiles. certification.mandatory_for_role is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to job_profile enables automat']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Donor and regulatory obligations frequently mandate specific volunteer certifications (e.g., USAID requires first-aid certification for health volunteers). Linking certification to the driving complia']
  training_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to volunteer.training. Business justification: A certification is frequently awarded upon successful completion of a specific training program. Linking certification to the training that produced it enables traceability between the training catalo']
  verified_by_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Tracks which staff member verified volunteer certification authenticity. Critical for audit trail, compliance with safeguarding requirements, and accountability in volunteer credentialing process. No ']
  volunteer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the volunteer who holds this certification. Links to the volunteer master record.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accreditation or regulatory body that oversees the issuing organization (e.g., national medical board, international humanitarian standards body). Used for credential validation.']
  assessment_passing_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the certification assessment. Used to determine whether the volunteer met competency standards.']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score or grade achieved on the certification assessment or examination. Null if no formal assessment was conducted.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or credential number issued by the certifying body. Used for verification and audit purposes.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the certification, credential, or training program (e.g., Certified Emergency Medical Technician, SPHERE Practitioner Certificate, PSEA Compliance Training, Community Health Worker Level 2).']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of certification record: professional license (e.g., nursing, medical), certificate (e.g., first aid, SPHERE practitioner), training completion, self-assessed proficiency, verified competency, or compliance certificate (e.g., Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (PSEA), safeguarding).']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory or organizational compliance category this certification satisfies: Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (PSEA), safeguarding, data protection, security clearance, health and safety, ethics, or none for non-compliance certifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: psea|safeguarding|data_protection|security|health_safety|ethics|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  continuing_education_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of continuing education or professional development hours required for renewal. Null if not applicable.']
  cost_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to obtain or renew the certification. Used for volunteer investment tracking and reimbursement processing.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost amount.']
  country_of_issue varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the country where the certification was issued. Relevant for professional licenses and country-specific credentials.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  deployment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification qualifies the volunteer for field deployment. True for certifications that meet deployment readiness criteria (verified, current, and role-appropriate).']
  evidence_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the supporting documentation (certificate scan, transcript, license copy) stored in the document management system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the certification expires and requires renewal. Null for certifications with no expiration (e.g., academic degrees, lifetime licenses).']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the certification or credential was originally issued to the volunteer.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, institution, or authority that issued the certification (e.g., Red Cross, Sphere Association, Ministry of Health, professional licensing board, internal training department).']
  language_of_certification varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the certification training and assessment were conducted. Important for language-specific skill matching and deployment planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the certification was most recently verified by the organization through documentation review or third-party confirmation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about the certification, including special conditions, restrictions, verification challenges, or other relevant information for volunteer coordinators.']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Level of proficiency or competency in the skill: beginner (basic awareness), intermediate (can perform with supervision), advanced (can perform independently), expert (can train others), master (recognized authority).']
  recognized_by_organization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is formally recognized and accepted by the organization for volunteer deployment and role assignment. False for certifications that are recorded but not organizationally endorsed.']
  reimbursed_by_organization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization reimbursed the volunteer for the cost of obtaining this certification. Used for financial tracking and volunteer benefit reporting.']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewals for time-limited certifications. Null if renewal is not required or frequency is irregular.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification requires periodic renewal to remain valid. True for time-limited certifications (e.g., first aid, PSEA training), false for permanent credentials.']
  skill_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of the skill or competency. Aligns with organizational skill taxonomy including medical, logistics, language, psychosocial support (PSS), Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH), Information and Communication Technology for Development (ICT4D), community mobilization, protection, nutrition, and education. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|logistics|language|psychosocial|wash|ict4d|community_mobilization|protection|nutrition|education — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  skill_name varchar [note: 'Specific name of the skill or competency (e.g., First Aid, Community Health Worker Training, SPHERE Standards, Gender-Based Violence (GBV) Response, Mid-Upper Arm Circumference (MUAC) Screening, Psychosocial Support (PSS) Facilitation).']
  training_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of training or instruction hours completed to earn this certification. Used for volunteer contribution tracking and competency assessment.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification status of the certification: verified (documentation confirmed), pending verification (under review), self-reported (not yet verified), expired (past validity date), revoked (withdrawn by issuer), not verified (no documentation provided).']

  Note: 'Unified competency and credential product — the single source of truth for all skills taxonomy and individual volunteer credentials within the volunteer domain. As REFERENCE CATALOG: defines skill categories (medical, logistics, language, psychosocial, WASH, ICT4D, community mobilization), proficiency levels, role-to-skill requirements, and mandatory vs. preferred designations for volunteer_role entries. As INDIVIDUAL RECORDS: tracks specific credentials held by each volunteer including professional licenses, first aid certificates, SPHERE practitioner credentials, PSEA compliance certificates, verified and self-assessed proficiency levels, issuing body, issue and expiry dates, certificate numbers, verification status, renewal requirements, and evidence document references. Supports skill-based volunteer matching, competency gap analysis, compliance tracking, deployment readiness assessment, and training needs identification.'
}

This link enables community-level volunteer coverage analysis, program reporting, and geographic deployment planning — esse']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Volunteer teams operate within cost center structures for budget accountability. Team-level budget allocation, equipment costs, and operational expenses are tracked by cost center for financial manage']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Volunteer teams operate under country office administrative oversight for compliance, budget allocation, and reporting. Required for organizational hierarchy, resource management, and country-level vo']
  distribution_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_plan.   emergency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.emergency. Business justification: Volunteer teams are mobilized and activated for specific emergency response operations. Linking teams to the emergency they serve supports emergency response sitrep reporting, team performance trackin']

  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: NGO field coordination requires knowing which partner organization a volunteer team is affiliated with or co-deployed under. Field coordination reports, joint response planning, and partner performanc']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary field project site or geographic location where this team operates.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who serves as the team supervisor or coordinator. May be null if led by a volunteer.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse. Business justification: Volunteer teams assigned to specific warehouses (warehouse management committees, inventory audit teams, donation processing teams) require warehouse linkage for team coordination, performance trackin']

  budget_allocation decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial budget allocated to support this volunteer team\'s operations, including training, supplies, and recognition activities. Stored in the organization\'s base currency.']

  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this volunteer team record was last updated in the system.']

  primary_language varchar [note: 'The primary language used for communication and coordination within the team and with beneficiaries (e.g., \'English\', \'French\', \'Arabic\', \'Swahili\').']
  recognition_awards_count varchar [note: 'Total number of recognition awards, certificates, or commendations received by the team for outstanding service.']
