  safety_incidents_count varchar [note: 'Total number of safety or security incidents involving team members during operations.']
  target_member_count varchar [note: 'The planned or target number of volunteers intended to be part of this team for optimal operational capacity.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code or identifier used for quick reference and operational communication (e.g., \'CHW-D5\', \'EDT-ALPHA\', \'PSS-RCB\').']
  team_name varchar [note: 'The official name or designation of the volunteer team (e.g., \'Community Health Worker Group - District 5\', \'Emergency Distribution Team Alpha\', \'PSS Support Group - Refugee Camp B\').']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the volunteer team based on its primary function and operational focus. PSS refers to Psychosocial Support, WASH refers to Water Sanitation and Hygiene. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: community_health_worker_group|distribution_team|pss_support_group|event_crew|wash_team|nutrition_outreach_team|education_support_team|protection_response_team|logistics_support_team|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_volunteer_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of volunteer hours contributed by all team members since team formation.']
  training_completion_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all team members are required to complete specific training or certification before joining the team.']
  volunteer_team_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the volunteer team in its lifecycle.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this team record.']

  Note: 'Defines volunteer teams or groups organized for specific programs, field operations, or community outreach activities, including team roster membership. Captures team name, team type (community health worker group, distribution team, PSS support group, event crew), program affiliation, geographic area of operation, team lead (volunteer or staff FK), formation and dissolution dates, current status, and member roster with individual roles, join/exit dates, and leadership responsibilities within the team. Enables group-level coordination, communication, shift planning, and historical team composition reporting.'
}

Table volunteer.team_membership {
  team_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the team_membership association']
  volunteer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the volunteer who is a member of the team']
  volunteer_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the volunteer team the volunteer is a member of']
  hours_contributed_to_team decimal [note: 'Cumulative number of hours this volunteer has contributed specifically to this team. Distinct from total volunteer hours, as a volunteer may contribute different hours to different teams. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  join_date date [note: 'The date on which the volunteer officially joined and became an active member of this team. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  leave_date date [note: 'The date on which the volunteer exited or was removed from this team. Null if the volunteer is still an active member. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this volunteer\'s membership in the team. Drives safeguarding compliance and roster accuracy. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  role_in_team varchar [note: 'The functional role the volunteer holds within this specific team, such as team lead, health worker, logistics support, or data collector. A volunteer may hold different roles in different teams. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Membership relationship between volunteer and volunteer_team. It captures the formal enrollment of a volunteer into a team, including their role, tenure dates, membership status, and hours contributed. Each record links one volunteer to one volunteer_team and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that specific membership — enabling accurate roster management, safeguarding compliance (knowing exactly who is or was on each team), and program-level reporting on volunteer contributions.. Existence Justification: In NGO volunteer management, a single volunteer can be a member of multiple teams simultaneously (e.g., a community health worker on both a distribution team and a PSS support group), and each volunteer team has many volunteer members. Team membership is a formally managed business concept with its own lifecycle — volunteers join teams, hold roles within them, contribute hours, and eventually leave — making this a genuine operational M:N relationship, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table partnership.partner_org {
  partner_org_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a partner organization record. This is the primary key and Single Source of Truth (SSOT) identifier for all inter-organizational partner entities in the partnership domain.']
  relationship_manager_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Partnership management best practice assigns NGO staff as relationship owner for each partner organization. Critical for due diligence oversight, capacity assessment coordination, performance monitori']
  statutory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.statutory_registration. Business justification: Partner organizations must maintain valid statutory registrations (NGO registration, tax-exempt status) for due diligence and partnership eligibility. Links partner vetting to legal compliance status.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the partner organization against recognized humanitarian or development standards such as the Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS), HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership), or equivalent national accreditation bodies.']
  annual_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Most recently reported annual organizational budget of the partner in US Dollars. Used for financial capacity assessment, sub-award sizing decisions, and due diligence under OMB Uniform Guidance. Sourced from partner financial disclosures or audited accounts.']
  authorized_rep_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual legally authorized to sign agreements (MoUs, LoAs, SoWs, sub-award agreements) on behalf of the partner organization. Required for agreement execution and legal compliance.']
  authorized_rep_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the authorized signatory at the partner organization (e.g., Executive Director, CEO, Country Representative). Validates signing authority for sub-award and partnership agreements.']
  bank_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the partner organization\'s primary bank account is held. Used for international wire transfer compliance and OFAC/sanctions screening.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary banking institution used by the partner organization for receiving sub-grant disbursements. Required for financial due diligence and sub-award payment processing in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP.']
  capacity_assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent formal partner capacity assessment was conducted. Used to determine whether a reassessment is due and to track the currency of due diligence records.']
  capacity_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (typically 0–100) resulting from the most recent formal partner capacity assessment covering governance, financial management, HR, and program delivery. Used to determine sub-award eligibility, required oversight level, and capacity-strengthening needs under OMB Uniform Guidance.']
  chs_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner organization holds a valid Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) certification. CHS certification demonstrates commitment to accountability, quality, and improvement in humanitarian response.']
  cluster_memberships varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of OCHA humanitarian cluster(s) in which the partner organization holds active membership or co-lead roles (e.g., WASH, Health, Shelter, Food Security, Protection). Used for coordination mapping and IASC working group engagement tracking.']
  countries_of_operation varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the partner organization has active field operations. Distinct from country of registration. Used for geographic coverage analysis and OCHA 3W (Who does What Where) reporting.']
  due_diligence_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current due diligence clearance expires and a new assessment must be completed before the partner can enter into new sub-award or partnership agreements.']
  due_diligence_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the partner organization\'s due diligence vetting process. \'Expired\' indicates that a previously completed assessment has passed its validity period and renewal is required before new agreements can be executed.']
  finance_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the partner organization\'s designated financial officer or finance focal point. Used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) reporting, financial documentation requests, and sub-grant financial management communications.']
  founding_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the partner organization was founded or established. Used to assess organizational maturity, track record, and eligibility for capacity-strengthening programs.']
  hq_address varchar [note: 'Full mailing address of the partner organization\'s global headquarters including street, city, postal code, and country. Used for official correspondence, legal notices, and sub-award agreement documentation.']
  hq_city varchar [note: 'City where the partner organization\'s global headquarters is located. Used for correspondence, coordination meetings, and geographic reporting.']
  hq_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the partner organization\'s global headquarters is located. Used for geographic analysis, donor reporting, and OCHA cluster coordination mapping.']
  iati_org_identifier varchar [note: 'Globally unique organization identifier assigned under the International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) standard (e.g., GB-CHC-1234567). Enables cross-organization data linkage and transparency reporting to IATI registry. Required for DFID and many EU donor compliance reports.']
  mission_statement varchar [note: 'Official mission statement of the partner organization describing its core purpose and mandate. Used for alignment assessment with Ngo\'s Theory of Change (ToC) and program design.']
  org_acronym varchar [note: 'Commonly used acronym or short name for the partner organization (e.g., CARE, IRC, MSF). Widely used in SitReps, cluster coordination, and OCHA reporting to identify partners concisely.']
  org_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the partner organization as it appears on official registration documents. Used as the primary human-readable identity label for the partner across all systems including Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud.']
  org_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the partner organization type. Distinguishes between International Non-Governmental Organizations (INGOs), Civil Society Organizations (CSOs), Community-Based Organizations (CBOs), UN agencies, government bodies, consortia, private sector entities, and academic institutions. Drives eligibility rules for sub-award agreements and due diligence requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: INGO|NGO|CSO|CBO|UN_AGENCY|GOVERNMENT|CONSORTIUM|PRIVATE_SECTOR|ACADEMIC — promote to reference product]']
  org_website varchar [note: 'Official public website URL of the partner organization. Used for due diligence verification, public profile validation, and IATI transparency reporting.']
  partnership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the partner organization relationship. Controls whether the partner is eligible for new agreements, sub-awards, or field collaboration. \'pending_vetting\' indicates due diligence is in progress; \'blacklisted\' indicates the partner is barred from engagement per donor or regulatory requirements.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code representing the partner organization\'s preferred language for official communications, reporting, and agreement documentation (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'ar\', \'es\').']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Official email address of the primary focal point at the partner organization. Used for formal communications, agreement notifications, and reporting requests.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary focal point or authorized representative at the partner organization. This is the principal contact for partnership coordination, agreement negotiations, and official communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number (including country code) for the primary focal point at the partner organization. Used for urgent field coordination and emergency communications.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or designation of the primary focal point at the partner organization (e.g., Country Director, Partnership Manager, Executive Director). Used to assess seniority and authority level for agreement signing.']
  record_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when the partner organization record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  record_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when the partner organization record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  sanctions_screen_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent sanctions screening was completed for the partner organization. Used to determine whether re-screening is required before new agreements or disbursements.']
  sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner organization has been screened against applicable sanctions lists (e.g., OFAC SDN List, UN Consolidated Sanctions List, EU Sanctions List) as part of the due diligence process. Required for USAID and US Government-funded programs.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of UN Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) numbers that the partner organization\'s mandate aligns with (e.g., \'1,2,3,6\'). Used for strategic alignment reporting and donor narrative reporting.']
  staff_count varchar [note: 'Total number of paid staff (national and international) employed by the partner organization at the time of last capacity assessment. Used as an indicator of organizational capacity for program delivery and sub-award management.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number (e.g., EIN in the US, VAT number in the EU) for the partner organization. Required for financial transactions, sub-award agreements, and compliance with NICRA (Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement) reporting.']
  thematic_focus_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of primary thematic areas in which the partner organization operates (e.g., WASH, GBV, PSS, Health, Education, Livelihoods, NFI Distribution). Used for partner matching in program design and cluster coordination with OCHA.']
  volunteer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active volunteers engaged by the partner organization. Reflects community mobilization capacity and is used in operational capacity assessments for field program delivery.']

  Note: 'Master record and SSOT for all inter-organizational partners including CSOs (Civil Society Organizations), CBOs (Community-Based Organizations), INGOs (International Non-Governmental Organizations), government bodies, UN agencies, and consortia. Captures organizational identity, registration details (registration number, country of registration, registration date), legal status, countries of operation, organizational type classification, accreditation status, and operational capacity summary. Includes embedded contact records for key personnel: focal points, authorized representatives, technical leads, financial officers, and program coordinators with name, title, role type, communication channels (email, phone, messaging), language preference, and active status. This is the SSOT for partner identity, classification, and contact information in the partnership domain.'
}

Table partnership.agreement {
  agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the partnership agreement record. Primary key for the partnership_agreement data product.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Partnership agreements in nonprofit operations typically implement specific grant awards. Tracking which donor award funds each partnership is essential for financial reporting, compliance monitoring,']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Partnership agreements create compliance obligations (donor reporting schedules, regulatory filings, IATI publication requirements). Each agreement generates specific obligations tracked in compliance']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Partnership agreements in nonprofits are funded through specific restricted or unrestricted funds. Financial reporting, fund accounting, and donor compliance require tracking which fund finances each ']
  grant_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_budget. Business justification: NGO finance teams link partnership agreements to grant budgets for sub-award burn-rate monitoring, donor compliance reporting, and budget vs. actual analysis. A domain expert expects every implementin']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: Partnership agreements are established to implement specific interventions. Linking agreement to intervention enables program-level financial consolidation, results aggregation, donor reporting, and i']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: CRITICAL missing link. Every partnership agreement must reference its partner organization. Currently stores partner_organization_name as denormalized text. Adding partner_org_id FK allows JOIN to par']
  program_manager_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Partnership agreements require designated NGO program manager for oversight, amendment approvals, performance review sign-off, and donor reporting accountability. Distinct from partner_org relationshi']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the partnership agreement. draft = under preparation; pending_signature = awaiting execution; active = fully executed and in force; suspended = temporarily halted; expired = past end date without renewal; terminated = ended before expiry; amended = superseded by an amendment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_signature|active|suspended|expired|terminated|amended — promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formal inter-organizational legal instrument. MoU = Memorandum of Understanding; LoA = Letter of Agreement; SoW = Scope of Work; sub_award = sub-grant or sub-award agreement; consortium = multi-party consortium agreement; other = any other instrument type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MoU|LoA|SoW|sub_award|consortium|framework|other — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent amendment to the agreement was executed. Null for original agreements with no amendments.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the changes introduced by the most recent amendment (e.g., budget increase, no-cost extension, scope modification). Null for original agreements.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer indicating the amendment version of the agreement. Zero (0) indicates the original agreement; increments by 1 for each subsequent amendment. Supports amendment history tracking.']
  capacity_assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent partner capacity assessment or due diligence review was completed.']
  capacity_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the partner due diligence and capacity assessment conducted prior to or during the agreement. not_required = exempt; pending = assessment initiated but not complete; completed = assessment passed; failed = partner did not meet threshold; waived = formally waived by authorized approver.']
  cluster_lead_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the OCHA cluster lead agency (e.g., UNICEF for WASH, WFP for Food Security) under whose coordination framework the agreement\'s activities are implemented. Relevant for humanitarian response agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the partnership agreement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the funding ceiling and financial obligations under the agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'Agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising under the agreement. Captured from the legal instrument\'s dispute resolution clause.']
  due_diligence_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the partner following due diligence review. Informs monitoring intensity, financial controls, and reporting requirements under the agreement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the agreement expires or obligations cease. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Corresponds to the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the agreement becomes legally binding and operational obligations commence. Corresponds to the EFFECTIVE_FROM category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which the agreement was formally signed and executed by all required parties. May differ from the effective start date if the agreement is backdated or has a future commencement.']
  funding_ceiling_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total financial value authorized under the agreement in the agreement\'s stated currency. For sub-awards, this is the maximum sub-grant amount. Null for non-financial MoUs.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the geographic area covered by the agreement (e.g., \'Borno and Yobe States, Nigeria\' or \'Kabul Province, Afghanistan\'). Used for operational planning and GIS-based reporting.']
  governing_law_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement (e.g., GBR, USA, CHE).']
  iati_activity_identifier varchar [note: 'IATI-standard activity identifier for the agreement, enabling public disclosure and cross-organization traceability on the IATI Registry (e.g., \'GB-CHC-123456-WASH-NGA-2024\').']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate (NICRA) or agreed Facilities and Administration (F&A) rate applied to direct costs under this agreement, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Relevant for US federal sub-awards under OMB Uniform Guidance.']
  is_consortium_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement governs a multi-party consortium arrangement (True) rather than a bilateral agreement (False). Consortium agreements may have additional members tracked separately.']
  is_sub_award boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement is a sub-award (True) or a prime/direct agreement (False). Drives compliance requirements under OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 for pass-through entities.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the partnership agreement record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  lead_organization_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the primary organization (typically the prime recipient or lead INGO) that is the principal party initiating or managing the agreement. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE anchor for the agreement.']
  operational_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country where the agreement\'s activities are implemented (e.g., SSD, YEM, HTI).']
  parent_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Agreement reference number of the parent or prime agreement under which this sub-award or amendment was issued. Enables hierarchical agreement traceability. Null for top-level agreements.']
  program_sector varchar [note: 'Humanitarian or development sector under which the agreement\'s activities fall. WASH = Water Sanitation and Hygiene; GBV = Gender-Based Violence; PSS = Psychosocial Support. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WASH|health|education|food_security|protection|shelter|livelihoods|GBV|PSS|nutrition|other — promote to reference product]']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the agreement, used in correspondence, filing, and cross-system lookups (e.g., MOU-2024-0042, LOA-2023-WASH-007). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this agreement.']
  renewal_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement contains a renewal or extension option clause (True) that allows the parties to extend the agreement beyond the effective end date without a full renegotiation.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Agreed frequency at which the partner must submit programmatic and/or financial reports under the agreement (e.g., monthly SitRep, quarterly narrative, annual audit).']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the agreed scope of work, activities, and deliverables covered by the agreement. Summarizes the SoW or program objectives as stated in the legal instrument.']
  sdg_alignment_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of UN Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) goal numbers to which the agreement\'s activities contribute (e.g., \'3,6,16\'). Used for donor reporting and IATI disclosure.']
  signatory_name_lead varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized signatory representing the lead organization on the agreement. Captured for legal validity and audit purposes.']
  signatory_name_partner varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized signatory representing the partner organization on the agreement. Captured for legal validity and audit purposes.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the agreement was terminated, if terminated before the effective end date. Null for active or expired agreements.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days\' written notice required by either party to terminate the agreement without cause, as specified in the termination clause.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early termination of the agreement. Null for agreements that expire naturally or remain active.']
  theory_of_change_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or title of the Theory of Change (ToC) or LogFrame (Logical Framework) document that underpins the program activities governed by this agreement. Links the agreement to the MEL framework.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the partnership agreement as it appears on the signed legal instrument (e.g., \'Memorandum of Understanding for WASH Program Delivery in Borno State\').']

  Note: 'Core master record for all formal inter-organizational agreements including MoUs (Memoranda of Understanding), LoAs (Letters of Agreement), SoWs (Scope of Work), sub-award agreements, and consortium agreements. Captures agreement type, effective dates, expiry dates, signatory parties, governing law, agreement scope, funding ceiling, current status, and amendment history references. Includes embedded obligation line-items tracking specific deliverables and commitments: obligation description, responsible party, due date, completion status, evidence of fulfillment, and escalation status. Serves as the SSOT for all partnership legal instruments, their tracked commitments, and obligation monitoring in the partnership domain.'
}

Table partnership.capacity_assessment {
  capacity_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each partner capacity assessment record. Primary key for the capacity_assessment data product.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Capacity assessments are frequently conducted as a prerequisite or condition of a specific partnership agreement (e.g., required before a sub-award is executed). Linking capacity_assessment to the spe']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or funding agreement that triggered or is associated with this capacity assessment. Links due diligence records to specific funding instruments.']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donors frequently mandate partner capacity assessments as a specific donor requirement before disbursement. Linking the assessment to the triggering donor_requirement enables compliance officers to ve']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization (CSO, CBO, INGO, government body, UN agency, or consortium) being assessed. Links to the partner master record.']
  primary_capacity_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or consultant who led the capacity assessment process. Used for accountability, quality assurance, and audit trail purposes.']
  prior_assessment_capacity_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding capacity assessment record for the same partner, enabling longitudinal tracking of capacity development progress over time.']
  subaward_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.subaward. Business justification: Capacity assessments directly inform subaward risk ratings and payment modality decisions. This FK links the assessment to the specific subaward it informed, enabling subaward monitoring teams to retr']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The principal date on which the capacity assessment was conducted or the field data collection was completed. Used as the primary business event date for reporting and due diligence timelines.']
  assessment_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the capacity assessment process was formally concluded and the final report was issued.']
  assessment_location_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the capacity assessment was physically conducted. Relevant for field-based assessments of local partners.']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'Standardized framework or tool used to conduct the capacity assessment. Common methodologies include USAID Organizational Capacity Assessment (OCA), DFID Organizational Capacity Assessment Tool (OCAT), ECHO Partner Assessment for Capacity Assessment (PACA), IRC Collaborative Capacity Assessment Tool (CCAT), and UNDP Capacity Scorecard. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USAID_OCA|DFID_OCAT|ECHO_PACA|IRC_CCAT|UNDP_CAPACITY_SCORECARD|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this capacity assessment, used in correspondence, donor reporting, and audit trails (e.g., NGO-CA-2024-000123).']
  assessment_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or document management system path to the full capacity assessment report. Classified as confidential due to sensitive organizational information about the partner.']
  assessment_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates the depth and modality of the assessment: FULL (comprehensive on-site assessment covering all functional areas), ABBREVIATED (targeted review of selected areas), DESK_REVIEW (document-based review without site visit), FIELD_VISIT (on-site verification component).']
  assessment_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the capacity assessment process formally commenced, including document review and stakeholder engagement initiation.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the capacity assessment record: DRAFT (initiated but not submitted), IN_PROGRESS (assessment underway), COMPLETED (assessment finalized), VALIDATED (reviewed and approved by authorized staff), ARCHIVED (superseded or closed).']
  assessment_tool_version varchar [note: 'Version number or edition of the assessment methodology tool used (e.g., \'USAID OCA v3.0\', \'DFID OCAT 2019\'). Ensures comparability of scores across assessments conducted using different tool versions.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of when and why the assessment was conducted: PRE_AWARD (due diligence before partnership), PERIODIC (scheduled review during partnership), TRIGGERED (event-driven reassessment due to risk flag), or CLOSE_OUT (end-of-partnership evaluation).']
  capacity_building_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment results mandate the development of a formal capacity-building plan (True) or whether the partner\'s capacity is sufficient without a structured intervention plan (False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when the capacity assessment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting purposes.']
  enhanced_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment outcome requires enhanced programmatic or financial monitoring of the partner (True), such as more frequent reporting, additional site visits, or pre-approval of expenditures, as mandated by donor regulations.']
  financial_mgmt_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score assigned to the partner\'s financial management functional area, covering accounting systems, internal controls, audit compliance, and fund stewardship. Typically scored on a 0–100 or 1–5 scale per the assessment methodology.']
  financial_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Specific risk rating for the partner\'s financial management functional area, used to determine sub-award monitoring intensity, payment modality, and required financial controls.']
  governance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score for the partner\'s organizational governance functional area, including board oversight, legal registration, organizational policies, and leadership accountability.']
  hr_mgmt_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score for the partner\'s human resources management functional area, including HR policies, recruitment practices, staff development, and safeguarding compliance.']
  it_systems_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score for the partner\'s ICT4D and information systems functional area, covering data management infrastructure, cybersecurity practices, and digital reporting capabilities.']
  key_findings_summary varchar [note: 'Concise narrative summary of the principal findings from the capacity assessment, highlighting major strengths, critical gaps, and priority areas for improvement across all assessed functional areas.']
  kobo_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Unique submission identifier from KoboToolbox or similar field data collection platform used to capture assessment data in the field. Enables traceability back to the source data collection record.']
  lead_assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who led the capacity assessment. Retained for reporting and audit purposes even when the staff record may change.']
  lead_assessor_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization to which the lead assessor belongs (e.g., the NGO\'s headquarters, an independent consulting firm, or a donor agency). Relevant when external assessors are engaged.']
  mel_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score for the partner\'s Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) functional area, covering data collection systems, indicator tracking, reporting quality, and learning practices.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the next capacity assessment must be completed, based on risk rating, donor requirements, or organizational policy. Drives compliance monitoring and workflow triggers.']
  overall_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Categorical risk classification assigned to the partner based on the capacity assessment results: LOW (strong capacity, minimal oversight needed), MEDIUM (adequate capacity with some gaps), HIGH (significant capacity gaps requiring intensive support), CRITICAL (fundamental weaknesses posing fiduciary or programmatic risk).']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric score representing the partner\'s overall organizational capacity across all assessed functional areas. Calculated per the assessment methodology\'s weighting scheme and recorded as the official assessment result.']
  partner_self_assessment_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner organization completed a self-assessment component prior to or alongside the external assessment (True). Many OCA methodologies include a parallel self-assessment to identify perception gaps.']
  partner_self_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall score submitted by the partner organization in their self-assessment component. Compared against the external assessor\'s score to identify perception gaps and inform capacity-building dialogue.']
  payment_modality_recommendation varchar [note: 'Payment modality recommended for sub-award disbursements based on the partner\'s financial risk rating: ADVANCE (funds transferred before expenditure), REIMBURSEMENT (funds transferred after expenditure documentation), DIRECT_PAYMENT (NGO pays vendors directly on partner\'s behalf), MIXED (combination of modalities).']
  procurement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score for the partner\'s procurement and supply chain management functional area, covering procurement policies, competitive bidding, vendor management, and asset control.']
  program_mgmt_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score for the partner\'s program design and management functional area, including project planning, implementation capacity, beneficiary targeting, and service delivery quality.']
  recommended_interventions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific capacity-building interventions recommended based on assessment findings, such as financial management training, procurement system upgrades, MEL system strengthening, or governance policy development.']
  score_scale_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum possible score on the scoring scale used by the assessment methodology (e.g., 100 for percentage-based scales, 5 for Likert-based scales). Required to normalize scores across different methodologies for comparative analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when the capacity assessment record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail purposes.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the capacity assessment was formally reviewed and validated by the authorized staff member, marking the record as officially approved for use in partnership and sub-award decisions.']

  Note: 'Records formal partner capacity assessments conducted prior to or during partnership engagement. Captures assessment methodology (e.g., USAID OCA, DFID OCAT), assessment date, assessor identity, scores across functional areas (financial management, HR, procurement, governance, MEL), overall risk rating, and recommended capacity-building interventions. Essential for due diligence and risk management.'
}

Table partnership.due_diligence_record {
  due_diligence_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the due diligence and partner verification record. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — this record represents a formal compliance verification container with its own lifecycle, effective dates, and binding outcome.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Due diligence is conducted in the context of a specific partnership agreement — it is triggered when a new agreement is being established or renewed. Currently due_diligence_record links to partner_or']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Due diligence assessments are often triggered by specific award risk thresholds, donor requirements, and subaward value ceilings. Award context determines diligence scope, required certifications, and']
  capacity_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.capacity_assessment. Business justification: due_diligence_record stores capacity_assessment_score and capacity_assessment_date as denormalized attributes but has no FK to the actual capacity_assessment record. Due diligence incorporates capacit']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Due diligence records are frequently initiated to satisfy specific donor requirements (e.g., USAID mandatory AML/CFT checks, EU partner vetting requirements before sub-award). Linking due_diligence_re']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization (CSO, CBO, INGO, government body, UN agency, or consortium) that is the subject of this due diligence record.']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Due diligence records must reference specific sanctions screening results for anti-terrorism compliance and donor requirements. Mandatory step in partner vetting. Links DD workflow to compliance evide']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member (typically a Partnership Officer or Compliance Officer) responsible for conducting and owning this due diligence record.']
  subaward_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.subaward. Business justification: USAID, EU, and UN donors require documented pre-award due diligence before subaward execution. This FK links the due diligence clearance to the specific subaward it authorized — enabling compliance of']
  aml_cft_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter-Financing of Terrorism (CFT) due diligence checks conducted on the partner organization and its key principals, per FATF Recommendations and donor requirements.']
  anti_terrorism_cert_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner\'s anti-terrorism certification was signed and received. Required for USAID and BHA-funded sub-awards.']
  anti_terrorism_certification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner has signed and submitted a valid anti-terrorism certification (e.g., USAID Standard Provision — Certification Regarding Terrorist Financing) as required for US Government-funded awards.']
  chs_cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner\'s CHS certification expires. Triggers renewal alerts and may affect partner eligibility for humanitarian sub-awards.']

  completion_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which all due diligence checks were completed and a final outcome was recorded. Null if the process is still in progress.']
  conditions_of_approval varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any conditions, restrictions, or corrective actions attached to an approved-with-conditions outcome. Documents required remediation steps and timelines before full approval is granted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this due diligence record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for compliance and data lineage tracking.']
  debarment_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent debarment and suspension check was performed against SAM.gov and equivalent exclusion registries.']
  debarment_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of verification against the US System for Award Management (SAM.gov) exclusions list and equivalent debarment/suspension registries to confirm the partner is not excluded from receiving federal awards.']
  diligence_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the due diligence record, tracking progress from initiation through completion or failure. Drives workflow routing and compliance dashboards.']
  diligence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the due diligence exercise by its trigger and scope. Pre-award is conducted before sub-award execution; periodic review is scheduled; triggered review is initiated by a risk event; close-out is conducted at agreement end; enhanced due diligence applies to high-risk partners.']
  financial_audit_opinion varchar [note: 'Type of auditor\'s opinion issued in the partner\'s most recent external financial audit. An unqualified (clean) opinion is required for most donor sub-award eligibility. Adverse or disclaimer opinions trigger enhanced due diligence.']
  financial_audit_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner\'s most recent external financial audit report has been reviewed and assessed as part of this due diligence exercise.']
  financial_audit_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year covered by the most recent external financial audit report reviewed during due diligence. Used to assess currency of financial information.']
  governance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner\'s governance structure (board composition, conflict of interest policies, organizational chart, and decision-making authority) has been reviewed and verified.']
  hap_membership_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the partner\'s membership status with the Humanitarian Accountability Partnership (HAP), a predecessor accountability framework now integrated into the CHS Alliance.']
  initiation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the due diligence process was formally initiated for this partner. Marks the start of the MASTER_AGREEMENT effective period for compliance tracking purposes.']
  iso_cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner\'s ISO certification expires. Null if no ISO certification is held or if certification is not time-limited.']
  iso_certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of ISO certification held by the partner organization (e.g., ISO 9001 Quality Management, ISO 27001 Information Security). Null if no ISO certification is held. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISO_9001|ISO_27001|ISO_14001|ISO_45001|ISO_31000|none — promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this due diligence record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for compliance reporting.']
  legal_registration_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner\'s legal registration and incorporation status in its country of operation has been verified against official national NGO registration records or company registry.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled calendar date for the next periodic due diligence review of this partner. Drives automated reminders and compliance calendar management. Effective-until equivalent for the current verification cycle.']
  ngo_registration_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the partner is legally registered as an NGO or nonprofit entity.']
  ngo_registration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner\'s national NGO registration expires and must be renewed. Null if registration is perpetual. Triggers compliance alerts when approaching expiry.']
  ngo_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number issued by the national NGO regulatory authority or charity commission in the partner\'s country of primary operation. Key identifier for legal compliance verification.']
  overall_outcome varchar [note: 'Final determination of the due diligence exercise indicating whether the partner is cleared for sub-award or partnership engagement. Approved with conditions requires a documented corrective action plan.']
  record_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric reference code assigned to this due diligence record, used in correspondence, audit trails, and donor reporting. Format: DDR-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  reviewer_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential narrative notes recorded by the responsible staff member summarizing key findings, concerns, and recommendations from the due diligence review. Restricted to compliance and partnership leadership.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Composite risk rating assigned to the partner following due diligence, reflecting financial, governance, compliance, and operational risk factors. Determines monitoring intensity and sub-award conditions.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric composite risk score (typically 0–100) calculated from weighted assessment criteria across financial, governance, legal, and operational dimensions. Supports quantitative risk ranking across the partner portfolio.']
  safeguarding_policy_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner has a documented and operational safeguarding policy covering protection from sexual exploitation, abuse, and harassment (PSEAH), child protection, and GBV prevention, as required by IASC and donor standards.']
  sphere_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of the partner\'s operational compliance with the Sphere Humanitarian Charter and Minimum Standards in Humanitarian Response, covering WASH, food security, shelter, and health sectors.']
  supporting_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL pointing to the document management system location where supporting due diligence evidence (audit reports, registration certificates, signed certifications) is stored.']

  Note: 'Formal due diligence and partner verification master record covering legal compliance checks, sanctions screening (OFAC, EU, UN), anti-terrorism certification, financial audit review, governance verification, and regulatory standing. Includes embedded accreditation/certification tracking for CHS (Core Humanitarian Standard), Sphere compliance, HAP membership, ISO certifications, and national NGO registration status with issuing body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, and verification status. Tracks due diligence type, completion date, outcome, risk level, next review date, and responsible staff. Required for compliance with 2 CFR 200, USAID ADS 303, and donor partner vetting regulations. This is the SSOT for all partner verification, screening, and accreditation data in the partnership domain.'
}

Table partnership.partner_contact {
  partner_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the partner contact record. Primary key for the partner_contact data product within the partnership domain.']
  focal_point_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: NGO partnership teams assign individual staff as day-to-day focal points for specific partner contacts (distinct from the org-level relationship manager). This supports contact management, meeting sch']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization to which this contact belongs. Links the individual contact to their parent Civil Society Organization (CSO), Community-Based Organization (CBO), International Non-Governmental Organization (INGO), UN agency, government body, or consortium entity.']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Individual partner contacts — especially authorized signatories and financially authorized contacts — must be individually screened against sanctions lists (OFAC, UN, EU). The existing sanctions_scree']
  cluster_membership varchar [note: 'Name of the OCHA humanitarian cluster(s) or IASC working group(s) in which this contact participates as a representative or lead (e.g., WASH Cluster, Health Cluster, Protection Cluster, Food Security Cluster). Supports inter-agency coordination tracking.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner contact provided or last renewed their data sharing consent. Used to track consent validity periods and trigger re-consent workflows in compliance with GDPR and organizational data protection policies.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the partner contact record. Indicates whether the contact is actively engaged in partnership coordination, temporarily unavailable, or no longer with the organization.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the partner contact is currently based or assigned. Used for geographic segmentation of partner networks and country-level coordination reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when the partner contact record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the record\'s lifecycle in compliance with OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 record retention requirements.']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner contact has provided explicit consent for their personal data to be shared with third parties, donor agencies, or inter-agency coordination bodies. Required for compliance with GDPR, data protection regulations, and the Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) accountability principles.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or unit within the partner organization where the contact is based (e.g., Finance, Programs, Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL), Operations). Supports routing of communications to the correct functional area.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates that the partner contact has requested to not be contacted, or has been flagged by the organization as ineligible for outreach. Overrides all communication workflows and must be respected in all outreach campaigns.']
  duty_station varchar [note: 'Specific city, town, or field location where the partner contact is stationed. Used in humanitarian operations to identify field presence, coordinate cluster meetings, and assign contacts to geographic response zones.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner contact\'s engagement formally ended, such as upon departure from the organization or role change. Null for currently active contacts. Used to maintain historical records and audit trails.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the partner contact individual. Used for personalized communication and formal correspondence across the partnership lifecycle.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self-identified gender of the partner contact. Used for gender disaggregation in partnership reporting, Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 5 compliance tracking, and gender-balanced stakeholder engagement analysis.']
  is_authorized_signatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner contact is an authorized legal signatory for agreements such as Memoranda of Understanding (MoU), Letters of Agreement (LoA), sub-award agreements, and Scope of Work (SoW) documents. Critical for compliance with OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 and donor requirements.']
  is_financial_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner contact holds financial authorization authority, such as approving sub-grant disbursements, signing financial reports, or authorizing Budget versus Actual (BvA) submissions. Supports financial stewardship and fund accounting controls.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this individual is the designated primary focal point for the partner organization. Only one contact per organization should be flagged as primary. Drives automated routing of official communications and partnership notifications.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or designation of the partner contact within their organization (e.g., Country Director, Finance Manager, Program Coordinator). Used in formal correspondence and stakeholder mapping.']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent recorded interaction or communication with the partner contact. Used to identify dormant relationships, prioritize re-engagement, and support relationship management workflows in the Constituent Relationship Management (CRM) system.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the partner contact individual. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for reporting and relationship management.']
  linkedin_profile varchar [note: 'LinkedIn professional profile URL for the partner contact. Used for prospect research, due diligence verification, and professional background validation during partner capacity assessments.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or second given name of the partner contact, used in formal legal correspondence and official documentation such as Memoranda of Understanding (MoU) and Letters of Agreement (LoA).']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular phone number for the partner contact in E.164 international format. Critical for field operations communication, WhatsApp coordination, and emergency contact in humanitarian contexts.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the nationality of the partner contact. Used for visa and travel coordination, national staff vs. international staff classification, and compliance with local labor regulations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, relationship history, or coordination notes about the partner contact. May include background on the contact\'s expertise, past collaboration history, or special communication instructions.']
  office_address varchar [note: 'Physical office address of the partner contact\'s workplace. Used for formal correspondence, field visit coordination, and partner mapping in Geographic Information System (GIS) tools.']
  office_phone varchar [note: 'Direct office or landline telephone number for the partner contact, including extension where applicable. Used for formal business communications and official coordination calls.']
  preferred_contact_channel varchar [note: 'The partner contact\'s preferred communication channel for receiving outreach and coordination messages. Supports effective stakeholder engagement and respects communication preferences in diverse field contexts.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-3 language code representing the partner contact\'s preferred language for communications and documentation (e.g., \'en\' for English, \'fr\' for French, \'ar\' for Arabic, \'es\' for Spanish). Supports multilingual coordination in international humanitarian operations.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary professional email address for the partner contact. Used as the main digital communication channel for coordination, reporting, and official correspondence including Memoranda of Understanding (MoU) and Scope of Work (SoW) exchanges.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the partner contact in E.164 international format. Used for urgent coordination, field communications, and stakeholder engagement across the partnership lifecycle.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Functional role classification of the contact within the partnership relationship. Determines the contact\'s authority and responsibilities in coordination workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: focal_point|authorized_representative|technical_lead|financial_officer|program_manager|legal_signatory|cluster_coordinator|field_officer — promote to reference product]']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Honorific or title prefix used when addressing the partner contact in formal communications (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr., Prof.).']
  skype_handle varchar [note: 'Skype username or identifier for the partner contact. Used for virtual coordination meetings, remote field communications, and inter-agency conference calls in areas with limited connectivity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this partner contact record originated (e.g., Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud, Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits, Raisers Edge NXT). Supports data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation.']
  source_system_contact_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned to this contact record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Salesforce Contact ID, Dynamics 365 Contact GUID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the lakehouse Silver layer.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner contact was first formally engaged or registered in the partnership management system. Marks the beginning of the contact\'s active role in the partnership lifecycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner contact record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner contact\'s identity and credentials were last verified as part of the partner due diligence process. Used to schedule re-verification cycles and maintain compliance audit trails.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the identity and credential verification process for the partner contact. Supports due diligence requirements for sub-award management and partner capacity assessments per USAID, DFID, and OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual contacts within partner organizations including focal points, authorized representatives, technical leads, and financial officers. Captures name, title, role type, communication channels, language preference, and active status. Supports relationship management and coordination workflows across the partnership lifecycle.'
}

Table partnership.agreement_amendment {
  agreement_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each formal modification record to a partnership agreement. Serves as the primary key for the agreement_amendment entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent partnership agreement (MoU, LoA, SoW, or sub-award) that this amendment modifies. Links the amendment to its originating agreement record.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Agreement amendments modify specific budget line amounts (budget_change_amount, revised_budget_amount). NGO grant managers must update budget lines when amendments are executed, and donors require pri']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.donor_fund. Business justification: Agreement amendments affecting budget or scope require donor prior approval when funded by restricted donor funds. The existing donor_approval_reference field signals this process. NGO grants managers']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Agreement amendments requiring donor prior approval are driven by specific donor requirements. agreement_amendment already has donor_prior_approval_required and donor_approval_reference as plain attri']
  grant_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.grant_amendment. Business justification: When a donor issues a grant amendment, NGOs must cascade changes into partnership agreement amendments. This FK links the triggering grant amendment to the resulting agreement amendment — a mandatory ']
  primary_agreement_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member record of the individual who formally approved this amendment. Links to the workforce domain for accountability and audit trail purposes.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known sequential identifier for this amendment within the context of the parent agreement (e.g., AMD-2024-001). Used in official correspondence, donor reporting, and compliance filings. Supports full amendment history tracking per agreement.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the amendment record. Draft indicates preparation in progress; pending_approval indicates submission to approval authority; approved indicates fully executed; rejected indicates declined by approver; superseded indicates replaced by a subsequent amendment; withdrawn indicates retracted before approval.']
  amendment_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the purpose of the amendment (e.g., \'No-Cost Extension to December 2025\', \'Budget Realignment Q3 2024\'). Used in correspondence, dashboards, and partner communications.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formal modification being made to the agreement. Scope change alters deliverables or geographic coverage; budget realignment redistributes funds across cost categories; no-cost extension extends the period without additional funding; period of performance adjusts start/end dates; signatory change updates authorized representatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scope_change|budget_realignment|no_cost_extension|period_of_performance|signatory_change|other — promote to reference product]']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the individual, committee, or organizational body with delegated authority to approve this amendment (e.g., Country Director, Grants Manager, Donor Agreement Officer). Supports RACI accountability tracking.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment was formally approved by the designated approval authority. Null if not yet approved or if rejected/withdrawn.']
  beneficiary_target_change boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment modifies the target beneficiary numbers, population groups, or vulnerability criteria for the program. True = beneficiary targets modified; False = no change. Relevant for MEL/MEAL reporting and SDG indicator tracking.']
  budget_change_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net monetary change to the agreement budget introduced by this amendment (revised_budget_amount minus original_budget_amount). Positive values indicate budget increases; negative values indicate reductions. Null for non-financial amendments.']
  clauses_modified varchar [note: 'Enumeration or description of the specific agreement clauses, articles, or sections that are modified by this amendment (e.g., \'Article 3 — Scope of Work, Article 7 — Budget\'). Supports legal review and compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this amendment record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with record-keeping requirements under 2 CFR 200 and FASB ASC 958.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts in this amendment record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency partnership portfolios.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL pointing to the executed amendment document (signed PDF or equivalent). Supports audit trail and compliance evidence requirements.']
  donor_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number, letter identifier, or correspondence tracking code for the donor\'s written prior approval of this amendment. Required for audit evidence when donor_prior_approval_required is True.']
  donor_prior_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires prior written approval from the donor or prime recipient before implementation, as mandated under 2 CFR 200.308 or specific award terms. True = prior approval required; False = notification only or no approval needed.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment becomes legally binding and operationally effective. This is the real-world event date, distinct from the signature date or record creation timestamp. Critical for period-of-performance tracking and financial reporting.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all required signatories formally signed and executed the amendment document. May differ from the effective date when retroactive amendments are issued.']
  extension_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the period of performance is extended by this amendment. Null for non-extension amendments. Used for program planning, resource allocation, and donor reporting on timeline changes.']
  geographic_scope_change boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment modifies the geographic coverage or operational areas of the agreement (e.g., adding or removing districts, countries, or OCHA cluster areas). True = geographic scope modified; False = no geographic change.']
  gms_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this amendment as recorded in the Grant Management System (GMS) or donor\'s award management platform (e.g., USAID ASIST, DFID ARIES). Enables cross-system reconciliation and donor audit support.']
  iati_activity_identifier varchar [note: 'IATI-standard activity identifier associated with the parent agreement, used to publish amendment data to the IATI Registry for transparency and open data compliance. Format follows IATI hierarchical identifier standard.']
  is_no_cost_extension boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment constitutes a no-cost extension (NCE), extending the period of performance without additional funding. True = NCE; False = other amendment type. Critical for grant closeout planning and donor reporting.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the business rationale for the amendment, including the circumstances that necessitated the change (e.g., context changes, operational constraints, donor-directed modifications). Required for donor reporting and audit compliance under 2 CFR 200.']
  ngo_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized representative of Ngo who signs this amendment on behalf of the organization. Required for legal enforceability and signatory change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, internal commentary, or operational notes related to this amendment that do not fit structured fields. Used by grants management and partnership teams for internal coordination.']
  original_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total authorized budget of the agreement prior to this amendment, expressed in the agreement currency. Retained for Budget versus Actual (BvA) analysis and audit trail of financial modifications.']
  original_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period of performance as defined in the agreement prior to this amendment. Used to calculate extension duration and track no-cost extension history.']
  original_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period of performance as defined in the agreement prior to this amendment. Retained for audit trail and comparison purposes when a period-of-performance amendment is processed.']
  partner_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized representative of the partner organization who signs this amendment on behalf of the CSO, CBO, INGO, government body, or UN agency. Required for signatory change amendments and legal enforceability.']
  revised_beneficiary_target varchar [note: 'Updated total number of direct beneficiaries targeted under the agreement following this amendment. Null if beneficiary_target_change is False. Used for MEL/MEAL indicator tracking and donor results reporting.']
  revised_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total authorized budget of the agreement as updated by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the budget. Used for BvA reporting and donor financial compliance.']
  revised_end_date date [note: 'The updated end date of the period of performance as established by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the end date. Key field for no-cost extension tracking.']
  revised_start_date date [note: 'The updated start date of the period of performance as established by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the start date.']
  scope_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing changes to the Scope of Work (SoW), program activities, geographic coverage, target beneficiary populations, or deliverables introduced by this amendment. Null if amendment type is not scope_change.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment request was formally submitted to the approval authority or donor/prime for review. Used to track processing timelines and compliance with advance notification requirements under 2 CFR 200.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this amendment record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing formal modifications to existing partnership agreements including scope changes, budget realignments, no-cost extensions, period of performance adjustments, and signatory changes. Tracks amendment number, amendment type, effective date, justification, approval authority, and the specific clauses modified. Maintains the full amendment history of each agreement.'
}

Table partnership.partner_performance_review {
  partner_performance_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each partner performance review record. Primary key for the partner_performance_review data product.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing agreement (MoU, LoA, SoW, or sub-award) under which this performance review is conducted.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Partner performance reviews assess budget_utilisation_rate as a core financial performance indicator. NGO program managers need the linked budget record to compute accurate utilization scores and prod']
  capacity_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.capacity_assessment. Business justification: A partner performance review should reference the capacity assessment that was in effect during the review period, providing the capability baseline against which performance is evaluated. This is a d']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donors routinely require periodic partner performance reviews as a named condition of grant agreements. Linking partner_performance_review to the specific donor_requirement it satisfies enables compli']
  implementation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project that the implementing partner is delivering under review.']
  internal_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.internal_review. Business justification: Partner performance reviews may trigger or reference internal compliance reviews when issues are identified. Links performance monitoring to compliance oversight. Essential for integrated risk managem']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the implementing partner organisation being reviewed. Links to the partner master record in the partnership domain.']
  primary_partner_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or team lead who conducted and is accountable for this performance review.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the performance review was formally approved by the authorised reviewer or partnership manager.']
  areas_for_improvement varchar [note: 'Narrative description of performance gaps, weaknesses, or risks identified during the review that require corrective action or capacity strengthening.']
  beneficiary_feedback_incorporated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether beneficiary feedback (e.g., from Post-Distribution Monitoring (PDM), Focus Group Discussions (FGD), or KoboToolbox surveys) was incorporated into the review findings.']
  budget_utilisation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the sub-award budget expended by the partner during the review period (0.00–100.00). Key Budget versus Actual (BvA) indicator for financial stewardship assessment.']
  capacity_building_areas varchar [note: 'Narrative description of specific capacity building areas recommended for the partner (e.g., financial management, MEL systems, safeguarding, ICT4D). Populated when capacity_building_recommended is True.']
  capacity_building_recommended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reviewer recommends a formal capacity building or organisational development intervention for the partner based on identified gaps.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of required corrective actions, including specific steps the partner must take to address identified deficiencies. Populated when corrective_action_required is True.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the partner must demonstrate completion of required corrective actions. Null when corrective_action_required is False.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Corrective Action Plan (CAP) is required as an outcome of this review. True triggers a CAP workflow in the partnership management system.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action plan associated with this review. Tracks partner remediation progress for risk management and compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance review record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  field_visit_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical field visit to the partner\'s implementation site was conducted as part of this review. Required for high-risk partners per OMB Uniform Guidance subrecipient monitoring standards.']
  financial_accountability_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s financial management practices including Budget versus Actual (BvA) variance, expenditure documentation, and compliance with Indirect Cost Rate (ICR) / NICRA requirements.']
  key_findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the principal findings from the performance review covering programmatic, financial, and compliance dimensions. Informs partnership renewal and risk management decisions.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance review record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  milestone_achievement_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of agreed SoW milestones achieved within the review period (0.00–100.00). Key Results-Based Management (RBM) indicator for partner performance tracking.']
  milestones_achieved_count varchar [note: 'Number of SoW milestones fully achieved within the review period. Numerator for milestone achievement rate calculation.']
