  milestones_planned_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SoW milestones scheduled for delivery within the review period. Denominator for milestone achievement rate calculation.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next performance review of this partner under the current agreement. Determined by risk level and agreement terms.']
  overall_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Composite performance rating assigned to the partner for the review period. Drives partnership renewal, risk classification, and sub-award decisions. Aligned with DAC evaluation criteria.']
  overall_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric composite score (e.g., 0.00–100.00) aggregating all scored dimensions of the review. Enables quantitative ranking and trend analysis across partners.']
  partner_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the implementing partner formally acknowledged the review findings. Null until acknowledgement is received.']
  partner_acknowledgement_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the implementing partner has formally acknowledged receipt of the review findings and agreed corrective actions. Required for CHS accountability compliance.']
  partnership_renewal_recommendation varchar [note: 'Reviewer\'s recommendation on whether to renew, conditionally renew, or terminate the partnership agreement based on this review\'s findings. Feeds into partnership pipeline decisions.']
  programmatic_quality_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s delivery quality against agreed SoW milestones, Theory of Change (ToC) outputs, and LogFrame indicators. Distinct from financial accountability.']
  reporting_compliance_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s adherence to agreed reporting schedules, SitRep submissions, and donor reporting requirements including IATI data publication obligations.']
  reports_due_count varchar [note: 'Total number of reports required from the partner during the review period per the agreement schedule.']
  reports_submitted_count varchar [note: 'Number of required narrative and financial reports submitted by the partner during the review period.']
  review_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the performance review meeting or assessment was conducted with the partner.']
  review_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the performance review. Field visits provide direct observation; desk reviews rely on submitted documentation; joint reviews involve donor or cluster lead participation.']
  review_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the performance period being assessed in this review. Together with review_period_start_date defines the assessment window.']
  review_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the performance period being assessed in this review. Aligns with the reporting cycle defined in the partner SoW.']
  review_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this review for tracking in correspondence, donor reports, and audit trails. Format: PPR-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the performance review record. Drives workflow routing for approval and archiving.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review occasion. Scheduled reviews occur at fixed intervals per the SoW; triggered reviews are initiated by risk events or compliance concerns; final reviews occur at agreement close-out.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the partner as an outcome of this review. Informs sub-award monitoring intensity, financial controls, and escalation protocols.']
  safeguarding_compliance_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s compliance with safeguarding policies including GBV prevention, child protection, and HAP/CHS accountability commitments.']
  strengths_noted varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the partner\'s notable strengths and positive performance areas identified during the review. Used for learning and best-practice documentation.']

  Note: 'Periodic performance review records for implementing partners assessing delivery against agreed SoW milestones, reporting compliance, financial accountability, and programmatic quality. Captures review period, performance rating, key findings, corrective action requirements, and reviewer details. Feeds into partnership renewal and risk management decisions.'
}

Table partnership.consortium {
  consortium_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the consortium record. Primary key for the consortium master data product within the partnership domain.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Consortium currently stores agreement_reference as STRING but has no FK to the actual partnership_agreement record. Consortia are formalized through partnership agreements (MoUs). Adding partnership_a']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.donor_fund. Business justification: Consortia are financed through specific donor funds whose restrictions govern pooled_fund_mechanism, ngo_funding_share, and reporting_frequency. NGO consortium managers must trace total_funding_amount']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: Consortia are formed to deliver specific interventions through multi-partner collaboration. Linking consortium to intervention enables consolidated program reporting, multi-partner results tracking, a']
  managing_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Consortium operations are managed by a specific NGO org unit (country office or thematic department). This link enables organizational accountability reporting, budget ownership tracking, and headcoun']
  ngo_focal_point_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Consortium management requires designated NGO staff focal point for coordination, steering committee participation, and member communication. Standard practice in multi-agency humanitarian response co']
  primary_donor_constituent_id varchar [note: 'FK to donor.constituent']
  single_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.single_audit. Business justification: Consortia receiving US federal funds above threshold require single audits per OMB Uniform Guidance. Links consortium structure to regulatory audit requirement. Essential for federal grant compliance ']
  chs_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status of the consortium\'s adherence to the Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) on Quality and Accountability, assessed through partner due diligence and capacity assessments.']
  consortium_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the multi-partner consortium or joint programming arrangement as registered with the lead donor or coordinating body (e.g., OCHA cluster).']
  consortium_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consortium arrangement indicating Ngo\'s role and the structural form. Lead_agency means Ngo is the prime recipient; member means Ngo is a sub-partner; pooled_fund indicates a CERF or similar mechanism. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lead_agency|member|co_implementer|pooled_fund|consortium_of_consortia — promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the primary country of consortium operations. For multi-country consortia, this represents the lead country of registration or primary operational context.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consortium record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  decision_making_protocol varchar [note: 'Formal protocol governing how decisions are made within the consortium. Consensus requires unanimous agreement; majority_vote uses simple majority; lead_agency_authority delegates decisions to the prime recipient; weighted_vote allocates votes by funding share.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the consortium agreement expires or joint programming is scheduled to conclude. Nullable for open-ended arrangements. Critical for compliance reporting and closeout planning.']
  funding_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the total funding envelope (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with IATI currency reporting standards and SAP S/4HANA fund accounting.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic coverage of consortium operations, including countries, regions, or administrative zones. May reference GIS-coded areas or OCHA P-codes for field operations.']
  governance_structure varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the consortium\'s governance model including steering committee composition, decision-making protocols, voting rights, and escalation procedures as defined in the consortium agreement.']
  grand_bargain_localization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consortium is formally counted toward Ngo\'s Grand Bargain localization commitments, specifically the 25% funding to local and national actors target.']
  iasc_working_group varchar [note: 'Name of the IASC working group or task force with which this consortium coordinates, relevant for inter-agency humanitarian response planning and joint needs assessments.']
  iati_identifier varchar [note: 'Globally unique IATI activity identifier for the consortium, enabling public transparency reporting on aid flows, partner contributions, and results per IATI Standard requirements.']
  localization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total consortium funding envelope directed to local and national CSOs or CBOs as sub-partners, tracked against Grand Bargain 25% localization commitment.']
  logframe_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document title of the consortium\'s Logical Framework (LogFrame) used for results-based management (RBM), KPI tracking, and donor reporting.']
  mel_framework_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document title of the consortium\'s joint MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) or MEAL (Monitoring Evaluation Accountability and Learning) framework used for collective results measurement.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of partner organizations formally participating in the consortium, including lead agency and all sub-partners (INGOs, CSOs, CBOs, UN agencies, government bodies).']
  ngo_funding_share decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total consortium funding envelope allocated to Ngo, whether as lead agency managing sub-awards or as a sub-partner receiving a sub-grant. Used for BvA (Budget versus Actual) reporting and ICR/NICRA calculations.']
  ngo_role varchar [note: 'Specific role that Ngo plays within this consortium arrangement. Lead_agency means Ngo holds the prime award and manages sub-awards; co_lead means shared leadership; sub_partner means Ngo receives a sub-award from the lead.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual information about the consortium not captured in structured fields, including coordination challenges, strategic rationale, or historical context.']
  ocha_cluster_alignment varchar [note: 'Name of the OCHA humanitarian cluster(s) or sector working group(s) to which this consortium is formally aligned or co-leads (e.g., WASH Cluster, Food Security Cluster, Health Cluster, Protection Cluster).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consortium. Pipeline indicates pre-formation; active indicates ongoing joint programming; suspended indicates temporary halt; closed indicates completed; dissolved indicates formally terminated with no further obligations.']
  pooled_fund_mechanism varchar [note: 'Name of the pooled funding mechanism associated with the consortium if applicable (e.g., CERF, Country-Based Pooled Fund (CBPF), ECHO, BHA). Supports ODA tracking and joint donor reporting.']
  reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the consortium by the lead donor, OCHA, or internal grant management system (GMS) for cross-system identification and reporting.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Agreed frequency of joint donor reporting and SitRep (Situation Report) submissions for the consortium, as stipulated in the consortium agreement or donor grant conditions.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of UN Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) numbers to which the consortium\'s programming contributes (e.g., \'2,3,6\' for Zero Hunger, Good Health, Clean Water). Supports IATI and donor reporting.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name or acronym for the consortium used in SitReps, donor reports, and inter-agency communications.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the consortium agreement becomes effective and joint programming operations commence. Used for grant financial management period-of-performance tracking.']
  steering_committee_composition varchar [note: 'Description of the steering committee membership including designated representatives from each partner organization, their roles (chair, co-chair, member), and meeting frequency.']
  thematic_focus varchar [note: 'Primary humanitarian or development sector(s) addressed by the consortium (e.g., WASH, GBV, food security, health, education, PSS). Aligns with OCHA cluster system and IASC sector classifications.']
  theory_of_change_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document title of the consortium\'s Theory of Change (ToC) that articulates the causal pathway from inputs and activities to intended outcomes and impact. Used in MEL and MEAL frameworks.']
  total_funding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total funding envelope committed to the consortium across all member organizations and donor sources, expressed in the reporting currency. Used for Grand Bargain localization tracking and joint donor reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consortium record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking and data governance.']

  Note: 'Master record for multi-partner consortia and joint programming arrangements where Ngo acts as lead agency or member. Captures consortium name, lead organization, governance structure (steering committee composition, decision-making protocols), consortium agreement reference, geographic scope, thematic focus, total funding envelope, and operational status. Supports coordination with OCHA cluster leads, IASC working groups, and pooled fund mechanisms. Essential for Grand Bargain localization commitments and joint donor reporting.'
}

Table partnership.partner_report_submission {
  partner_report_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each partner report submission record. Primary key for this entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sub-award, MoU, LoA, or SoW under which this report is submitted. Links the submission to its governing partnership agreement.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or funding award associated with this report submission, enabling traceability to the donor funding source.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Partner financial reports must be reconciled against the approved budget for donor compliance and variance analysis. NGO finance teams run \'budget vs. actuals\' reports per partner submission. approved']
  budget_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.budget_plan. Business justification: Partner financial reports must reference the approved budget plan for budget-vs-actual variance analysis, expenditure verification against approved line items, burn rate tracking, and financial accoun']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.donor_fund. Business justification: Partner reports must satisfy specific donor fund restriction and reporting requirements. NGO finance staff track expenditure against restricted donor funds via partner submissions. This named complian']
  donor_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.donor_report. Business justification: NGO compliance officers consolidate partner report submissions into donor reports. This FK enables the donor reporting aggregation process — tracing which partner submissions fed each donor report — r']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Partner report submissions are driven by specific donor requirements (e.g., quarterly narrative, financial reports). The existing regulatory_filing FK covers regulatory submissions; donor_requirement ']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project under which the partner is implementing activities and for which this report is submitted.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Partner financial reports trigger accounting entries when partner-reported expenses are recognized or accruals posted. Required for financial close process, expense recognition, and reconciling partne']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the implementing partner (Civil Society Organization, Community-Based Organization, INGO, government body, or UN agency) that submitted this report.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or grants officer responsible for reviewing and assessing this partner report submission.']
  subaward_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.subaward. Business justification: Partners submit performance and financial reports specific to their subaward scope, not just prime award. Subaward-level reporting is standard practice in tiered grant management for tracking downstre']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'The date on which the report was formally accepted by the organization, signifying that all reporting obligations for the period have been satisfactorily met by the implementing partner.']
  beneficiaries_reached varchar [note: 'Total number of direct beneficiaries (Persons of Concern, IDPs, or target population) reached by the partner\'s program activities during the reporting period, as reported by the partner.']
  cluster_sector varchar [note: 'The humanitarian cluster or sector (e.g., WASH, Health, Shelter, Food Security, Protection, GBV) under which the partner\'s reported activities fall, aligned with OCHA cluster coordination structures.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary country of implementation covered by this report submission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this partner report submission record was first created in the system, providing an audit trail for data governance and compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which the partner reported expenditures (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency grant environments.']
  days_late varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the submission exceeded the due date. Zero or negative values indicate on-time or early submission. Used for partner performance scoring and compliance reporting.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the primary report document stored in the document management system or GMS (Grant Management System), enabling direct access to the submitted report file.']
  donor_reporting_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner report submission is required to satisfy a specific donor reporting obligation (e.g., USAID BHA, DFID, CERF), meaning the data must be incorporated into the prime recipient\'s donor report.']
  due_date date [note: 'The contractually obligated deadline by which the partner was required to submit this report, as specified in the sub-award agreement or LoA.']
  feedback_provided varchar [note: 'Detailed written feedback, comments, or queries provided by the reviewer to the implementing partner following the review of the submitted report. Captures specific issues, clarifications required, or commendations.']
  financial_documentation_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner attached supporting financial documentation (receipts, invoices, bank statements) to this report submission as required under 2 CFR 200 and the sub-award agreement.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic areas (districts, regions, or country) where the partner implemented activities during the reporting period, as reported. May reference GIS coordinates or administrative boundaries.']
  is_late_submission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the report was submitted after the contractually obligated due date. True if submission_date is after due_date. Used for partner performance monitoring and compliance tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this partner report submission record was most recently modified in the system, supporting audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  mel_indicators_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner included MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) indicator data and results in this report submission, as required by the sub-award agreement.']
  narrative_summary varchar [note: 'A concise summary of the programmatic progress, key achievements, challenges, and lessons learned as reported by the implementing partner for the reporting period.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric quality assessment score assigned by the reviewer to the submitted report, typically on a standardized scale (e.g., 0–100). Reflects completeness, accuracy, and adherence to reporting standards. Used for partner performance monitoring.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, contextual observations, or internal comments recorded by the grants management team regarding this report submission, such as exceptional circumstances, force majeure events, or escalation notes.']
  report_period_frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled reporting cadence for this submission as defined in the sub-award agreement, indicating whether the report covers a monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or ad hoc period.']
  report_title varchar [note: 'The formal title or name of the submitted report document as provided by the implementing partner, used for identification and document management purposes.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the report submitted by the partner. Narrative reports cover programmatic progress; financial reports cover expenditure against budget (BvA); SitRep (Situation Report) covers operational context; PDM (Post-Distribution Monitoring) covers distribution outcomes; combined covers both narrative and financial. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: narrative|financial|situation_report|post_distribution_monitoring|combined|other — promote to reference product]']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the report submission, incremented each time the partner resubmits a revised version following feedback or a revision request. Version 1 is the initial submission.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the period covered by this report submission, as defined in the sub-award or agreement reporting schedule.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the period covered by this report submission, as defined in the sub-award or agreement reporting schedule.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the review of the partner report was completed and a decision (accepted, feedback issued, or rejected) was communicated to the partner.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal review of the submitted report was initiated by the grants management team.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the report submission in the review and acceptance lifecycle. Tracks progression from initial receipt through quality review, feedback, revision, and final acceptance or rejection.']
  revision_deadline date [note: 'The deadline by which the implementing partner must resubmit a revised report following a revision request or feedback issuance. Null if no revision has been requested.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the partner submitted this report to the organization. Used to calculate timeliness and compliance with reporting obligations.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism through which the partner submitted the report, such as email, online portal, GMS (Grant Management System) upload, hard copy, or KoboToolbox.']
  submission_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this report submission, used in correspondence with the partner and in donor reporting systems such as Unit4 ERP or GMS.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the partner report was received or uploaded into the grant management system, capturing the exact moment of submission for audit trail purposes.']
  target_beneficiaries varchar [note: 'The planned number of beneficiaries the partner was expected to reach during the reporting period as defined in the sub-award agreement or LogFrame targets.']
  total_expenditure_reported decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total financial expenditure reported by the implementing partner for the reporting period, as stated in the financial report. Used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) analysis and donor financial reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks all programmatic and financial reports submitted by implementing partners under their sub-award or agreement obligations. Captures report type (narrative, financial, SitRep, PDM), reporting period, submission date, review status, feedback provided, and acceptance date. Supports compliance monitoring and donor reporting obligations under 2 CFR 200.'
}

Table partnership.capacity_building_plan {
  capacity_building_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the capacity building plan record. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — this product represents a structured, time-bound plan (agreement-like container) governing capacity-building interventions for a partner organization.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: A capacity building plan is typically developed as a requirement or condition of a specific partnership agreement (e.g., a sub-award agreement may mandate capacity strengthening). Linking capacity_bui']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or sub-award under which this capacity building plan is funded and implemented. Links to the grant master record in the grant domain.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Capacity building plans require formal budget allocation for training, technical assistance, and organizational development activities. Links plan to approved budget for expenditure tracking, variance']
  capacity_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal capacity assessment record that identified the gaps and informed the design of this capacity building plan. Ensures traceability from assessment findings to planned interventions.']
  corrective_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action_plan. Business justification: Capacity building plans are directly created in response to corrective action plans when audit findings or compliance incidents identify partner capability gaps (e.g., a CAP finding of weak financial ']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donors frequently require capacity building plans as a condition of sub-awards to local NGO partners (Grand Bargain localization commitments, USAID partner capacity requirements). Linking capacity_bui']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization (Civil Society Organization, Community-Based Organization, or local INGO) for whom this capacity building plan has been developed. Links to the partner organization master record in the partnership domain.']
  partner_performance_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_performance_review. Business justification: A capacity building plan is frequently triggered by findings from a partner performance review (e.g., when capacity_building_recommended = TRUE on the review). Linking the plan back to the triggering ']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Ngo staff member (e.g., Partnership Officer, Capacity Building Advisor) who is the primary owner and accountable lead for this plan\'s implementation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this capacity building plan was formally approved by the designated authority at Ngo, authorizing commencement of activities and budget expenditure.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Ngo staff member or management authority who formally approved this capacity building plan for implementation. Supports governance and accountability documentation.']
  baseline_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the partner organization\'s overall organizational capacity at the time of the baseline assessment, prior to plan implementation. Typically scored on a standardized scale (e.g., 1–5 or 0–100) per the assessment tool used.']
  capacity_score_scale varchar [note: 'The scoring scale used for baseline, target, and current capacity scores in this plan (e.g., \'1_to_5\', \'0_to_100\'). Ensures consistent interpretation of scores across different assessment tools and reporting periods.']
  chs_standard_aligned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity building plan is designed in alignment with the Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) on Quality and Accountability, ensuring the partner is supported to meet CHS commitments.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the partner organization operates and where this capacity building plan is being implemented (e.g., \'SOM\', \'SDN\', \'ETH\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this capacity building plan record was first created in the system. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency in which the plan budget is also tracked (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'KES\'). Supports multi-currency financial reporting for field operations.']
  current_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Most recently recorded organizational capacity score for the partner, updated following periodic reassessments or milestone reviews during plan implementation. Enables tracking of progress toward the target capacity score.']
  donor_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether progress on this capacity building plan must be reported to the donor as part of grant compliance obligations. True = donor reporting required; False = internal plan only.']
  end_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date on which the capacity building plan concludes and all activities are expected to be completed. Nullable for open-ended plans. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  expenditure_to_date_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual expenditure incurred against this capacity building plan to date, expressed in US Dollars (USD). Used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) tracking and financial stewardship reporting.']
  gender_mainstreaming_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether gender mainstreaming and Gender-Based Violence (GBV) prevention components are explicitly included in this capacity building plan, in line with donor gender policies and SDG (Sustainable Development Goal) 5 commitments.']
  grand_bargain_commitment_ref varchar [note: 'Reference code or description of the specific Grand Bargain commitment (e.g., Workstream 2 — Localization) that this capacity building plan contributes to. Supports donor accountability and IATI transparency reporting.']
  implementation_region varchar [note: 'Sub-national region, state, or province where the partner organization and this capacity building plan are primarily based. Supports geographic disaggregation in MEL dashboards and GIS (Geographic Information System) reporting.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review or progress assessment of this capacity building plan, during which activity completion, capacity scores, and budget utilization were evaluated.']
  localization_strategy_aligned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity building plan is formally aligned with Ngo\'s localization strategy and Grand Bargain commitments to strengthen local and national actors. True = aligned and documented; False = not formally aligned.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the next formal review of this capacity building plan is scheduled. Drives workflow reminders and ensures timely monitoring of plan progress.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual notes, observations, or qualitative commentary on the capacity building plan, including lessons learned, contextual challenges, or coordination notes with OCHA cluster leads.']
  overall_objective varchar [note: 'High-level statement of the primary objective of this capacity building plan, describing the intended organizational capability improvement to be achieved by the plan\'s end date.']
  overall_progress_status varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the overall implementation progress of the capacity building plan relative to the planned timeline and milestones. Distinct from plan_status (lifecycle state); this reflects delivery performance.']
  partner_focal_point_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated focal point or primary contact person at the partner organization who is responsible for coordinating and participating in capacity building activities under this plan.']
  partner_focal_point_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the partner organization\'s designated focal point for this capacity building plan (e.g., \'Executive Director\', \'Finance Manager\', \'MEL Officer\').']
  plan_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this capacity building plan, used in donor reports, partner correspondence, and IATI disclosures. Format: CBP-[PartnerCode]-[Year]-[Sequence].']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity building plan. \'draft\' = under development; \'active\' = approved and in implementation; \'on_hold\' = temporarily suspended; \'completed\' = all activities delivered and closed; \'cancelled\' = plan discontinued.']
  plan_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the capacity building plan, typically referencing the partner organization name and the primary functional area targeted (e.g., \'Financial Management Strengthening Plan — Partner XYZ 2024\').']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary focus area of the capacity building plan. \'organizational\' = overall organizational development; \'technical\' = sector-specific technical skills; \'financial\' = financial management and accountability; \'governance\' = board and leadership strengthening; \'mel\' = Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organizational|technical|financial|governance|mel|procurement|hr|communications|safeguarding — promote to reference product]']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which progress updates on this capacity building plan must be reported internally or to the donor. Drives scheduling of review meetings and SitRep (Situation Report) contributions.']
  safeguarding_component_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity building plan includes a dedicated safeguarding (Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse — PSEA) component. Required for compliance with donor safeguarding policies and CHS commitments.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the capacity building plan officially commences and activities are scheduled to begin. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  target_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the desired organizational capacity level to be achieved by the partner organization upon successful completion of this plan. Used to measure progress and plan effectiveness.']
  targeted_functional_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of organizational functional areas targeted by this plan for strengthening, as identified in the capacity assessment. Common values include: financial management, human resources, procurement, governance, MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning), safeguarding, communications, ICT4D (Information and Communication Technology for Development).']
  theory_of_change_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the Theory of Change (ToC) underpinning this capacity building plan, describing the causal pathway from planned interventions to expected organizational capability improvements and ultimate program outcomes.']
  total_activities_completed varchar [note: 'Count of capacity building activity line-items that have been fully delivered and marked as completed under this plan. Used to calculate overall plan completion rate for MEL dashboards and donor reporting.']
  total_activities_planned varchar [note: 'Total number of capacity building activity line-items (trainings, mentoring sessions, technical assistance visits, peer learning events) planned under this plan. Used for progress tracking and workload planning.']
  total_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated for the implementation of this capacity building plan, expressed in US Dollars (USD). Covers all activity costs including training, technical assistance, materials, and facilitator fees.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this capacity building plan record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master record with embedded activity line-items for structured capacity-building interventions developed for partner organizations based on capacity assessment findings. Plan-level data captures objectives, targeted functional areas (financial management, HR, procurement, governance, MEL), timeline, responsible staff, budget allocation, and overall progress status. Activity-level line-items capture individual trainings, mentoring sessions, technical assistance visits, and peer learning events with delivery dates, facilitators, participant lists, topics covered, pre/post assessment scores, and completion status. Supports localization strategies, partner strengthening, and Grand Bargain commitments. This is the SSOT for all capacity-building planning and delivery tracking in the partnership domain.'
}

Table partnership.consortium_membership {
  consortium_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the consortium_membership association']
  consortium_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the consortium that this membership record belongs to.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the partner organization that is a member of the consortium.']
  exit_date date [note: 'The date on which this partner organization formally exited or withdrew from the consortium. Null if the member is currently active. Enables historical membership records and supports donor reporting on partner changes.']
  funding_share_percentage decimal [note: 'The percentage of the total consortium funding envelope allocated to this specific member organization. This value belongs to the membership relationship — it varies per member per consortium and cannot reside on either the consortium or partner_org master record.']
  is_lead_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner organization is the designated lead agency for the consortium. Replaces the lead_partner_org_id FK on the consortium master record, enabling the lead to be tracked as a membership attribute rather than a separate FK.']
  join_date date [note: 'The date on which this partner organization formally joined the consortium, as recorded in the consortium agreement or amendment. Supports historical membership tracking and audit requirements.']
  member_role varchar [note: 'The formal role of this partner organization within the consortium. Replaces the lead_partner_org_id FK on consortium — is_lead_member boolean now captures lead status at the membership level, enabling full role enumeration across all members.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this partner organization\'s membership in the consortium. Supports operational filtering and donor reporting on active vs. historical members.']
  reporting_obligations varchar [note: 'Description of the specific reporting obligations assigned to this member organization within the consortium arrangement, such as narrative reports, financial reports, cluster reports, or IATI submissions. Obligations vary per member and per consortium.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal Membership relationship between a consortium and a partner organization. It captures each partner organization\'s participation in a specific consortium, including their role, funding share, membership period, and reporting obligations. Each record links one consortium to one member partner organization and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that specific membership — data that belongs neither to the consortium master record nor to the partner organization master record alone. Supports OCHA cluster reporting, Grand Bargain localization tracking, and joint donor reporting requirements.. Existence Justification: In the humanitarian NGO sector, a consortium is explicitly a multi-partner arrangement where multiple partner organizations formally participate, each with a defined role, funding share, and membership period. Conversely, a single partner organization routinely participates in multiple consortia simultaneously or over time. The business actively manages consortium membership as a formal operational concept — tracking who is in which consortium, in what role, with what funding share, and for what period — making this a genuine operational M:N relationship, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table finance.finance_fund {
  finance_fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fund record. Primary key for the finance_fund product.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Restricted funds must track applicable compliance obligations (donor reporting schedules, audit requirements, IATI publication). Fund managers need to know which obligations apply to each fund for com']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor or funding source that provided the resources for this fund. Links to Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud constituent records.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: finance_fund has fiscal_period (STRING) and fiscal_year (STRING) as scalar fields but no fiscal_period_id FK. Fund master records must reference the fiscal period for fund lifecycle management, period']
  funding_source_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific funding source record that channels resources into this fund. May represent government agencies, foundations, or institutional donors.']
  managing_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Finance funds are managed and administered by specific org units (country offices, program units) in NGOs; FK enables fund portfolio reporting by org unit and supports organizational accountability fo']
  parent_fund_finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent fund if this fund is a sub-fund or component of a larger fund structure. Enables hierarchical fund accounting and roll-up reporting.']
  allowable_cost_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of cost categories that are allowable charges to this fund per donor restrictions or OMB Uniform Guidance. Guides expense posting and audit compliance.']
  audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fund is subject to external audit requirements under OMB Uniform Guidance Single Audit provisions or donor-specific audit clauses.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount allocated to the fund for the current fiscal period. Used as the baseline for Budget versus Actual (BvA) variance analysis.']
  chart_of_accounts_segment varchar [note: 'The fund dimension segment code in the organization\'s Chart of Accounts structure. Used for General Ledger posting and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the fund was officially closed. Marks the completion of the fund lifecycle and triggers closeout procedures per OMB Uniform Guidance.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount committed through purchase orders, contracts, or other encumbrances but not yet expended. Used for fund availability calculations.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fund is currently in compliance with all donor conditions, OMB Uniform Guidance requirements, and internal policies. False triggers compliance review workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fund record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fund\'s primary currency. Essential for multi-currency fund management and foreign exchange reporting.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current available balance in the fund after all posted transactions. Updated in real-time by the ERP system for fund availability checking.']
  donor_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which financial reports must be submitted to the donor or funding source. Drives reporting calendar and compliance workflows.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the fund expires or is scheduled to close. Critical for grant closeout planning and restricted fund compliance.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the fund becomes active and available for transactions. Aligns with grant award start dates or board approval dates.']
  expended_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount expended from the fund during the current fiscal period. Tracks actual spending against budget for BvA reporting.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the fund for identification in financial systems and reporting. Used as the business identifier across SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP.']
  fund_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the fund as it appears in financial statements and donor reports.']
  fund_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the fund in the financial system. Determines whether transactions can be posted to the fund.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fund based on restriction type and purpose. Determines how funds can be used and reported per FASB ASC 958 net asset classification requirements.']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) percentage applicable to this fund. Used for Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation per OMB Uniform Guidance.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent external audit that included this fund. Used for audit cycle tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  last_report_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent financial report was submitted to the donor for this fund. Tracks reporting compliance and timeliness.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member or department responsible for managing and overseeing the fund. Accountable for fund stewardship and compliance.']
  match_fulfilled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of matching funds or in-kind contributions fulfilled to date. Tracked for grant reporting and compliance verification.']
  match_requirement_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of matching funds or cost share required by the donor or grant agreement. Critical for tracking cost share commitments and compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fund record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next external audit covering this fund. Supports audit planning and documentation preparation.']
  next_report_due_date date [note: 'The due date for the next scheduled financial report to the donor. Critical for compliance and relationship management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or contextual information about the fund. Used by fund managers for operational guidance.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fund balance at the beginning of the current fiscal year or at fund inception. Represents the starting financial position for Budget versus Actual (BvA) tracking.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'The program or project code linked to the fund. Enables program-level financial reporting and Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL) integration.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the intended use and purpose of the fund, including any donor-specified restrictions or programmatic objectives.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Specific nature of restrictions placed on the fund by donors or the board. Governs allowable expenditures and compliance requirements under OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.']
  unallowable_cost_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of cost categories that are explicitly unallowable for this fund. Prevents non-compliant expense posting.']

  Note: 'Master record for each restricted or unrestricted fund managed by the NGO. Captures fund name, fund code, restriction type (donor-restricted, board-designated, unrestricted), associated grant or donor source, fund purpose, opening balance, currency, fiscal year, fund status, and compliance flags per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 and FASB ASC 958. The SSOT for fund identity used across grant financial management, cost allocation, and BvA reporting in Unit4 ERP and SAP S/4HANA.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost center. Primary key for the cost center master record.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Cost centers (programs/field offices) have specific compliance obligations (CHS commitments, sphere standards, donor-specific requirements). Program managers track obligations by cost center for opera']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables roll-up reporting from field offices to regional hubs to headquarters. Nullable for top-level cost centers.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who is accountable for budget performance, expense approval, and financial stewardship of this cost center. Used in RACI (Responsible Accountable Consulted Informed) matrices and budget versus actual (BvA) reporting.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'The primary activity or service provided by this cost center (e.g., beneficiary registration, field assessment, grant management, financial reporting, volunteer coordination). Used for activity-based costing and internal service billing.']
  annual_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total approved budget amount for this cost center for the current fiscal year. Used in Budget versus Actual (BvA) variance analysis and financial stewardship reporting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for new or modified cost centers. Draft cost centers are under construction; pending approval cost centers await finance or executive review; approved cost centers are active; rejected cost centers require revision.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost center was approved for activation. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting. Nullable for cost centers not yet approved.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost center\'s budget and financial reporting. Used for multi-currency organizations with country offices operating in local currencies.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost center was permanently closed and all balances were transferred or reconciled. Used for grant closeout, project completion, and organizational restructuring. Nullable for active cost centers.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA or Unit4 ERP company code representing the legal entity to which this cost center belongs. Used for multi-entity consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA controlling area (CO module) to which this cost center is assigned. Controlling areas represent organizational units for internal cost accounting and management reporting.']
  cost_allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to allocate shared or indirect costs to this cost center. Direct allocation assigns costs explicitly; proportional uses a percentage driver; headcount allocates by staff count; square footage uses physical space; activity-based costing (ABC) uses cost drivers; not allocated for cost centers that do not receive shared cost allocations.']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA cost center category classification. Production cost centers deliver direct program services; service cost centers provide internal support; administrative cost centers manage overhead; auxiliary cost centers support shared services; project cost centers are time-bound; temporary cost centers are short-term or pilot initiatives.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP. Used in general ledger posting, budget tracking, and financial reporting.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the cost center\'s purpose, scope, and responsibilities. Includes information on program activities, geographic coverage, donor restrictions, and organizational context.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'The full descriptive name of the cost center as it appears in financial reports and organizational charts.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active cost centers accept expense postings; inactive cost centers are temporarily disabled; suspended cost centers are under review; closed cost centers are permanently retired; pending activation cost centers are awaiting approval.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by functional expense category per GAAP for nonprofits. Program cost centers support direct mission delivery; administrative cost centers support organizational management; fundraising cost centers support donor cultivation and gift processing; general and administrative (G&A) support overall operations; mission support provides enabling services; field operations manage country office and site-level activities.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary geographic location of this cost center. Used for country office cost centers, field operations, and regional program management. Nullable for headquarters or global cost centers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  department varchar [note: 'The functional department or division to which this cost center belongs (e.g., Health Programs, WASH, Education, Finance, Human Resources, Monitoring Evaluation and Learning). Used for cross-cutting organizational reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this cost center ceases to be active. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Used for project-specific or time-bound organizational units.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this cost center becomes active and can accept expense allocations and budget assignments.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this cost center in external systems such as donor grant management systems (GMS), partner organization ERPs, or government reporting portals. Used for cross-system reconciliation and International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) reporting.']
  functional_expense_classification varchar [note: 'GAAP functional expense category per FASB ASC 958-720. Program services are mission-delivery activities; management and general (M&G) are administrative overhead; fundraising supports donor cultivation; membership development supports constituent engagement. Required for Form 990 and audited financial statements.']
  fund_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of donor-imposed restrictions on funds allocated to this cost center per FASB ASC 958-205. Unrestricted funds have no donor-imposed restrictions; temporarily restricted funds have time or purpose restrictions that will lapse; permanently restricted funds (endowments) must be maintained in perpetuity.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The depth of this cost center in the organizational hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents headquarters or top-level divisions; higher numbers represent nested field offices, country offices, or project sites.']
  indirect_cost_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether expenses posted to this cost center are eligible for indirect cost recovery under the organization\'s Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA). True for direct program cost centers; false for administrative or already-indirect cost centers. Used in Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost center record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  nicra_base_category varchar [note: 'The indirect cost base category used for calculating indirect cost recovery for this cost center per the organization\'s NICRA with the federal government. Modified Total Direct Costs (MTDC) is the most common base; direct salaries and wages is used for some research organizations; total direct costs includes all direct expenses. Not applicable for cost centers that do not participate in indirect cost allocation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, donor conditions, compliance requirements, or historical context relevant to this cost center.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'The profit center code for organizations using profit center accounting in SAP S/4HANA. Used for segment reporting and internal performance measurement. Nullable for nonprofits not using profit center structures.']
  program_area varchar [note: 'The thematic program area or sector this cost center supports (e.g., WASH - Water Sanitation and Hygiene, GBV - Gender-Based Violence, Health, Education, Livelihoods, Emergency Response). Used for Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) alignment and donor reporting.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region for multi-country or regional cost centers. Used for regional program offices, humanitarian response hubs, and Development Assistance Committee (DAC) reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: africa|asia_pacific|europe|latin_america_caribbean|middle_east|north_america|global — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this cost center master data originated. Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation between SAP S/4HANA, Unit4 ERP, and legacy systems.']
  year_to_date_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative actual expenses posted to this cost center from the start of the fiscal year to the current reporting period. Used in BvA reporting and burn rate analysis.']

  Note: 'Master record for organizational cost centers used to allocate expenses across programs, departments, country offices, and projects. Captures cost center code, name, type (program, administrative, fundraising), responsible manager, parent cost center (for hierarchy), organizational hierarchy level, associated country or region, active status, and functional expense classification per GAAP. The SSOT for organizational cost structure used in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP for cost allocation, indirect cost recovery, NICRA base calculations, and Budget vs. Actual reporting.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the general ledger account. Primary key for the GL account master data.']
  account_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the GL account purpose, usage guidelines, and what types of transactions should be posted to this account.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'The account group or category within the Chart of Accounts structure (e.g., \'Current Assets\', \'Fixed Assets\', \'Restricted Contributions\', \'Program Expenses\').']
  account_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable descriptive name of the GL account (e.g., \'Cash - Operating Account\', \'Grant Revenue - USAID\', \'Program Salaries - WASH\').']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique account number within the Chart of Accounts (CoA) structure. This is the business identifier used in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP for all financial transactions and reporting.']
  account_owner varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the business owner or responsible party for this GL account (e.g., Finance Director, Program Manager, Grant Manager).']
  account_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the GL account: active (available for posting), inactive (temporarily suspended), blocked (posting prohibited), or closed (permanently retired).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'The fundamental accounting classification of the GL account: asset, liability, net asset (equity for nonprofits per FASB ASC 958), revenue, or expense.']
  budget_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is subject to budget control and Budget versus Actual (BvA) monitoring. When true, transactions require budget availability checking before posting.']
  coa_structure_code varchar [note: 'The Chart of Accounts structure identifier or version code. Nonprofits may maintain multiple CoA structures for different legal entities or reporting requirements.']
  consolidation_account_number varchar [note: 'The GL account number used for consolidated financial reporting across multiple legal entities or country offices. Used when the nonprofit has a multi-entity structure.']
  cost_allocation_base_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account serves as an allocation base for distributing indirect costs across programs and grants (e.g., direct salaries, total direct costs).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for this GL account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Nonprofits operating internationally may maintain accounts in multiple currencies.']
  dac_sector_code varchar [note: 'The OECD Development Assistance Committee sector classification code for international development activities. Required for International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) reporting.']
  donor_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account tracks donor-restricted funds requiring specific usage compliance and separate reporting per donor agreements and FASB ASC 958.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this GL account was closed or became inactive. Null for currently active accounts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this GL account became active and available for transaction posting in the Chart of Accounts.']
  external_reporting_code varchar [note: 'The mapping code used for external regulatory reporting (e.g., IRS Form 990 line item, IATI activity code, donor-specific reporting category).']
  financial_statement_classification varchar [note: 'The primary financial statement where this account appears: balance sheet (statement of financial position), statement of activities (income statement equivalent for nonprofits), statement of functional expenses, statement of cash flows, or off-balance-sheet.']
  functional_expense_category varchar [note: 'The functional expense classification required by FASB ASC 958 for expense accounts: program services, management and general (administrative), fundraising, or not applicable (for non-expense accounts). Used for Form 990 reporting and functional expense allocation.']
  fund_restriction_class varchar [note: 'The net asset restriction classification per FASB ASC 958: unrestricted (no donor restrictions), temporarily restricted (time or purpose restrictions), or permanently restricted (endowment principal). Critical for nonprofit fund accounting and donor compliance.']
  grant_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether expenses posted to this account are eligible for reimbursement under federal grants per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200. Critical for grant financial management and cost allocation.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The level of this account within the Chart of Accounts hierarchy (e.g., 1 for top-level summary accounts, 2 for sub-accounts, 3 for detail posting accounts).']
  indirect_cost_pool_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is part of the indirect cost pool for Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) calculations and Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this GL account record was most recently updated.']
  last_posting_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent transaction posted to this GL account. Used for account activity monitoring and dormant account identification.']
  natural_expense_classification varchar [note: 'The natural classification of expense by type (e.g., salaries, benefits, travel, supplies, professional fees, rent, utilities). Used alongside functional classification for comprehensive expense reporting per FASB ASC 958.']
  normal_balance_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this account normally carries a debit or credit balance per double-entry accounting principles.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this GL account usage, restrictions, or compliance requirements.']
  posting_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions can be directly posted to this account (true for detail accounts) or if it is a summary/roll-up account that only receives aggregated balances (false).']
  program_service_code varchar [note: 'The program or service line code that this account supports (e.g., WASH, Health, Education, Emergency Response). Used for program-level financial reporting and Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL).']
  reconciliation_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account requires regular reconciliation (e.g., bank accounts, accounts receivable, accounts payable control accounts). Used for internal control and audit compliance.']
  sdg_alignment_code varchar [note: 'The United Nations Sustainable Development Goal(s) that activities posted to this account contribute toward (e.g., SDG 1 - No Poverty, SDG 6 - Clean Water and Sanitation). Used for impact reporting.']
  tax_relevance_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the tax treatment of transactions posted to this account: taxable income, non-taxable, tax-exempt per IRS 501(c)(3) regulations, or not applicable.']
  unrelated_business_income_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether revenue posted to this account constitutes Unrelated Business Income (UBI) subject to taxation under IRS regulations for tax-exempt organizations.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master of the nonprofit\'s Chart of Accounts (CoA) and General Ledger account structure in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP. Each record represents a GL account within the CoA hierarchy, capturing GL account number, account description, account group, parent account (for CoA hierarchy navigation), hierarchy level, CoA structure code, account type (asset, liability, net asset, revenue, expense), balance sheet or income statement classification, normal debit/credit indicator, currency, fund restriction class (unrestricted, temporarily restricted, permanently restricted per FASB ASC 958), functional expense category (program, management & general, fundraising), natural expense classification, tax relevance, reconciliation account flag, and posting eligibility. Serves as the single authoritative reference (SSOT) for all fund accounting, grant financial management, budget planning, BvA reporting, and GAAP-compliant financial reporting. Subsumes the Chart of Accounts structure — no separate CoA entity exists.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry entity.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the grant or award that this journal entry is financially associated with. Critical for grant-specific financial tracking, budget versus actual reporting, and donor reporting compliance.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: journal_entry has no bank_account_id FK. Bank-related journal entries (bank reconciliation adjustments, bank charges, interest income) must reference the specific bank account they affect. Adding bank']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entries are allocated to cost centers at the header level. This FK enables cost center reporting and management analysis.']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Journal entries are often specific to a country office. Finance needs country-level expense tracking for management reporting, donor reporting, and HQ consolidation.']
  created_by_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Finance tracks which staff member created each journal entry for audit trail and accountability - fundamental accounting control requirement in nonprofit financial management.']
  field_deployment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.deployment. Business justification: Deployment costs are recorded via journal entries. Finance needs to link JEs to deployments for cost tracking, audit trail, and deployment cost analysis.']
  field_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.team. Business justification: Team operating costs are recorded via journal entries. Finance needs to link JEs to teams for team budget tracking and team cost analysis.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Journal entries are allocated to funds at the header level. This FK enables fund-level reporting and ensures compliance with donor restrictions.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Journal entries are posted to specific fiscal periods for financial reporting and period close processes. The existing fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) columns are denormalized representation']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the program or project that this journal entry supports. Enables program-level cost tracking, budget monitoring, and Results-Based Management (RBM) financial reporting.']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Many journal entries relate to specific field sites (site operating costs, site setup costs). Finance needs to track expenses by site for management reporting and site cost analysis.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this journal entry was approved for posting. Null if not yet approved. Part of the audit trail for approval workflow compliance.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments added for audit trail purposes, explaining unusual circumstances, supporting documentation references, or special handling instructions.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier grouping multiple journal entries that were posted together as a single batch. Used for batch-level approval workflows and mass reversal operations.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code within the ERP system to which this journal entry belongs. Essential for multi-entity nonprofit organizations with separate legal entities or country offices.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Compliance status flag indicating whether this journal entry has been reviewed for regulatory compliance (OMB Uniform Guidance, FASB ASC 958, donor restrictions). Used for compliance monitoring and audit preparation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Foundational audit timestamp for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the transaction currency of this journal entry (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Essential for multi-currency grant accounting and foreign exchange management.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date the journal entry document was created or originated, which may differ from the posting date. Used for audit trail and document sequencing.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique document number assigned to this journal entry in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA or Unit4). Used for audit trails and cross-system reconciliation.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the journal entry document type indicating the nature of the posting: original entry, reversal of prior entry, accrual for period-end, cost allocation, reclassification between accounts, or manual adjustment.']
  entry_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the business purpose and nature of this journal entry. Provides context for financial reviewers, auditors, and future reference.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency amounts to the organization\'s functional currency (typically USD). Null for entries in functional currency. Critical for multi-currency grant financial management.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Functional area classification (e.g., program services, management and general, fundraising) required for nonprofit functional expense reporting per FASB ASC 958. Supports Form 990 and donor reporting.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code for the organization\'s functional reporting currency. All journal entries are ultimately reported in this currency for consolidated financial statements.']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) rate applied to this journal entry for facilities and administration (F&A) cost allocation. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 for 15%). Critical for federal grant compliance.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a manual adjustment or correction entry (as opposed to a system-generated or routine operational entry). True for adjustments; false for standard entries.']
  is_inter_company boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an inter-company or inter-entity transaction requiring elimination in consolidated financial statements. True for inter-company entries; false otherwise.']
  ledger_group varchar [note: 'Ledger group or ledger type identifier (e.g., leading ledger, extension ledger, parallel accounting ledger) used in systems supporting multiple parallel accounting views. Supports GAAP and IFRS parallel reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this journal entry was last modified. Null if never modified. Critical for audit trail and change history tracking.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The business date on which this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. This is the effective date for financial reporting and determines which fiscal period the entry impacts.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'System-specific posting key code (from SAP or Unit4) that determines the account type (debit/credit) and controls which fields are required during entry. Technical control field for ERP posting logic.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry in the approval and posting workflow. Tracks progression from draft creation through approval to final posting in the general ledger.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number linking this journal entry to source documents such as invoices, payment vouchers, grant agreements, or other originating transactions. Supports audit trail and source document traceability.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previously posted entry. True if this entry reverses another entry; false otherwise.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this journal entry was reversed. Required for audit compliance and internal control documentation when reversal_indicator is true.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true. Maintains bidirectional audit trail for reversals.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or interface that originated this journal entry. Distinguishes between entries created directly in the ERP (SAP S/4HANA, Unit4), manual entries, or entries interfaced from subsidiary systems.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every GL journal entry posted in SAP S/4HANA or Unit4 ERP. Captures document number, posting date, fiscal period, fiscal year, document type (original, reversal, accrual, allocation), posting key, currency, exchange rate, reference document, posting user, approval status, reversal indicator, and source system. The foundational transactional entity for all financial reporting, audit trails, and GAAP-compliant fund accounting. Each journal entry has one or more journal_entry_line records.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
